sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/NDES and SCEP/How to Setup NDES

symptom: Certificate Connector installation fails or sign-in fails during NDES connector setup
rootCause: Admin account used to sign into the connector does not have an Intune license assigned, or wrong cloud environment selected for GCCH
solution: Verify the Global Admin/Intune Admin account has an Intune license assigned before signing in. For GCCH environments, select 'Azure for Government' during connector sign-in. Use SYSTEM account (recommended) for the connector service account.
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/NDES and SCEP/How to Setup NDES

symptom: SCEP requests fail; MaxFieldLength or MaxRequestBytes registry values not set correctly on NDES server
rootCause: HTTP parameters MaxFieldLength and MaxRequestBytes under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HTTP\Parameters are not set to 65534, causing large SCEP requests to be rejected
solution: Set both MaxFieldLength and MaxRequestBytes to 65534 (DWORD, decimal) in registry. Also configure IIS Request Filtering: Max content length=30000000, Max URL length=65534, Max query string=65534. Run iisreset and restart NDES server.
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/NDES and SCEP/How to Setup NDES

symptom: SCEP certificate fails to renew on a subset of Windows devices while others renew successfully
rootCause: Third-party or middleware software (e.g., security agents) interfering with dmcertinst.exe (Windows certificate enrollment process) by loading third-party DLLs
solution: Use Procmon to diagnose: 1) Filter for Process Name=dmcertinst.exe and Path contains SCEP policy ID (replace - with _). 2) Look for error codes, use certutil /error or https://windowsinternalservices.azurewebsites.net/Static/Errors/ to decode. 3) Check Process tab for third-party DLLs loaded by dmcertinst.exe. 4) Remove/exclude the interfering software to resolve.
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/NDES and SCEP/Troubleshooting SCEP Certificate Renewal Failures using Procmon

symptom: SCEP profile shows error; NDES URL not reachable (Can't reach this page / site can't be reached)
rootCause: NDES URL misconfigured, NDES service not running, networking issues (firewall, reverse proxy), or IIS binding certificate lacks Server Authentication key usage or proper SAN
solution: 1) Verify NDES URL spelling. 2) Check NDES service on server via https://localhost/certsrv/mscep/mscep.dll. 3) Check local/corporate firewall, reverse proxy forwarding. 4) Verify IIS binding certificate has Server Authentication key usage and SAN with NDES FQDN. 5) If all correct, reinstall NDES Role (engage Directory Services team).
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/NDES and SCEP/Troubleshooting

symptom: NDES URL shows green SCEP page instead of HTTP 403 after Certificate Connector installation
rootCause: Microsoft Intune Certificate Connector is not healthy or not properly installed; the connector should intercept requests and return 403
solution: Check the Microsoft Certificate Connector status. Validate it is healthy. If not, reinstall the connector per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/protect/certificate-connector-install
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/NDES and SCEP/Troubleshooting

symptom: Android Enterprise personally-owned work profile 部署 Trusted Certificate profile 时报错 -2016281112 (Remediation failed)
rootCause: Trusted Certificate profile 中部署了非 Root 或非 Intermediate 证书。Intune 的 Trusted Certificate profile 仅支持部署 root 或 intermediate 证书，部署其他类型证书不受支持
solution: 确保 Trusted Certificate profile 仅包含 root 或 intermediate 证书。移除非 root/intermediate 证书，重新部署。参考 Intune 文档: Trusted root certificate profile for Android enterprise device shows error -2016281112
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/NDES and SCEP/Troubleshooting/Android

symptom: iOS/macOS 设备上出现重复的 SCEP 证书
rootCause: Apple 平台设计行为：iOS/macOS 会为每个 dependent profile 安装一份 SCEP 证书副本
solution: By design。参见 Microsoft 文档: Create and assign SCEP certificate profiles in Intune，末尾 NOTE 说明。无需修复，属于平台正常行为
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/NDES and SCEP/Troubleshooting/Apple

symptom: iOS 设备证书排查时 Company Portal 日志无证书相关信息
rootCause: iOS 平台限制：Company Portal logs 不包含证书 profile 相关信息，需通过 Console logs 实时捕获
solution: 使用 Console logs 排查：1) 将 iOS 设备连接到 macOS（推荐）获取最完整的 debug logs，2) 如无 macOS 可用内部工具在 Windows PC 上采集（功能有限），3) 采集时需同步设备
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/NDES and SCEP/Troubleshooting/Apple

symptom: Intune 通过 SCEP/PKCS 颁发的证书缺少 Strong Mapping OID (1.3.6.1.4.1.311.25.2)，导致 certificate-based authentication 失败
rootCause: CA 模板使用 Compatibility 2012+ 或 Key Storage Provider (KSP) 时，msPKI-Enrollment-Flag 值为 0x80001，该标志阻止将 Offline request 中的 OID 写入证书。Legacy CSP 模板默认 flag=1 不受影响
solution: 1) 先验证 Connector 是否发送 OID：在 CA 的 Issued Certs 中导出 Binary Request → 搜索 OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.311.25.2。2) 若 OID 存在于 request（Intune scope 正常），问题在 CA 模板。3) 在 CA 运行: certutil -dstemplate [TemplateName] msPKI-Enrollment-Flag -0x00080000 启用 OID。4) 验证: certutil -v -dstemplate [TemplateName]。参考 KB5014754
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/NDES and SCEP/Validating CSP request for Strong Mapping Authentication

symptom: 修改 PKCS profile 的 assignment 或配置后，设备收到的是旧证书而非新证书
rootCause: PKCS 设计行为：与 SCEP 不同，PKCS 在修改 assignments 或 profile 配置时只会重新推送之前已颁发的证书，不会触发新证书颁发
solution: By design。要触发新 PKCS 证书颁发，需重新注册设备或部署全新的 PKCS profile。SCEP 则会在修改 assignment 或 profile 时自动颁发新证书
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/PKCS

symptom: PKCS 证书长时间停留在 Pending 状态，Connector Event Log 显示 NullReferenceException at PkiCreateProcessor.UploadResults
rootCause: Certificate Connector 的 PkiCreateProcessor.UploadResults 方法存在 bug，导致 NullReferenceException。CA 已成功颁发证书但 Connector 无法上传结果到 Intune，证书状态保持 Status=0 (Pending)
solution: 1) 等待自动恢复（通常 2-4 小时），Connector 每 ~10 分钟重试。2) 重启 Connector 服务（立即修复）。3) 更新到最新版 Connector（永久修复）。Kusto 诊断：查找 DownloadNewMessages 后无对应 Upload 的模式
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/PKCS/PKCS Certificate Troubleshooting

symptom: iOS 设备 PKCS 证书显示 Pending 或 Not Applicable，VPN profile 安装失败，日志报 Dependent payload not found 或错误 2016341112 (iOS device is currently busy)
rootCause: iOS 上 PKCS 证书关联 VPN profile 时存在严格部署依赖：VPN App 必须先安装 → VPN Profile → PKCS Certificate。VPN App 未安装时整个链路被阻塞
solution: 1) 确保 VPN App 先安装在设备上。2) 检查设备日志中的 NotNow 响应。3) VPN App 安装后触发手动同步。4) 考虑将证书和 VPN profile 分开部署
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/PKCS/PKCS Certificate Troubleshooting

symptom: Windows Device Configuration policy 显示 error (Remediation Failed) 但设备实际行为符合预期，设置已确认在 MDMDiagReport 和 Registry 中
rootCause: CSP 不支持 Get 操作。Intune 下发 Add/Replace 操作后，通过 Get 操作验证值，如果 Get 返回值与下发值不匹配或 CSP 不支持 Get，则报告 compliance error (remediation failed)。这是 by design 行为
solution: 确认 CSP 文档中是否支持 Add/Get/Replace/Delete 操作。如果 CSP 不支持 Get 操作（如 Account CSP），设置会报 failed 但实际已生效，属于预期行为。参考: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/client-management/mdm/configuration-service-provider-reference
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows

symptom: Single-app/Multi-app Kiosk profile 在 Hybrid Azure AD Joined 设备上部署失败，报 AssignedAccessConfigurationRS5 / -2016281112 (Remediation Failed)
rootCause: Kiosk profile 在 Hybrid Azure AD Joined 设备上存在已知兼容性问题
solution: 参考内部文档: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/bdacb094-82fa-f990-dbcb-5d0c0075efeb
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows

symptom: 部署 Security Baseline 后无法通过 RDP 远程访问 PC，或 Miracast 投屏功能被阻止
rootCause: Security Baseline 默认启用了限制性安全设置，可能阻止 RDP 或 Miracast 相关功能
solution: RDP 问题参考: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/a1703643-7bcc-5cc6-810a-80299e59ceae。Miracast 问题参考: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/3a148351-3457-4471-8e29-ecfc5e36db2f。检查 Security Baseline 中的相关设置并根据业务需求调整
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows

symptom: Endpoint Security Firewall 端口配置使用逗号分隔时报 Invalid regEx format 错误
rootCause: Firewall 端口配置的格式验证使用正则表达式，逗号分隔格式不符合预期的输入格式
solution: 参考: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/8da0116c-a406-4690-ec83-7cc4e4e332db。使用正确的端口分隔格式
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows

symptom: PKCS 证书长时间处于 Pending 状态，Connector Event Log 显示 PkiCreateProcessor.UploadResults NullReferenceException
rootCause: Certificate Connector 的 PkiCreateProcessor.UploadResults 方法存在 bug，CA 成功签发证书后 Connector 上传结果到 Intune 时抛出 NullReferenceException，导致证书状态停留在 Status=0 (Pending)
solution: 1. 等待自动恢复（通常 2-4 小时）；2. 重启 Connector 服务可立即修复；3. 更新 Connector 到最新版本永久修复。Kusto 诊断：搜索 DownloadNewMessages 和 Upload 操作，如果有多次 Download 但无对应 Upload 则确认为 Connector 问题
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/PKCS/PKCS Certificate Troubleshooting

symptom: PKCS 证书 policy 已成功分配，设备已注册，但证书 Pending 数小时后才最终交付，Intune portal 无明显错误
rootCause: 多因素导致延迟：外部 CA 性能（负载/网络延迟）、Connector 服务器资源不足、设备 MDM 同步间隔（iOS/Android/Windows 默认 8 小时）、杀毒软件干扰 Connector Processing 文件夹
solution: 1. 检查 Connector Event Log 中的处理延迟；2. 监控 Connector 服务器资源；3. 为 Connector 文件夹添加杀毒排除项；4. 考虑部署多个 Connector 做负载均衡；5. 手动触发设备同步缩短等待
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/PKCS/PKCS Certificate Troubleshooting

symptom: iOS 设备 PKCS 证书显示 Pending 或 Not Applicable，VPN profile 安装失败，设备日志报 Dependent payload not found 或 2016341112 iOS device is currently busy
rootCause: iOS 上 PKCS 证书关联 VPN profile 时，Intune 强制执行部署依赖链：VPN App → VPN Profile → PKCS Certificate。VPN App 未安装时整条链被阻塞
solution: 1. 确保 VPN App 先安装到设备；2. 检查设备日志中的 NotNow 响应；3. VPN App 安装后触发手动同步；4. 考虑将证书和 VPN profile 分开部署
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Certificates Email VPN Wifi/PKCS/PKCS Certificate Troubleshooting

symptom: Intune 管理员通过 Portal 触发 On-Demand Remediation (Run Remediation) 远程操作后设备未执行脚本，WNS 推送通知未到达设备
rootCause: 设备上 Toast Notification 被 GPO 或 Intune 策略禁用，导致 Windows Push Notification Service 本地无法接收云通知（Event ID 1010 不触发），On-Demand Remediation 的 WNS 推送被阻塞
solution: 1. 检查设备注册表是否 ToastEnabled=0（被禁用）；2. 启用 Toast Notification（取消 GPO 限制或调整 Intune 策略）；3. 确认 WNS 服务正常运行；4. 注意：On-Demand Remediation 不需要脚本分配到用户/设备组，但必须启用 WNS
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Remediations

symptom: Windows Backup for Organizations 还原页面在 OOBE 中未显示，设备为 Hybrid Azure AD Joined
rootCause: Windows Backup 还原功能不支持 Hybrid Azure AD Join，仅支持纯 Entra ID joined 设备。同时不支持自部署模式、预配置设备、Autopilot 重置、Settings 手动注册、GP 注册、ConfigMgr 共管、共享/无用户设备
solution: 确认设备为纯 Entra joined + 用户驱动注册。还原要求：Windows 11 22H2 build 22621.3958+ 或 23H2 build 22631.3958+ 或 24H2 build 26100.4770+。若为 Hybrid join 场景，目前无法使用还原功能（计划后续支持）
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Backup for Organizations

symptom: Windows Backup 备份或还原时用户收到 You don't have access to this 或 You can't get there from here 错误
rootCause: 租户的 Conditional Access 策略干扰了 Windows Backup 的身份验证流程
solution: 参考 Microsoft 文档排查 Conditional Access 策略干扰: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/configuration/windows-backup/?tabs=intune#conditional-access-policy-interference
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Backup for Organizations

symptom: Windows Backup for Organizations 功能在 GCC-H 或 Mooncake (21Vianet) 环境不可用
rootCause: Windows Backup for Organizations 目前不支持 GCC-H 和 Mooncake 环境，Portal 配置租户级设置时有明确提示
solution: 此功能在 GCC-H/Mooncake 暂不支持，计划 1Q26CY 支持。告知客户等待功能上线
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Backup for Organizations
Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Hello for Business
symptom: WHFB provisioning UI fails to load, CloudExperienceHost app cannot launch, ShellCore_Oper.evtx shows ClearTemporaryWebDataAsyncFailed
rootCause: CXH app cache not cleared properly, known bug on OS versions below Nickel (22H2)
solution: WAM recovery: 1) Rename %localappdata%\Packages\Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy to .backup 2) Add-AppxPackage -Register to re-register 3) Rename HKCU\Software\Microsoft\IdentityCRL\TokenBroker\DefaultAccount to _backup 4) Reboot

sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Hello for Business
symptom: WHFB PIN sign-in fails with The request is not supported, DC System log Event ID 19/29 KDC cannot find suitable certificate
rootCause: DC missing certificate with Kerberos Authentication EKU, or old certificate selected by lsass
solution: 1) Verify 2016 DC has Kerberos Auth EKU cert 2) Import new cert to AD DS store and restart DC if multiple certs exist 3) certutil -verify -urlfetch to validate 4) Verify issuing CA in NTAuth store

sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Hello for Business
symptom: WHFB provisioning fails at ADFS redirect with Error Code: 0X801C044F
rootCause: ADFS configuration issue causing device registration service authentication failure
solution: Collect ADFS logs and client User Device Registration/Admin event logs, verify ADFS DRS configuration

sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Hello for Business
symptom: WHFB PIN sign-in fails with KDC certificate could not be validated, System Event ID 9 Kerberos
rootCause: DC certificate chain validation failure (CRL revocation check or root trust issue)
solution: 1) Export DC cert as .cer 2) certutil -verify -urlfetch 3) Fix PKI CRL/root cert issues 4) Verify CA in NTAuth store 5) Enable CAPI2 logs

sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Hello for Business
symptom: WHFB configured but all Sign-in Options grayed out in Settings
rootCause: AllowSignInOptions registry value set to 0 by Intune policy or GPO
solution: Set HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\Settings\AllowSignInOptions Value to 1, check Intune/GPO policies

sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Hello for Business
symptom: Key Admins and Enterprise Key Admins security group names not displayed after AD 2016 schema upgrade
rootCause: RID 526/527 friendly name resolution requires Windows Server 2016 DC to hold PDC FSMO role
solution: Move PDC Emulator FSMO to 2016 DC: Move-ADDirectoryServerOperationMasterRole -Identity dc2016 -OperationMasterRole PDCEmulator

sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Hello for Business
symptom: Biometric data (fingerprint/facial recognition) lost after Windows in-place upgrade
rootCause: Biometric device had Allow computer to turn off this device to save power checked before upgrade
solution: Before upgrade, uncheck Allow computer to turn off this device to save power for biometric reader and camera devices

sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Hello for Business
symptom: WHFB provisioning fails after cross-domain migration with error 0x801C0451 (DSREG_E_USER_TOKEN_SWITCH_ACCOUNT)
rootCause: WAM cache contains stale tokens from old domain
solution: 1) certutil -deletehellocontainer 2) dsregcmd /leave + /join if device also migrated 3) Clear WAM cache: delete files in %LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy\AC\TokenBroker\Accounts\ 4) Reboot and re-enroll WHFB

sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Hello for Business
symptom: Hybrid AADJ device user cannot get PRT (AzureAdPrt: NO), WHFB/CA/SSO all fail
rootCause: User UPN prefix changed after device registration, CloudAP plugin uses cached old UPN
solution: Fixed in Windows 10 RS4 (1803) - CloudAP uses login credentials instead of cache. For older versions: re-register device or upgrade to 1803+

sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/Windows/Windows Hello for Business
symptom: WHFB Key Trust PIN sign-in fails with That option is temporarily unavailable
rootCause: Client can only access RODC, msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute not replicated to RODC
solution: Ensure client has access to writable Domain Controller (Writable DC), RODC does not support WHFB Key Trust sign-in

symptom: User sees 'Recovery key could not be retrieved' when trying to access BitLocker recovery key from Company Portal website
rootCause: Entra ID device setting 'Restrict non-admin users from recovering BitLocker keys' is set to 'Yes'
solution: In Entra ID > Devices > Device settings, change the BitLocker key restriction setting to 'No' to allow users to recover their own keys. Alternatively, an admin can retrieve the key on behalf of the user.

symptom: macOS custom profile (DLP/TCC payload) fails with error 'The profile must be a system profile. User profiles are not supported' in syslog_intune.log (NSPOSIXErrorDomain Code=1)
rootCause: Profile was deployed to a user group instead of a device group. TCC and certain payloads require device-level (system profile) assignment and cannot be installed as user profiles.
solution: Reassign the custom configuration profile to a device group instead of a user group. TCC/Privacy Preferences, DLP, and similar payloads must be delivered as system profiles (device-scoped assignment).
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/macOS

symptom: Error uploading macOS shell script (.sh) in Intune console - script preview shows extra Unicode characters (e.g. 'i p c o n f i g' with embedded spaces, BOM header)
rootCause: Script file saved with Unicode/UTF-16 encoding, injecting non-standard Unicode BOM and wide-character spacing into the file content
solution: Copy the script content to a new file in Notepad, then Save As with ANSI encoding (or UTF-8 without BOM). Re-upload the corrected .sh file to Intune.
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/macOS

symptom: macOS Platform SSO: 'app-sso platform -s' command shows null output after enrollment — registration not completed
rootCause: Either the com.apple.extensiblesso Profile is missing from System Settings (policy not assigned), or user clicked 'Remove account from this device' in Company Portal which deletes the PRT and primaryrefresh key from keychain
solution: Cause 1: Verify user/device is assigned to the Platform SSO device configuration profile. Cause 2: In Company Portal, click user account top-right > 'Add account to this device' to re-register.
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/macOS/PlatformSSO macOS

symptom: macOS Platform SSO password sync fails with 'window shake' error during registration when Per-User MFA is enabled on the user account
rootCause: Per-User MFA blocks the Platform SSO registration flow. This does not occur when MFA is enforced via Conditional Access.
solution: Disable Per-User MFA and use Conditional Access MFA instead, which suppresses MFA during enrollment allowing password synchronization to complete successfully.
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/macOS/PlatformSSO macOS

symptom: macOS Platform SSO password synchronization silently fails — local password not updated to match Entra ID password
rootCause: MDM passcode policy specifies higher complexity requirements than the Microsoft Entra ID account password policy, causing a mismatch that silently blocks sync
solution: Ensure password complexity requirements between local machine passcode policy (MDM) and Microsoft Entra ID password policy are aligned. Intune password/compliance policy must match Entra ID password policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/macOS/PlatformSSO macOS

symptom: macOS Platform SSO device registration fails silently — no error message shown to user
rootCause: User does not have permission to join/register devices in Microsoft Entra ID. The setting 'Users may join devices to Microsoft Entra' under Devices > Device Settings is not set to All or does not include the user.
solution: In Entra ID portal > Devices > Device Settings: set 'Users may join devices to Microsoft Entra' to 'All', or if 'Selected', ensure the registering user is included in the list.
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/macOS/PlatformSSO macOS

symptom: macOS Platform SSO: Platform Credential not available as passkey option in browser Sign-in Options after setup
rootCause: Company Portal is not enabled under 'Use passwords and passkeys from' in macOS System Settings > Passwords. There is no MDM setting to manage this automatically.
solution: Open System Settings > Passwords and enable 'Company Portal' under 'Use passwords and passkeys from'. This must be done manually by the user.
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Device Config Features Restrictions and Custom/macOS/PlatformSSO macOS

symptom: EPM automatic elevation rule Match=False for target file — elevation does not occur despite policy being assigned
rootCause: Certificate payload in the elevation rule does not match the actual certificate of the target executable (or other rule criteria mismatch)
solution: Check EpmServiceLogs (EpmService_YYYYMMDD_0.log) for 'Match=False;Message=' entries. The Message field explains the mismatch (e.g., certificate type/payload validation failed). Fix the rule's certificate/hash/file criteria to match the target executable.

1) Verify OS is supported version. 2) Check registry key contains PrivilegeManagement. 3) Ensure EPM client is Enabled in EPM Client Settings and assigned to device. 4) Restart IME (Microsoft Intune Management Extension) service, then recheck registry. 5) Verify dual enrollment via Event Viewer Event 4023. 6) If Event 4022 (endpoint address URL is invalid), ensure nothing blocks *.dm.microsoft.com and no SSL inspection on manage.microsoft.com/dm.microsoft.com.
symptom: EPM Client Settings policy shows error: Allow Device Health Monitoring state = noncompliant
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Endpoint Security/Endpoint Privilege Management/Troubleshooting Guide for Endpoint Privilege Management
rootCause: Registry key HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\current\device\DeviceHealthMonitoring\ConfigDeviceHealthMonitoringScope does not contain PrivilegeManagement — EPM client not enabled or IME service not restarted after policy assignment

solution: 1) Verify OS is supported. 2) Check registry HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\current\device\DeviceHealthMonitoring\DHMScopeValue contains PrivilegeManagement. 3) Ensure EPM client is enabled in EPM Client Settings and deployed. 4) Restart IME and recheck. 5) Verify default elevation settings are not set to deny all.
symptom: EPM error: organization doesnt allow you to run this app as administrator — elevation blocked
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Endpoint Security/Endpoint Privilege Management/Troubleshooting Guide for Endpoint Privilege Management
rootCause: EPM client not enabled (registry DHMScopeValue missing PrivilegeManagement) or default EPM elevation settings set to deny all

solution: 1) On Windows 11 select Show more options in right-click menu. 2) Verify OS is supported. 3) Verify EPM client enabled and targeted via Elevation Settings policy. 4) Check dual enrollment: Task Scheduler > Microsoft > Windows > EnterpriseMgmt — two GUIDs should be present (may take up to 15 min). 5) Check C:\Program Files\Microsoft EPM Agent exists. 6) Verify EPM Service is running in Task Manager. 7) Validate policy received via EPM PowerShell module at C:\Program Files\Microsoft EPM Agent\EpmTools.
symptom: EPM: missing right-click Run with elevated access menu option on Windows device
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Endpoint Security/Endpoint Privilege Management/Troubleshooting Guide for Endpoint Privilege Management
rootCause: On Windows 11 the option is hidden under Show more options context menu; or EPM client not properly enrolled (agent not installed, service not running)

solution: 1) Wait 24 hours for reports to process. 2) Verify OS supported. 3) Verify EPM Reporting enabled in EPM Client Settings. 4) Check registry ConfigDeviceHealthMonitoringScope contains PrivilegeManagement. 5) Check sensor.log exists at C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\IntuneManagementExtension\Logs. 6) In sensor.log search for GUID e4cd0c46-8d75-4d93-b5ac-99cf25388591 (diagnostic events) and 2ef6314a-cc15-487d-abfc-24a02cc9180f (elevation requests).
symptom: EPM elevation reporting data missing — no elevation reports appearing in Intune admin center
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Endpoint Security/Endpoint Privilege Management/Troubleshooting Guide for Endpoint Privilege Management
rootCause: EPM elevation reports take 24 hours to populate; or EPM Reporting not enabled in Client Settings; or DeviceHealthMonitoringScope registry missing PrivilegeManagement; or sensor.log not present

symptom: MDE Attach device does not show up in Intune UX All Devices list view; Rave shows Last Contact Time before July 15 2022 and IsDeleted=true
rootCause: Bug in MDE enrollment flow caused device records to be soft-deleted in StatelessDeviceService. Fixed for devices enrolled after July 15 2022.
solution: Verify via Rave: Last Contact Time < July 15 2022 AND IsDeleted=true. If confirmed, transfer ticket to OCE team (Hani Chabban) to fix backend data. For OCE: check StatelessDeviceService via GenevaAction (Search Entity, query Devices?$filter=ReferenceId eq guid'<AADDeviceId>'), then SQLAgg, then MMPC CosmosDB.

symptom: MDE Attach policy deployment fails with error 2146233088 'Invalid flags specified' in Sense Event Viewer (SenseCM records)
rootCause: Firewall rule contains an incorrect/invalid value that cannot be applied by the MDE Attach client.
solution: Check Sense Event Viewer (Application and Services logs → Microsoft → Windows → Sense) for error details. Review the Firewall rule configuration in Intune/Defender portal and correct the invalid value. Verify supported profiles for MDE Attach at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/protect/mde-security-integration

symptom: Customer has Defender licensing but MEM (Microsoft Endpoint Manager) portal is inaccessible; no Intune license
rootCause: Missing Intune_Defender service plan. This service plan is embedded with all MDE Products and is required for MEM access without a full Intune license.
solution: Check if Intune_Defender service plan is provisioned on the tenant. All accounts with Defender Products should have this included entitlement. If missing, investigate the products and entitlements. Once Intune_Defender is provisioned: 1) Intune polls tenant, 2) queues tenant provisioning workflow, 3) returns success. Customer can then use MDE Attach, access Endpoint Security blade, but cannot enroll non-MDE Attach devices without licensed users.

symptom: MDE Attach tenant not found error; device enrollment fails because tenant onboarding to MMPC did not complete
rootCause: MDEAttachOnboardingState is neither 0 (default) nor 1 (succeeded), indicating an onboarding failure in PartnerTenantService → MMPCSync → MMPC chain.
solution: 1) Query PartnerTenantService logs in Intune Kusto for onboarding events. 2) Get GenevaAction JIT access and check onboarding state: if MDEAttachEnabled=false → no action (admin didn't flip toggle). If MDEAttachEnabled=true and MDEAttachOnboardingState not 0 or 1 → patch MDEAttachEnabled=true via GenevaAction to re-trigger onboarding. Verify MDEAttachOnboardingState becomes 1.

symptom: MDE device compliance shows Deactivated state in Intune; device marked as non-compliant despite being managed
rootCause: Device exceeded the compliance grace period (typically 7 or 14 days configured in Intune > Endpoint Security > MDE > 'Number of days until partner is unresponsive'). No heartbeat signal received from MDE within threshold.
solution: 1) Query wcdprod Kusto IntuneDeviceStateUpdates for DeviceState. 2) If Deactivated, check MachineInfoEvents for heartbeat gaps via ReportArrivalTimeUtc. 3) Investigate why device stopped sending heartbeats (network, device offline, MDE client issue). 4) Once device resumes heartbeats, compliance will auto-update.

symptom: Security baseline shows 'Conflict' status on device; two settings applied to same device with conflicting values
rootCause: Multiple security baselines or device configuration profiles configure the same setting with different values. Intune cannot resolve the conflict automatically.
solution: 1) Use Kusto query to identify conflicting settings: query IntuneEvent where Col1 contains 'conflict' and ApplicationName startswith 'DeviceCheckin'. 2) Review all assigned baselines and device config profiles for overlapping settings. 3) Modify or remove conflicting profiles. KB: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/60d80b3a-8db4-a7de-670f-02a6f19b5d6d

symptom: Security baseline shows 'Pending' status; device never receives the policy
rootCause: Device hasn't checked in with Intune, or user/device is not in the effective group membership for the assigned group.
solution: 1) Confirm device last check-in is recent (force sync if needed). 2) Verify user/device is direct member of assigned group. 3) Check Azure deviceId/objectId matches group membership in ASC. 4) Troubleshoot effective group membership: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/63526f19-9ef6-9b79-d381-d8ac8dda1ee5

symptom: Security baseline shows 'Error' status; setting fails to apply due to scope or applicability issue
rootCause: Setting either assigned to wrong scope (user vs device) or device does not support the CSP (Configuration Service Provider) for the setting.
solution: 1) Check error code in Intune portal for explanation. 2) Review CSP pre-requirements for each failing setting. 3) Verify device OS version supports the setting. KB: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/47625e81-41a4-d0d2-6fa2-93e0adbd3d59

symptom: BitLocker silent encryption fails with Event ID 851: 'Group Policy settings for BitLocker startup options are in conflict and cannot be applied'
rootCause: 'Configure TPM startup PIN' (or 'Configure TPM startup key'/'Configure TPM startup key and PIN') is set to 'Require' in the BitLocker policy, which conflicts with silent encryption requirements.
solution: Change 'Configure TPM startup PIN' to either 'Allow' or 'Do not allow'. Same applies to 'Configure TPM startup key' and 'Configure TPM startup key and PIN'. Check BitLocker Management event log (Microsoft-Windows-BitLocker/BitLocker Management) for error details.

symptom: Devices encrypted with 128-bit instead of 256-bit as specified in BitLocker policy, especially during Autopilot provisioning
rootCause: Windows BitLocker automatic device encryption starts during OOBE before the Intune policy is applied. The automatic encryption uses 128-bit by default. Protection is suspended until user signs in with AAD account.
solution: Configure 'Automatic encryption during AADJ: Block' in device restriction policy. This prevents Windows auto-encryption from pre-empting the Intune BitLocker policy. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/autopilot/bitlocker

symptom: BitLocker encryption strength remains 128-bit after deploying 256-bit policy to already-encrypted drive
rootCause: Drive was already encrypted before the BitLocker policy was deployed. BitLocker encryption settings (including key strength) are applied only at time of encryption. Policy deployed post-encryption does not change existing encryption.
solution: Customer needs to decrypt the drive first, then let the BitLocker policy trigger re-encryption with the correct 256-bit setting. BitLocker key escrow also only happens at time of encryption.

symptom: BitLocker recovery key not escrowed to Azure AD; only saved to local Active Directory on Hybrid Azure AD joined Autopilot devices
rootCause: During Hybrid Azure AD joined Autopilot provisioning with BitLocker policy assigned to device group, local domain join happens before Hybrid Azure AD join. Device saves recovery key to the first location found (local AD). This is Windows behavior, not Intune/Autopilot.
solution: 1) Assign BitLocker policy to user group instead of device group (may still fail if hybrid Azure join doesn't complete in time). 2) Use a script to save recovery key to Azure (CSS doesn't support scripting). 3) Rotate BitLocker keys in bulk using a script. 4) For existing devices, check local Active Directory for recovery key using BitLocker Recovery Password Viewer on computer object.

symptom: Unhealthy Endpoint Report shows incorrect device status in MEM Admin Center; mismatch between portal data and actual device state
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: 1) Defender AV agent reporting incorrect status (MDE issue), 2) Intune reporting service not reflecting collected data correctly (Intune PG issue), 3) Device check-in delay.
solution: 1) Run Get-MpComputerStatus on device to get actual ComputerState/AMRunningMode/DefenderSignaturesOutOfDate. 2) Query Intune Kusto (IntuneDefenderServices/DefenderBTService/ProcessDefenderReportBTProvider) for collected data. 3) Compare: if PowerShell != actual state → engage MDE; if Kusto != UI → escalate to Intune PG; if all match → no issue.

symptom: Windows LAPS Event 10059: 'Local admin password solution is not enabled for this tenant' — password backup to Azure AD fails
rootCause: The 'Enable Azure AD Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS)' toggle in Azure AD Device Settings is set to No (default)
solution: Navigate to Azure AD > Devices > Device settings, toggle 'Enable Azure AD Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS)' to Yes. Can also enable via Graph API PUT to /policies/deviceRegistrationPolicy with {localAdminPassword:{isEnabled:true}}

symptom: Windows LAPS Reset-LapsPassword fails with hr:0x80070190 (ERROR_THREAD_MODE_ALREADY_BACKGROUND) and Event 10028 'LAPS failed to update Azure Active Directory with the new password'
rootCause: LAPS is not enabled at the tenant level in Azure AD Device Settings
solution: Enable LAPS in Azure AD Device Settings (toggle to Yes). The 0x80070190 error and Event 10028 are secondary symptoms of the tenant-level LAPS setting being disabled

symptom: Windows LAPS Event 10059 HTTP 400: 'The device {deviceId} in {tenantId} could not be found' — password backup fails with error_missing_device
rootCause: The device object has been removed from the Azure AD tenant
solution: Verify device registration status with 'dsregcmd /status' on the client. If device was deleted, re-register the device to Azure AD. The device must exist and be enabled in Azure AD for LAPS password backup to work

symptom: Windows LAPS Event 10059 HTTP 403: 'The specified request is not allowed for tenant' — authorization_error when backing up password
rootCause: The device has been disabled in Azure AD
solution: Check device status in Azure AD portal — re-enable the device if it was disabled. LAPS requires the device to be in enabled state for password rotation and backup

symptom: Windows LAPS Event 10025: 'Azure discovery failed' with error 0x80072EE7 (ERROR_ADAL_INTERNET_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED) — LAPS cannot reach Azure endpoint
rootCause: Network connectivity issue — the client cannot resolve enterpriseregistration.windows.net
solution: Verify network connectivity from the client by running 'curl https://enterpriseregistration.windows.net/ -D -' in an admin command prompt. If it fails, work with network team to ensure the endpoint is accessible (firewall/proxy rules)

symptom: MDM authority is set to Office365 and customer wants to change it to Intune — no SAW action available to switch from Office365MDM or Unknown to Intune
rootCause: MDM authority was initially set to Office365 and once Intune is enabled as MDM authority it cannot be reverted
solution: 1) Ensure user is Global Admin with Intune license (wait ~10 min for propagation). 2) Go to Intune Education portal (https://intuneeducation.portal.azure.com). 3) Navigate to Tenant Settings > Device enrollment Setup and click 'Manage devices with Microsoft Intune'. 4) Verify in Intune console under Tenant Administration > Tenant Status

symptom: MDM authority shows 'Unknown' in Intune console — cannot set MDM authority, Kusto Account_Snapshot shows MDM Authority as Unknown
rootCause: Customer purchased Intune P2 add-on before assigning a P1 license, or P1 license expired before P2 was added, skipping vital provisioning steps
solution: If P2 purchased before P1: create ICM for destructive tenant move. Workaround (if P1 license exists): go to Endpoint Manager > Troubleshooting + Support > Guided Scenarios (preview) > click 'Setup a test device'. Warning: do NOT use workaround without P1 license as it may cause further issues

symptom: Microsoft Intune Enrollment cloud app (AppId d4ebce55-015a-49b5-a083-c84d1797ae8c) not available in Conditional Access for new tenants — cannot target enrollment MFA separately from admin center MFA
rootCause: The Microsoft Intune Enrollment service principal is not created automatically for new tenants
solution: Run PowerShell: Connect-AzureAD; New-AzureADServicePrincipal -AppId d4ebce55-015a-49b5-a083-c84d1797ae8c. After creation, the Intune Enrollment cloud app will appear in Conditional Access policies allowing separate MFA control for enrollment vs admin center access

symptom: Managed Google Play (MGP) account upgrade to Entra identity fails with 'Domain is already being used' error during Google Enterprise upgrade flow
rootCause: The domain is already registered with another Google service or enterprise account
solution: Customer must contact Google Support directly to resolve the domain conflict. Reference: https://support.google.com/a/answer/80610

symptom: Managed Google Play account upgrade fails with generic 'Service Error' at Intune portal level during Google API upgrade flow
rootCause: Transient error from Google Enterprise Mobility Management (EMM) API service
solution: 1) Retry after some time. 2) If error persists, escalate — potentially involving Google support. Engage SME to evaluate need for ICM. If user fails to complete upgrade flow, tenant falls back to MGP automatically

symptom: Cannot perform MGP account upgrade — original user who connected Managed Google Play no longer exists, and no Google Super Admin is available to complete the upgrade
rootCause: MGP upgrade requires Google Super Admin role which was held by the user who initially connected MGP. If that user or GA account no longer exists, upgrade cannot proceed
solution: Customer can either request Google to provide current Super Admin details or initiate a request to assign a new Super Admin. Reference ICM: 617111109

symptom: MDE Attached device enrolled before July 15th 2022 doesn't show up in Intune UX All Devices list view — device shows IsDeleted=true in Rave and Last Contact Time is before July 15th 2022
rootCause: Bug in MDE enrollment flow caused devices enrolled before July 15th 2022 to be marked IsDeleted=true in StatelessDeviceService. Bug has been fixed for enrollments after that date
solution: Verify symptoms: 1) Last Contact Time before July 15th 2022 in Rave 2) IsDeleted=true in Device Info. If confirmed, transfer ticket to OCE team (Hani Chabban's team) with symptoms documented — OCE will fix the customer data on backend

symptom: Customer has Defender licensing but MEM Portal / Endpoint Security blade is inaccessible — cannot access Intune console for MDE Attach management
rootCause: Tenant is missing the Intune_Defender service plan which is required for Defender entitlement to grant access to MEM. All MDE Products should include Intune_Defender service plan
solution: Investigate the tenant's products and entitlements — verify Intune_Defender service plan is provisioned. Once provisioned: 1) Intune polls tenant based on Intune_Defender presence 2) Queues tenant provisioning workflow 3) Customer can then access Endpoint Security blade and use MDE Attach

symptom: MDE Attach tenant onboarding fails — tenant not found in MMPC, MDEAttachOnboardingState is neither 0 (default) nor 1 (succeeded)
rootCause: Failure in the onboarding flow from PartnerTenantService → MMPCSync → MMPC when admin flips the MDE onboard toggle. OnboardingFE calls PartnerTenantService which calls MMPCSync to onboard tenant
solution: 1) Check MDEAttachEnabled and MDEAttachOnboardingState via GenevaAction JIT access 2) If MDEAttachEnabled=true but OnboardingState failed, patch MDEAttachEnabled to true from GenevaAction to manually re-trigger onboarding 3) Verify OnboardingState becomes 1 4) Use Kusto queries to trace: IntuneEventTable for PartnerTenantService MDEOnboardingSuccess/Fail events, then correlate activityId to MMPC Kusto cluster

symptom: MDE device shows DeviceState=Deactivated in IntuneDeviceStateUpdates — device compliance status not syncing between MDE and Intune, device marked non-compliant
rootCause: Device has exceeded the compliance grace period (configurable 7 or 14 days via 'Number of days until partner is unresponsive' in Endpoint Security > MDE settings). No heartbeat signal received from device to MDE backend within threshold
solution: 1) Check IntuneDeviceStateUpdates Kusto query for UploadTime and DeviceState 2) Investigate heartbeat gaps via MachineInfoEvents in wcdprod cluster 3) Verify device connectivity and MDE client health 4) Check 'Number of days until partner is unresponsive' setting in Intune portal. See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-MDE-Compliance.md

symptom: Security baseline profile shows 'Conflict' status on device — two settings applied to same device with conflicting values from different baselines or device configuration profiles
rootCause: Multiple security baselines or device configuration profiles targeting same device contain conflicting values for the same setting. Can occur when using multiple baseline types or multiple instances of same baseline with different customizations
solution: 1) Use Kusto query with Col1 contains 'conflict' on IntuneEvent table to identify conflicting settings 2) Review per-setting status in Intune portal: Endpoint Security > Security Baselines > select baseline > Device status 3) Compare settings across all assigned baselines and device configuration profiles 4) Remove duplicate/conflicting settings. Reference: internal KB 60d80b3a-8db4-a7de-670f-02a6f19b5d6d

symptom: Security baseline profile shows 'Pending' status — device not receiving or applying security baseline policy
rootCause: Device may not have checked in recently, or user/device is not a direct member of the assigned group, or effective group membership issue
solution: 1) Confirm device last check-in status is recent and force sync 2) Confirm user/device is direct member of assigned group 3) Verify Azure deviceId/objectId matches group membership in ASC 4) Troubleshoot effective group membership (internal KB 63526f19-9ef6-9b79-d381-d8ac8dda1ee5)

symptom: Security baseline profile shows 'Error' status — security setting failed to apply on device
rootCause: Typically related to scope (wrong assignment to user vs device group) or applicability (device doesn't support the CSP or doesn't meet prerequisites for the setting)
solution: 1) Review CSP pre-requirements for each security setting 2) Check if device supports the setting version 3) Follow internal KB 47625e81-41a4-d0d2-6fa2-93e0adbd3d59 for CSP verification 4) Review error code explanation linked in Intune portal

symptom: Endpoint Security support boundary reference — which teams own which security features when setting applies correctly but feature doesn't work as expected
solution: Support boundaries: Defender Application Guard → Windows UEX | Firewall → Windows Networking | SmartScreen → Azure Security | Encryption → MSaaS Windows Devices | Exploit Guard/ASR → Azure Security | Application Control/Credential Guard → MSaaS Windows Devices | Security Center → Azure Security | Antivirus → Azure Security | Account Protection → MSaaS (credential guard) or Azure Identity (WHFB). All Windows ARs must first post in SME channel with WindowsAR tag

symptom: Endpoint security policy conflicts with security baselines or device configuration profiles — same setting managed by multiple policy types results in error/conflict on device
rootCause: Managing the same setting on the same device through multiple policy types (endpoint security policies, security baselines, device configuration profiles) or multiple instances of the same policy type. Security baselines set non-default values while endpoint security policies default to 'Not configured'
solution: 1) Develop a plan for using multiple policy types to minimize conflict risk 2) All policy types (security baselines, device configuration, endpoint security) are treated as equal sources by Intune 3) Review all assigned policies for overlapping settings 4) Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/mem/intune/troubleshoot-policies-in-microsoft-intune

symptom: Driver disappeared from Driver Update policy — specific driver no longer visible in Intune driver update policy
rootCause: OEM removed driver from MS Catalog; or driver aged out because all applicable devices installed it; or OEM replaced driver with newer recommended version causing older to move to Other/removed category
solution: 1) Run Kusto DriverUpdateInventory_Snapshot query with PolicyId to check driver category and applicability 2) Category 2 = 'PreviouslyApproved - No Longer Applicable' means all devices installed it 3) If new devices added after removal, old deployment may still apply 4) To pause an unavailable driver, escalate via TA/TL
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Manage Software Updates/Windows/Driver Updates/Troubleshooting/Driver disappeared from policy

symptom: Driver Update policy shows empty — drivers not populated for a driver update policy in Intune
rootCause: Prerequisites not met (license or Diagnostic Data not enabled); or co-managed device configured to scan WSUS instead of Windows Update for driver updates
solution: 1) Run Kusto Account_Snapshot to verify license and DPSW (Diagnostic Data) enabled 2) For co-managed devices, configure scan source to Windows Update (not WSUS) 3) Check Event Viewer: Microsoft-Windows-WindowsUpdateClient/Operational for scan issues 4) Verify device EG targeting with DriverUpdateEffectiveGroupTargeting query 5) If all OK, escalate via TA/TL or driver update SME
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Manage Software Updates/Windows/Driver Updates/Troubleshooting/Driver policy is empty

symptom: Driver deployed by Intune causing issues on device — customer reports problems after driver installation via Intune driver update policy
rootCause: Faulty driver from OEM — Intune is just the delivery mechanism and does not control driver quality
solution: Intune only delivers drivers. If driver causes issues, engage TA/TL to discuss with Windows DND team or redirect customer to OEM vendor directly
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Manage Software Updates/Windows/Driver Updates/Troubleshooting/Driver update causing issues

symptom: Driver was installed without admin approval in Intune driver update policy
rootCause: Device assigned to multiple driver update policies (one policy approves what another declines); or Plug and Play driver auto-installed by OS for new hardware; or Extension driver not managed by deployment service
solution: 1) Check Kusto DriverUpdatePolicy/DriverUpdateInventory/DriverUpdatePerPolicyPerDeviceStatus to identify which policy deployed driver 2) Check ReportingEvents.log — MoUpdateOrchestrator = managed driver, Device Driver Retrieval (DDR) = PnP auto-install 3) Check Update_History.txt for 'Extension' keyword = extension driver not managed by Intune 4) Ensure device has only ONE driver update policy assigned
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Manage Software Updates/Windows/Driver Updates/Troubleshooting/Driver was installed without approval

symptom: Specific driver not being offered in Intune Driver Update policy — policy assigned but certain drivers missing
rootCause: Driver not uploaded to MS Catalog by OEM; or HWID mismatch (driver not applicable for customer devices); or backend issue
solution: 1) Search MS Catalog for driver by name/version 2) If not found, OEM hasn't submitted it 3) Verify HWID match: Device Manager > device properties > Hardware IDs vs MS Catalog package details 4) Run Get-WmiObject Win32_PnPSignedDriver for all HWIDs 5) Check Kusto DriverUpdateInventory for driver visibility 6) Check DriverUpdatePerPolicyPerDeviceStatus with CatalogId 7) If Pending/Scheduled >2 days or CatalogId missing, escalate via TA/TL
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Manage Software Updates/Windows/Driver Updates/Troubleshooting/Specific driver is not being offered

symptom: Unable to upload Win32 app larger than 8GB in Intune
rootCause: Tenant is unpaid/trial/free — Win32 upload limit is 8GB for unpaid tenants vs 30GB for paid tenants (changed globally 12/18/2023)
solution: Verify tenant is paid in Assist365. Paid tenants automatically have 30GB per app limit across all clouds. No flighting tag required since 12/18/2023. If paid tenant still fails at >8GB, follow normal escalation path — SAW team cannot help

symptom: Admin loses permissions after enabling 'Scoped Permissions' in Intune RBAC Settings — previously available actions now show access denied
rootCause: Enabling scoped permissions stops Intune from silently merging permissions across role assignments with different scope tags. Each role assignment's permissions now apply only within its own scope tag context
solution: 1) Review permissions assessment report (Tenant Admin > Roles > Settings) before enabling 2) This is intended to be permanent 3) If high business impact, escalate to CxE requesting removal of 'EnablePerPermissionScopeTags' flighting tag 4) Use Kusto IntuneEvent with TagOperation + 'EnablePerPermissionScopeTags' to find who/when enabled it
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Role Based Access Control/Enable Scoped Permissions

symptom: HTTP 403 Forbidden / Access denied error in Intune portal or Graph API — admin cannot perform specific action
rootCause: Missing RBAC permission in admin's assigned role. May need multiple permissions for a single task. Common when role was configured without all required permissions
solution: 1) Collect F12 HAR trace from browser 2) Find first 403 response to graph.microsoft.com 3) Extract 'Date:' and 'client-request-id:' from response header 4) Run Kusto SecuritySubsystem query with EffectiveUserPermissionAuthorizationValidationFailureEvent to identify exact missing permission in Col1 5) Add missing permission to RBAC role 6) Note: may need to repeat if multiple permissions missing. Data freshness: SecuritySubsystem has 15min delay, stored 30 days
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Role Based Access Control/Troubleshooting RBAC problems

symptom: Unlicensed admin gets access denied when using Graph API, PowerShell cmdlets, or Intune SDK apps — even though Unlicensed Admins feature is enabled
rootCause: Unlicensed Admins feature only applies to Intune portal (MEM). Graph API, PowerShell cmdlets, and SDK apps still require a user license. Kusto shows 'User has no EffectiveMemberPermissions'
solution: 1) Verify user has Intune license assigned (check in Rave user details) 2) Verify user has RBAC role assigned 3) Unlicensed admin access only works in MEM portal, not Graph/PowerShell/SDK. Customer must assign license for programmatic access
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Role Based Access Control/Troubleshooting RBAC problems

symptom: Exclude groups option missing for PowerShell scripts assignments in Intune UI — cannot exclude groups from script targeting
rootCause: Older tenant was built without exclude groups functionality for PowerShell scripts. Newer tenants have it by default. Requires flighting tag 'EnableGAndTForPowershell'
solution: 1) Submit SAW request to add flighting tag 'EnableGAndTForPowershell' via ICM/IET 2) WARNING: procedure removes ALL existing Windows PowerShell script assignments 3) Customer must capture current assignments before procedure (screenshots or Graph API export) 4) Requires GA approval 5) Only affects Windows platform scripts, not macOS 6) Tag also enables 'All Users/All Devices' option

symptom: Error 808-ZtdDeviceAssignedToOtherTenant or 806-ZtdDeviceAlreadyAssigned when importing Autopilot hardware hash — or OOBE shows another tenant's sign-in screen
rootCause: Hardware hash already exists in Autopilot service. Common causes: device bought used without prior deregistration, company acquisition, OEM motherboard replacement without deregistering donor board, device previously in test tenant
solution: 1) Search serial number in Assist365 > Applications > Intune > Device Search 2) If not found, collect device CSV + proof of ownership (Premier customers: only need confirmation of physical possession, no proof of purchase required) 3) Create case note with template (serial, CSV collected, proof collected, Context ID) 4) Submit SAW request form at aka.ms/SAWRequestForm selecting 'Autopilot device deregistration' 5) Process takes up to 2 weeks; if longer, email Intunesupportsaw@microsoft.com 6) For Surface devices: contact deviceAPEsc@microsoft.com

symptom: MDM authority shows SCCM/System Center Configuration Manager — customer needs to change to Microsoft Intune
rootCause: SCCM MDM is long deprecated. Intune removed the self-service ability to change MDM authority. Backend SAW action required
solution: 1) Requires GA approval 2) Prerequisites: remove config policies from All Users/All Devices, check Automatic Enrollment not set to All, review App Protection policies 3) Submit SAW request via ICM using aka.ms/sawgeneral template with Tenant ID, Context ID, ASUID 4) WARNING: all existing Intune policies and devices will be deleted. Zero impact to SCCM 5) Cannot revert from Intune back to SCCM or O365 6) Not available for GCC-H tenants via SAW

symptom: DeviceNotSupported error during Windows device enrollment - MDM Enroll Server Returned Fault DeviceNotSupported
rootCause: Enrollment Restriction (ER) policy has the device platform set to Block/Disabled. ErrorCode: EnrollmentRestrictionsPlatformDisabled. NOT a platform limitation.
solution: 1) Get F12 HAR trace 2) Find first 403 to graph.microsoft.com 3) Extract Date + client-request-id 4) Run Kusto DeviceLifecycle + IntuneEvent with cV to identify blocking ER policy 5) Modify ER policy or exclude user from target group 6) Wait up to 1 hour for group propagation

symptom: Android Teams device sign-in timing out during enrollment - CA evaluation blocks device before Intune inventory completes
rootCause: Device info propagated from Intune to Azure AD after enrollment takes 10-15 min. Unsupported CA policies evaluate before device properties are available.
solution: 1) Add TWO CA exclusion filters: Filter 1 on displayName=ManufacturerModel for initial sign-in; Filter 2 on manufacturer/model for after rename 2) Or add named location exclusions 3) Or remove user from unsupported CA policies

symptom: Android Teams devices periodically signed out - device requires re-authentication unexpectedly
rootCause: CA policy enforcing sign-in frequency causes periodic sign-out. Expected behavior, documented known issue.
solution: Review CA policies for sign-in frequency settings. Known issue: https://learn.microsoft.com/microsoftteams/troubleshoot/teams-rooms-and-devices/teams-android-devices-conditional-access-issues

symptom: Android Teams device compliance/configuration not working - error 50199 or 53002 in Azure AD sign-in logs
rootCause: Unsupported compliance or configuration settings assigned to Teams device. 50199: unsupported CA, DA not enabled, or enrollment restriction. 53002: compliance not evaluated fast enough or unsupported setting.
solution: 1) Verify only supported settings 2) Enable Android DA enrollment 3) Check enrollment restrictions 4) Check Tenant Admin > Customization enrollment allowed 5) Update device software via Teams Admin Center 6) Use CA What-If tool

symptom: Target OS list is empty when generating Update Device Readiness & Compatibility Risks Report
rootCause: Prerequisites not met. Device diagnostics not enabled under Tenant Administration
solution: Enable Device diagnostics under Tenant Admin per https://learn.microsoft.com/mem/intune/remote-actions/collect-diagnostics

symptom: No devices found when Update Readiness (MEM-UR) report is generated
rootCause: Multiple causes: telemetry AllowTelemetry <1, OneSettings disabled via GPO, OS too old, network endpoints blocked, DiagTrack disabled
solution: 1) Enable Windows data checkboxes under Tenant Admin > Connectors > Windows data 2) Check AllowTelemetry >=1 at both registry locations 3) Ensure DisableOneSettingsDownloads not 1 4) Verify OS build 5) Allow diagnostics endpoints 6) Confirm DiagTrack running 7) If DiagTrack issue engage Windows UEX

symptom: Update Readiness report generated but missing some devices
rootCause: Device may not meet OS version prerequisites, GPO conflicts with AllowTelemetry or DisableOneSettingsDownloads
solution: 1) Verify device meets OS prerequisites 2) Check for GPO overriding AllowTelemetry/DisableOneSettingsDownloads 3) Ensure device in assigned policy group 4) Follow no-devices-found steps

symptom: Device Readiness status shows Unknown in MEM-UR report
rootCause: Data processing at EDP did not generate expected results for Intune data processor
solution: Run compattelrunner.exe -m:appraiser.dll -f:DoScheduledTelemetryRun ent on impacted devices. Wait 24-48h (max 52h latency)

symptom: macOS legacy MDM-based software update policies deprecated, replaced by DDM
rootCause: Starting 2510 release, legacy MDM update policies deprecated. From 2602, no new legacy policies can be created
solution: Migrate to DDM-based policies at https://aka.ms/Intune/Apple-DDM-software-updates. Existing legacy policies remain active

symptom: Jamf Pro integration with Intune — macOS CA deprecated Sep 2024, migrate to Device Compliance
rootCause: Jamf Pro Conditional Access platform no longer supported since Sep 1 2024. Must migrate to Device Compliance integration
solution: Follow Jamf migration guide at Migrating from macOS Conditional Access to macOS Device Compliance. Contact Jamf Customer Success for help. See https://aka.ms/Intune/Jamf-Device-Compliance

symptom: Zebra Mobility Extensions (MX) integration with Intune — DA management ending Aug 2024 for GMS devices
rootCause: Android device administrator management ending on GMS devices Aug 30 2024. Must switch to Android Enterprise
solution: Switch to Android Enterprise management before DA support ends. See https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/intune-customer-success/microsoft-intune-ending-support-for-android-device-administrator/ba-p/3915443

symptom: Customer requests Root Cause Analysis (RCA) for tenant-specific Intune issue
rootCause: Intune Engineering Team only provides PIR (Post Incident Report) for Live Site Incidents (LSIs); no tenant-specific RCAs are supported. Intune does not provide RCAs because they are not supported by the Intune Engineering Team.
solution: Share best-effort troubleshooting findings with customer to help avoid recurrence (not a formal RCA). For LSI PIRs not received by customer, contact IntuneExtPIR_Review@microsoft.com with the specific LSI details. For Azure service incidents: Azure RCA available on Azure portal or via https://aka.ms/iridias (CSS/TAM/Field). For O365 service incidents: PIR available in O365 portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Engineer Reference/Delivery Partners/Internal Processes/RCA(Root Cause Analysis)

symptom: Engineer bookmarks and links to www.intunewiki.com (original Intune PG Wiki) fail to resolve after February 2026 decommission
rootCause: The original Intune PG Wiki (www.intunewiki.com) was fully decommissioned in February 2026; all saved bookmarks and links break after decommission. DFM Knowledge (Evergreen) links have not been automatically updated.
solution: Use the Supportability wiki instead: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/Intune/_wiki/wikis/Intune/1321070/Welcome — as of January 8 2026 all links in this wiki were already updated to point to the Engineering Hub. For engineering-level content, access the Engineering Hub via https://eng.ms/docs/microsoft-security/management/intune/... (requires ReadOnly access via CoreIdentity).

symptom: Error AADSTS5000224 when authenticating to test tenant: 'We are sorry, this resource is not available. If you are seeing this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support'
rootCause: Test tenant was deprovisioned by MCAPS, typically due to policy violation, expiration, or inactivity. The Entra tenant ID may appear in ICM with an explanation.
solution: Go to https://aka.ms/tenantreauthentication, enter the Entra tenant ID, add a short description, attach a director-level (M2+ with 'Director' in GAL title) FTE approval screenshot, select prod environment and sev 4, select the correct cloud instance. Submit ICM to request restoration. Act within a couple of days of deprovisioning for best chance of recovery. Fairfax requires CVP-level approval.

symptom: Sign-in errors 'Insufficient or lack of permissions is causing access restriction' when trying to access Intune or Azure portals in an MCAPS test tenant that previously worked
rootCause: Licenses in the MCAPS test tenant have expired (1-year lifespan) and been disabled (30 days after expiration)
solution: Sign into the Microsoft 365 admin portal (admin.microsoft.com) with the tenant GA account and verify that licenses have not been disabled. To extend expiring licenses, open a ticket at https://aka.ms/mcapssupport with Category=Azure, Sub-Category=Licensing, Topics='License Extension (User Tenant)', and provide your Subscription ID and Tenant ID from the Managed Environments portal. Extension may take up to one week.

symptom: 'Oops! Page Not Found' (404 error) when trying to open a Cloud Academy training link from the SCIM Readiness Tool search results
rootCause: User is not logged into Cloud Academy. The 404 error is misleading — it is actually an authentication redirect, not a truly missing page. Cloud Academy shows a 404-style page for unauthenticated access to restricted training content.
solution: Look for the 'Go back to login' link shown below the 404 error text. Click it, log in to Cloud Academy, and the page will auto-redirect to the correct training content.

symptom: Videos do not play in the Readiness Tool Search Content page — video player shows no content or fails to load
rootCause: User is not authenticated to the SharePoint site (microsoft.sharepoint.com/teams/LearnCSSintune) hosting the Stream video content. The Readiness Tool embeds video directly from that site and requires an active session.
solution: Option 1: Log into the Intune SharePoint site at https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/teams/LearnCSSintune, then reload the Readiness Tool and retry. Option 2: Use the 'Copy Video URL' button below the video title to copy the URL to clipboard, then open it in a new tab directly.

symptom: Personal Windows device users are unexpectedly enrolled in Intune MDM when adding a work or school account; user clicks 'Allow my organization to manage my device' prompt and personal device becomes organization-managed (BYOD scenario breakage)
rootCause: When a work/school account is added to a personal Windows device, Windows displays an automatic MDM enrollment prompt. If the user clicks OK, the device is auto-enrolled in Intune, which is undesired for BYOD scenarios. No admin control was previously available to suppress this prompt.
solution: Enable the 'Disable MDM enrollment when adding work or school account on Windows' setting in Intune > Devices > Enrollment > Automatic Enrollment page. This prevents automatic MDM enrollment when users add work/school accounts on personal devices. Feature is in public preview — verify status before production deployment. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/intune-service/enrollment/windows-enroll (point 7)
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Engineer Reference/Tip of the Week/Disable Allow My Organization to Manage My Device Prompt

symptom: User Rights Management policy (Add/Replace/Remove users or groups) fails to apply correctly or targets wrong accounts when deployed to Windows devices with non-English OS language; policy referencing group display names doesn't match localized Windows built-in group names
rootCause: Windows built-in group and account display names are locale-dependent and differ across OS language packs. Intune/Defender/ConfigMgr policies that reference users/groups by display name (e.g., 'Administrators') will not match correctly on non-English Windows installations where the display name differs.
solution: Use Security Identifiers (SIDs) instead of display names when configuring user rights management policies. SIDs are locale-independent and work consistently regardless of OS language. Common well-known SIDs: S-1-5-32-544 (Built-in Administrators), S-1-5-18 (System), S-1-5-19 (NT Authority/LocalService), S-1-5-11 (Authenticated Users), S-1-5-113 (Local Account), S-1-5-114 (Local Account + Admin group). Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/manage/understand-security-identifiers#sid-architecture
sourceRef: Supportability/Intune/Intune:/Engineer Reference/Tip of the Week/Configuring the correct Security Identifiers (SIDs)

symptom: EUDB AVD loading screen stuck on 'Welcome' with spinner after launch
rootCause: User profile issue on the EUDB multi-session AVD
solution: Go to https://mswvd.microsoft.com/sessions → wait for load → click 'delete user profile' → select EUDB → enter justification → submit. Warning: removes any unsaved data in the session.

symptom: Procmon cannot be launched directly on EUDB AVD multi-session environment
rootCause: Launching Procmon directly requires admin rights which are not available on the EUDB AVD multi-session environment
solution: Map the .pml file type to open with Procmon, then launch the .pml file directly. This bypasses the admin requirement and allows Procmon to be used for reviewing captures.

symptom: 'Principal aaduser is not authorized to read Qrybkradxxxxxx' error when querying Snapshot Kusto clusters from standard Desktop/Web Kusto or Intune Kusto Dashboard
rootCause: Due to SFI (Security Future Initiative), access to Qrybkradxxxxxx Snapshot Kusto clusters has been removed from standard Desktop and Web Kusto to align with security and compliance mandates
solution: Use Kusto Explorer inside Assist365 (accessible via Gladiator/Titan with MID account) to query Snapshot Kusto clusters. In Kusto Explorer: click 'Select Database' and select the Qrybkradxxxxxx cluster. Query must include `let currentTenantId = "Customer-IntuneAccountId";` variable. Intune and MMPC Kusto clusters remain accessible from standard tools.

symptom: MyWorkspace Intune/ConfigMan Lab Generator (VMBuild.cmd) shows 'Storage Access failed' error when launched
rootCause: Bad, expired, or changed Azure blob storage token in the lab generator configuration
solution: 1) Download latest storage config .zip from the attachment link on corp machine. 2) Copy .zip to the host VM. 3) Extract with password 'memlabs' to get _storageConfigYEAR.json. 4) Place the file in E:\memlabs\vmbuild\config on the host VM. 5) Close and re-run VMBuild.cmd. Old _storageConfig.json files do not need to be removed.

symptom: Error 'This template cannot be deployed as it exceeds quota constraints' when provisioning a MyWorkspace template
rootCause: Account segment is not set to 'Intune & ConfigMan' (may be from previous product area), or workspace/machine quota is at limit
solution: 1) Click settings gear > View my Quotas. 2) On User tab, check 'Segment Name' — if it doesn't contain 'Intune & ConfigMan', go through the MyWorkspace access/onboarding process at https://aka.ms/myworkspace. 3) Also check Machines tab for available quota and Hyper-V Host tab for nested workspace limits. Note: existing workspaces may be deleted when changing segments (uncommon but possible).

symptom: 'Activate' JIT button is disabled/greyed out in MyWorkspace website for a workspace
rootCause: MyWorkspace website sometimes fails to enable the JIT activation button; workaround available via ModernRDP app
solution: 1) Connect to MSFT-AzVPN-Manual (or usual work VPN). 2) Open the ModernRDP app. 3) Click on the name of the workspace needing JIT. 4) Click the 'JIT Needed' button. 5) Select the workspace in the new window. 6) Specify duration. 7) Click 'Activate'. Status in both ModernRDP and the MyWorkspace website will update as approval processes.

symptom: Guest VM inside MyWorkspace nested Hyper-V host fails to start with error 'The computed authentication tag did not match the Input authentication tag'
rootCause: TPM-related issue in nested Hyper-V VM environment (root cause unknown; reported by multiple engineers)
solution: Delete the Saved State for the affected VM from the Hyper-V console — this resolves the startup failure. Important: take a snapshot before doing this if the VM contains custom work you don't want to lose.

symptom: 配置 ETG enrollment profile 时更新 Device Security Group 报错 / SG 找不到
rootCause: Intune Provisioning Client 未设置为该 Security Group 的 Owner，导致 SG 验证失败（返回 SG not found）
solution: 在 Entra 中将 'Intune Provisioning Client' Service Principal 添加为该 Static Security Group 的 Owner。参考: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/autopilot/device-preparation/tutorial/user-driven/entra-join-device-group#adding-the-intune-provisioning-client-service-principal

symptom: 设备使用 ETG enrollment profile 注册后未被添加到 ETG Security Group 成员
rootCause: DeviceMembershipUpdater 操作失败；若 72 小时内未成功加入 SG，GnT 宽限期到期后会触发 policy tattoo removal
solution: 1. 检查 Devices > Monitor > Enrollment time grouping failures 报告（2510+ 版本可用）。2. 用 Kusto 查询 DeviceMembershipUpdater 日志确认失败原因。3. 宽限期内补救：Intune console 删除设备并重新注册；宽限期后：直接在 Entra 更新 SG 成员。重试超时为 1 小时。

symptom: Linux 设备初始注册时出现 Error 2400 / 'Something went wrong'
rootCause: JavaBroker（identity broker <2.0.2）在新安装或重启后需要 5-10 分钟才能启动
solution: 等待 5-10 分钟后重试注册。如果问题持续，确认 microsoft-identity-broker 服务正在运行: systemctl --user status microsoft-identity-broker

symptom: Linux 设备注册时出现 Error 1001 / 'An unexpected error has occurred'；日志显示 ErrorCode:PasswordResetRegistrationRequiredInterrupt ErrorNo:50125
rootCause: 租户启用了 SSPR（Self-Service Password Reset），用户首次登录时被重定向到 mysignins 页面注册必需的 MFA 方法，Company Portal 无法完成 token 获取
solution: 让用户先打开浏览器登录 Azure Portal（portal.azure.com）→ 自动跳转到 mysignins → 注册所需的身份验证方法 → 返回 Linux 设备重新注册 Intune-Portal

symptom: Linux 设备访问 M365/Azure 资源时显示 Access Denied / Error 530003
rootCause: 用户未使用 Microsoft Edge 访问受 CA 策略保护的资源；或 Edge Dev 版本低于 100.x；或设备未注册/不合规
solution: 1. 确认用户使用 Microsoft Edge（非 Firefox/Chrome/Safari）。2. 更新 Edge 到最新版本（>=102.x）。3. 确认设备已在 Intune 注册且合规。4. 确认用户已登录 Edge Profile（使用企业账号）。

symptom: Linux 设备显示合规但仍无法访问公司资源（CA 检查通过但资源拒绝）
rootCause: /etc/pam.d/common-password 文件中 pam_pwquality 配置被重置，导致密码策略不满足要求，设备合规状态延迟或错误
solution: 1. 访问 https://aka.ms/CPWeb 确认设备合规（等待 10-20 分钟）。2. 检查 /etc/pam.d/common-password 是否包含 'password requisite pam_pwquality.so retry=3 dcredit=-1 ocredit=-1 ucredit=-1 lcredit=-1 minlen=12'，若缺失则补充。3. 重新登录 Intune Portal 触发策略重推，等待 10-20 分钟。

symptom: End user cannot see the Remove or Reset button on Company Portal website (portal.manage.microsoft.com)
rootCause: Admin has hidden the Remove and/or Reset self-service actions for corporate devices in Intune Admin Center under Tenant Administration > Customization settings
solution: Check Intune Admin Center > Tenant Administration > Customization. Verify if Remove/Reset actions have been disabled for corporate devices. Re-enable the actions if the customer wants end users to have self-service capabilities.

symptom: Passcode reset option not available or not working on personal Android device with work profile in Company Portal website
rootCause: Passcode reset is not supported on personal Android devices with a work profile running Android 8.0 or later. This is a known platform limitation.
solution: Inform user that passcode reset is not available for personal Android devices with work profile on Android 8.0+. User must reset passcode through device settings directly or use the device manufacturer's account recovery process.

symptom: App configuration policy created in Intune but settings are not being applied to the target app on managed device or managed app
rootCause: The target app does not support app configuration policies. Not all apps implement the required configuration interface. The app developer must explicitly expose configuration settings for Intune to deliver them.
solution: 1. Check with the app developer/vendor to confirm app configuration policy support. 2. Review the app's documentation for supported configuration keys. 3. For Managed Google Play apps, verify configuration settings are exposed by the developer via AppConfig Community. 4. For iOS, verify the app supports managed app configuration. 5. Ensure correct enrollment type (Managed devices vs Managed apps) matches the deployment scenario.

symptom: Customer using Intune App SDK Xamarin Bindings for MAM integration in their Xamarin.iOS or Xamarin.Android app
rootCause: Xamarin support ended on May 1, 2024. The Intune App SDK Xamarin Bindings are no longer maintained or updated.
solution: Advise customer to migrate from Xamarin to .NET MAUI or .NET SDK-style projects. Use the Intune App SDK for .NET MAUI going forward. Xamarin Bindings will not receive security or feature updates.

symptom: Customer expects MAM app protection policies to be applied to macOS LOB app after using IntuneAppUtil wrapping tool (.pkg to .intunemac conversion)
rootCause: The macOS App Wrapping Tool (IntuneAppUtil) does NOT inject MAM app protection policies. Unlike the iOS/Android wrapping tools, it only converts .pkg files to .intunemac format for Intune deployment and detection.
solution: Clarify to the customer that macOS LOB apps are managed via MDM device policies, not app protection policies. The macOS wrapping tool only packages the app into a format Intune can deploy and detect. If the customer needs app-level data protection on macOS, they should explore MDM-based device configuration policies or consider alternative approaches.

symptom: Endpoint security feature (Application Guard, Firewall, SmartScreen, Encryption, Exploit Guard, Application Control, Credential Guard, Security Center) not working as expected even though Intune policy and registry settings are correctly applied to the device
rootCause: The security feature functionality is outside Intune scope. Intune only handles policy/registry delivery to the device. Feature behavior is owned by the respective Windows or Azure security team.
solution: Verify Intune policy applied correctly via registry. If settings are correct but feature not working, transfer case to: Application Guard→Windows UEX, Firewall→Windows networking, SmartScreen→Azure Security, Encryption→MSaaS Windows Devices, Exploit Guard→Azure Security, Application Control→MSaaS Windows Devices, Credential Guard→MSaaS Windows Devices, Security Center→Azure Security, Local device security options→Intune, User Rights→MSaaS Windows Devices.

symptom: Kusto queries on intune.kusto.windows.net return incomplete results or fail to find expected Intune telemetry data for specific scale units
rootCause: The original Intune Kusto cluster (intune.kusto.windows.net) reached saturation due to log volume over time and was split across multiple regional clusters. Logs are now distributed across IntuneNA1, IntuneNA2, IntuneEU1, IntuneEU2, IntuneAP1, IntuneIN1, and dedicated PE clusters.
solution: Use the appropriate regional Kusto cluster based on scale unit location: IntuneNA1.kusto.windows.net (West US), IntuneNA2.kusto.windows.net (East US), IntuneEU1.kusto.windows.net (North Europe), IntuneEU2.kusto.windows.net (West Europe), IntuneAP1.kusto.windows.net (Southeast Asia), IntuneIN1.centralindia.kusto.windows.net (Central India). Cross-cluster functions on any cluster invoke queries across all clusters automatically. For fallback, use cluster('clustername').database('Intune').table('tablename') syntax to query a specific cluster directly. Check KustoClusterToScaleUnitMapping table for scale unit to cluster mapping.

symptom: After configuring an app protection policy (MAM policy) for the Outlook mobile app so that PDF attachments can only be opened with Acrobat Reader, when a user downloads Acrobat Reader and tries to open a .pdf attachment, the error 'Action Not Allowed - This data is protected by your organization' is displayed.
rootCause: This can occur if the user has not authenticated to Acrobat Reader for Intune, or has used thumbprint to authenticate to their organization. The first authentication must be done using logon credentials (e.g. UPN@domain.com).
solution: Login to Intune within the Acrobat Reader App, then when prompted for authentication, use organization credentials (UPN@domain.com).

symptom: Attempting to enroll Windows devices into Intune MDM fails if the user is not in the local Administrators group.
rootCause: This is by design. For regular MDM enrollment, the account used to enroll the device must be a member of the local Administrators group on the Windows PC.
solution: As a workaround, if the customer uses AutoPilot they can enroll the device in MDM during OOBE (System Context, no admin required). Another option is for an admin to enroll in AADJ, then a standard user could log in and be managed.

symptom: Windows 10 MDM auto-enrollment into Intune via GPO fails. Task Scheduler generates error on EnterpriseMgmt task: Event 76 Auto MDM Enroll: Failed (Unknown Win32 Error code: 0x8018002b). DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider shows related errors.
rootCause: This can occur if MDM user scope is set to None in Azure AD Mobility settings.
solution: Navigate to Azure portal > Azure Active Directory > Mobility (MDM and MAM) > Microsoft Intune. Set MDM user scope to All (or appropriate groups) and set MAM User scope to None.

symptom: After deploying a WebClip application to an iOS or Android device as Required, if you delete the WebClip app from the portal or configure the deployment to uninstall, the WebClip is not removed. End user has no option to delete the application.
rootCause: This can occur if the web clip has been deployed as a Required app and the user has been migrated to standalone Intune from SCCM. This is by design because there is no way to uninstall a WebClip in this scenario as there is no reference/identifier for it.
solution: Remove the deployment prior to migrating the user, then redeploy after migration. Or change to Available and instruct users to install again over the existing WebClip (requires a sync).
