symptom: When trying to configure the Microsoft Intune On-Premise Exchange connector, error: 'Configuration Failed - The Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector version is not supported. Mobile device management will be disabled until you install the latest version.'
rootCause: This issue can be caused by having an older version of the Intune on-premise Exchange connector installed.
solution: Download and install the latest version of the Intune on-premise Exchange connector from the Exchange Access blade.

symptom: Alert: 'The Service to Service Connector Failed to Retrieve ACS Token' when using Intune O365 Exchange Connector.
rootCause: An invalid/different certificate is used, tenant is not registered in ACS, or admin is not Intune/O365 licensed. Can also occur during Intune service maintenance.
solution: Wait 24 hours (may be maintenance). If not resolved: ensure Global Admin who set up the Service Connector has both Intune and O365 Exchange licenses. Verify certificate and ACS registration.

symptom: After assigning a SCEP profile to a Windows 10 computer, if the device is unenrolled then the certificate does not get removed and it is not revoked on the CA.
rootCause: This is by design as of Windows 1809. The certificate is not removed when the PC is unenrolled.
solution: This behavior is expected in 1809 but was scheduled to be addressed in a future release where the certificate will be removed upon unenrollment.

symptom: When using the Intune PC agent on a Windows PC, the firewall policies may be listed in the Intune Classic portal as noncompliant.
rootCause: This issue occurs when the Intune Windows PC agent checks in while it's not on the domain that the PC is joined to. The firewall checks have to access a domain resource to accurately report compliance.
solution: Connect the Windows PC to the domain that it's joined to, and then check in using the Intune PC agent.

symptom: There are advantages and disadvantages based on how a device is enrolled and seen in Intune. Whether a device is a desktop, laptop or tablet should not be the primary factor when enrolling the device.
rootCause: There are a couple methods of enrolling a device in Intune. Computers running Windows 8.1 and Windows 10 can be managed as mobile devices via MDM enrollment or PCs if the Intune client software is used. Customers should be aware of the differences and consider the advantages and disadvantages between the device being registered as a PC or a mobile device before enrolling the device.
solution: See the capability comparison chart: Windows PC management (Desktop) capabilities vs Mobile Device Management (Mobile) capabilities. Key differences include: PC management offers software updates, firewall policy, anti-malware, hardware/software inventory, and desktop app deployment (exe/msi). MDM offers certificate/email/VPN/WiFi profiles, conditional access, device settings, selective wipe, custom OMA-URI settings, and Modern Apps deployment only.

symptom: Unable to Enroll DEP Devices When Prompting for User Affinity. After entering username and password, the spinner at the top spins briefly then stops, remaining at the Username and Password prompt.
rootCause: Two known causes: (1) MFA is enabled — MFA is not supported for DEP during enrollment as there is no way to send an MFA prompt to the device during the setup assistant. (2) Customer is federated using PingFederate — DEP with user affinity attempts authentication on the user's behalf from the Intune Service, which fails with PingFederate.
solution: 1. Disable MFA until after enrollment is complete. 2. If using PingFederate, upgrade to PingFederate 8.4 and set Default Token Type to "SAML 1.1 for Office 365" in the WS-Trust portion of the Office 365 connection. Contact Ping Federate support if assistance is needed.

symptom: Unable to Configure Android for Work due to continuous looping back to Configure Page.
rootCause: Issue with IE Security Zone Settings. The manage.microsoft.com page and play.google.com page are in different IE security zones with different Protected Mode settings, causing the configuration page to loop.
solution: Workaround 1: If Protected Mode mismatch, go to Internet Options > Security, enable Protected Mode for the mismatched zone and restart IE. Workaround 2: Add https://*.google.com to the same security zone as manage.microsoft.com, restart IE and retry.

symptom: Domain joined machines with Microsoft Intune full client report error 0x80041013 in the Intune Admin console for firewall policies.
rootCause: Domain joined machines make an RPC call to a domain controller for authentication. If the machine cannot reach a domain controller, reporting fails but firewall policies are effectively configured. This is a cosmetic reporting error.
solution: This is a cosmetic error and PG stated it will not be fixed. The error only shows in the admin portal and can be safely ignored. Firewall policies are applied correctly.

symptom: MAM Without Enrollment (MAMWE) policy not preventing data from being moved/saved outside of managed apps on iOS.
rootCause: On iOS 9.x/10.x this is by design. On iOS 11+ data transfer is allowed but files are encrypted. Open-In is controlled by the OS and Intune cannot modify iOS Open-In behavior directly. It is an MDM setting that controls which MDM-managed apps can share data.
solution: Use app protection policies with iOS Open-in management: set "Allow app to transfer data to only managed apps". On iOS 11+ files are encrypted when opened in non-managed apps. For MDM-enrolled devices, use "Allow app to transfer data to other apps: Policy managed apps" to restrict transfers to managed apps only.

symptom: Licensed Intune user in AD security group does not sync with Intune groups. Users not populating in Intune Group based on Criteria Membership including said Azure AD Security Group.
rootCause: Parent group starts with a sub group instead of All Users.
solution: Modify the group so that the parent group is All Users instead of a sub group.

symptom: Customer has an exemption on how many devices per user that can be enrolled into Intune. Customer can enroll 7 devices but when trying to enroll an 8th device the enrollment does not finish. The enrollment just times out but no message about device cap reached is displayed.
rootCause: The setting for "Maximum number of devices per user" in Azure IS NOT set to "Unlimited". The following error is seen in the company portal logs: 2017-03-02T14:11:31.4520000 ERR_ Event com.microsoft.windowsintune.companyportal.workplace.WorkplaceJoinManager 6096 1 Workplace join failed. Workplace library error: Failure Type: DRS Error Message: {"ErrorType":"AuthorizationError","Message":"DeviceCapReached","TraceId":"eb309cda-5eb0-4555-935c-45fbd1b19002","Time":"03-02-2017 14:11:30Z"} Details:null Exception: com.microsoft.workaccount.workplacejoin.WorkplaceJoinException:
solution: Log into Azure console, under Users and groups -> Device Settings; change the value for "Maximum number of devices per user" to Unlimited.

When trying to configure the Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector you receive the following error message. The Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector experienced an error Exception of type 'Microsoft.SystemCenter.online.mobileservices.Exchange.ServiceContracts.ExchangeIncomingGatewayException was thrown. Try to connect again later or go to the event viewer on this computer for more details Error code: 0x000000b
rootCause: This error can be caused by two things :Customer has configured Intune Exchange Service to Service connector and now second Intune Exchange on-premise connector. The Intune on-premise Exchange Connector is not able to connect to the Intune web service url
solution: To resolve the issue you can do the following In Intune there can only be one Exchange connector. You can run either the Intune on premise Exchange connector or the Intune Exchange service to Service connector.. You cannot run both connectors at the same time. To resolve the issue remove one of the Intune Exchange connector and install the connector will be able to manage the user mailboxes that have Intune licenses. This error message can also be caused by not being able to connect to any of the Intune URLs. To troubleshoot the issue first make sure the customer can connect to the URLs mentioned in the following link below. Intune Network Bandwidth Usagehttps://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/get-started/network-bandwidth-use

An architecture diagram.Where to see it in the UI.Where to find status messages.The log files that are available and where they are located.Solutions to some of the most commonly seen customer issues.

All devices fail to receive a SCEP certificate profile. You will also find the following authentication error messages within the Intune Ibiza portal:Experiencing authentication issuesThe portal is having issues getting authentication tokens for Microsoft_Intune_DeviceSettingsNote that the root certificate authority profile can be deployed fine from the Intune admin console.If you use the NDESLongURLValidator tool to validate external connectivity to the NDES Server you will see the following error:An error occurred. See log for details.If you then look at the log file (NDESLongURLValidator.log) you will find the following error:ndeslongurlvalidator Error: 0: An error occurred: System.Net.WebException: The remote server returned an error: (414) Request-URI Too Long.
rootCause: These errors indicate that something is blocking the long URL (Challenge sting) that the device received from the Intune policy for SCEP. This usually occurs due to one of the following:The NDES server is NOT configured properly to accept long URLs per setup requirements.orThere is a firewall or proxy server that is blocking the long URL request before it gets to the NDES server.
solution: First, verify that the NDES server is properly configured to accept long URLs per the setup requirements. See "PART 2 � SCEP certificate enrolling using ConfigMgr 2012, CRP, NDES and Windows Intune" at https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/tune_in_to_windows_intune/2014/04/25/part-2-scep-certificate-enrolling-using-configmgr-2012-crp-ndes-and-windows-intune/. In the section titled NDES Server, it says the following:=====The NDES server will receive very long URL�s (queries) and therefore a few changes are needed. Open the registry editor and add two entries:Location: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HTTP\Parameters Value: MaxFieldLength Type DWORD Data: 65534 (decimal)Location: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HTTP\Parameters Value: MaxRequestBytes Type DWORD Data: 65534 (decimal) Also, open IIS manager, browse to the Default Web Site and select Request Filtering. On the right side of the console hit Edit Feature Setting and change the Maximum URL length and Maximum query string to 65534.=====Verify that these settings have been configured. Note that changes to these settings require that the server be rebooted. Simply restarting IIS is not sufficient. If this does not resolve the issue, have the customer validate that there is not a firewall of proxy that is blocking the long URLs. Have the customer verify that the firewall/proxy is configured to allow these requests, or see if the customer can test using a device on the same network segment as the NDES server. If it works when the device is on the same segment then you know it's a firewall/proxy issue.

symptom: When attempting to assign a license (Enterprise Mobility + Security / Intune / Office 365) to a user via the Azure portal, license assignment fails with the following error:Licenses not assignedLicense cannot be assigned to a user without a usage location specified
rootCause: This can occur if the Usage location setting within the users profile is not configured.
solution: To resolve this problem, set the Usage location property in the users profile: 1. Go to https://portal.azure.com2. Navigate to Azure Active Directory -> Users and Groups -> All Users -> <user having problem> -> Profile -> Usage location.3. Select the proper location for the user and save the changes.Once this is done you can then successfully assign a license.

Customers may want to create/deploy a custom .bat or .cmd file to machines enrolled in their Microsoft Intune environments.
rootCause: To date, Microsoft Intune does not support the deployment of .bat or .cmd files via the normal Microsoft Intune Software Publisher Wizard.
solution: To resolve this issue the customer can compile/package their .bat or .cmd file using IExpress (a utility that is bundled with Microsoft Windows) for use with deploying their custom .bat or .cmd files. To do this follow the below steps:Click on Start/Run and type IExpress to launch the IExpress 2.0 tool.NOTE: May need to launch as AdminAt the Welcome Screen Select "Create new Self Extraction Directive File" At the Package Purpose Screen Select "Extract files and run an installation command" At the Package title Screen Give the Package any name. At the Confirmation prompt Screen Select "No Prompt" At the License agreement Screen Select "Do not display a license" At the Packaged files Screen Add the .bat or .cmd file that is wanting to be deployed. At the Install Program to Launch Screen Enter "cmd.exe /c <name of .bat or .cmd file>" and for the Post Install Command Select "<None>" At the Show window Screen Select "Hidden" as Intune packages run silently and cannot prompt users during runtime. At the Finished message Screen Select "No Message" At the Package Name and Options Screen Give the Executable a Path and Name to save to. For Options Select both "Hide File Extracting Progress Animation for User" and "Store files using Long File Name inside Package" NOTE: You may be prompted when selecting the Long File Names Option with the following prompt, Select "Yes" at the message. At the Configure restart Screen Select "No Restart" At the Save Self Extraction Screen Select "Don't save" At the Create package Screen Select "Next" to create the package Once again at the Create package Screen Select "Finish" to complete the process. Once this process is complete proceed with deploying the newly created compile executable using the Microsoft Intune Software Publishing Wizard.

symptom: When trying to enroll and configure Windows phones into IBM MaaS360 Mobile Device Management, users receive the following error: Your <companyName> company account is expired, so you can't open this company app. Go to Settings > Company app to update it.
rootCause: This can occur if the Company Hub Certificate has expired.
solution: To resolve this problem, renew the Company Hub Certificate by following the steps in this article: https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/jdupuy/2013/03/10/windows-phone-8-company-hub-development/

Paid customers are not able to access Download Center or view product key
rootCause: The Download Center is maintained by a separate team than the Windows Intune PG.
solution: Determine if affecting one or multiple users. Reference article 2725048. If multiple customers, engage Tier 3 as outage. Escalate to MSVLOPS and CC CSS Intune Escalations.

symptom: Unable to sign-in to Office applications on Apple devices
rootCause: Office 365 OneDrive and OneNote apps for iOS and Android updated to use new authentication system eSTS.
solution: Use customer facing KB 3015526. If unresolved, consult CSS Intune Escalations.

symptom: Customer unable to sign into Windows Intune Portals except IAP. Account ID all zeros. SubscriptionState=EnableReadyFailed
rootCause: Account not properly provisioned. Provisioning requests can fail causing account ID of all zeros.
solution: Look up via CSS Gateway Set-SCOSubscriptioncontext. If EnableReadyFailed, get Tier 3 approval then Set-SCOSubscription -AccountBlock false to retry provisioning.

symptom: Not receiving email notifications from the Intune service. Unable to receive alerts.
solution: Check admin notification config: recipient is Administrator, email correct, added to alert rules, alerts exist. If correct, escalate to check SMTP service.

symptom: Customer requests a CritSit or Severity 1 on a case.
rootCause: Premier customers have CritSit agreements with GBS that Intune Support honors.
solution: Meet SLA, determine scope and business impact, confirm 24x7 availability. Engage T3 On Call agent, escalate if no response.

symptom: Customer needs assistance with Windows Intune Beta TAP Account/Service
rootCause: Non-SEV A issues should be redirected to CONNECT Forums.
solution: Non-SEV A: redirect to CONNECT Forums. SEV A: authenticate in MOCMC, cut ticket, escalate to Tier 3. 24x7 support via 1-800-865-9408.

symptom: How to implement Azure AD Conditional Access for Exchange Online access for enrolled mobile or Windows devices
When adding or updating APN cert getting error Please specify an Apple ID in the format alias@domainname.com
rootCause: Invalid character in the Apple ID used during cert upload
solution: Ensure valid email format. Email is validated against regex. Use any valid format email since the ID is only used as a reminder.

symptom: When updating DEP Token in Intune standalone or hybrid SCCM getting error The specified operation could not be completed
rootCause: Corrupt DEP Token
solution: Download new Encryption Key .pem from Intune Portal. Upload to deploy.apple.com, download new .p7m token. Upload new token in Intune Portal.

symptom: After updating the DEP Token, Intune Hybrid with SCCM is showing that the Last Sync is current but the Apple DEP Portal shows the Last Connection is Never and no devices are being synced.
rootCause: DEP Token was not synced to Intune Side after renewal from Apple site.
solution: Force a FULL sync from the ConfigMgr side by running SQL query on Primary/CAS DB: Update DRS_MessageActivity SET LastVersionSent=0 where ReplicationID in (select ID from ReplicationData where ReplicationGroup='CloudDmp'). Wait 15 minutes then validate with Kusto query.

symptom: Advisory walkthrough for configuring Windows Store for Business with SCCM and Intune Hybrid. Covers Azure AD registration, ConfigMgr configuration, Windows Store for Business setup, and app deployment.
When attempting to publish an app in Intune via the Software Publisher console, the upload fails with error: Upload of data to Microsoft Intune failed. Fiddler trace shows 401 Unauthorized.
rootCause: The user account used when uploading the app package does not have a valid Intune license.
solution: Assign a license to the user account (UPN) being used when uploading the application package. Once uploaded, the Intune license can be removed if desired.

symptom: When trying to edit, save, or delete existing MDM security policies in O365, you receive errors: Your request could not be completed, Policy cannot be deployed, or The compliance policy has been deleted and cannot be modified.
rootCause: The Office 365 portal is trying to modify a policy, and either the logged-on user does not have appropriate permissions or the policy no longer exists on the Intune backend.
solution: Workaround 1: Ensure logged-on account is a local Global Administrator with Compliance Administrator permissions. Workaround 2: Create a new MDM policy to trigger sync, or run O365MDMCleanAllPolicy.ps1 to remove all existing policies.

symptom: Advisory: How to collect console logs from iOS/Apple devices using Console app (macOS Sierra 10.12+), Apple Configurator 2, or Xcode. Covers step-by-step instructions for each method.
Under Administration Overview you see a message: You have exceeded the number of allowed devices for your subscription. Error 0x80043006 enrolling PCs using Intune PC agent software.
rootCause: Customers are allowed 15 devices per user by default. PC agent enrolled devices are calculated differently: 5 physical devices and 1 VM per seat.
solution: This message is expected. No major impact to the service if physical seats have not been exceeded.

symptom: On Android, the Intune MAM Protected version ofManaged Azure Information Protection can Save as PDF when Save As is prevented by Policy.
rootCause: By Design. The "Save as PDF" is discoverable under the Print option of the Azure Information Protection App.  "Save As..." functionality is actually working if it is enabled in the Intune App Protection policy.
solution: The "Disable Printing" parameter must be set to YES in the Intune App Protection policy to prevent the Print>Save as PDF functionality from working in a managed app.

symptom: After configuring OOBE enrollment for a Surface Hub device, enrollment appears to succeed although the device does not appear in the Intune console. However, it does appear in the Azure portal as managed by Intune. On the device, under Work Settings it shows as enrolled with an MDM and that it is Azure AD joined.Running Device Diagnostic in Viewpoint for the affected user will show that the EasID is missing or blank.Note that manual enrollment to MDM on the same device works fine: The device is displayed, enrolled, and receives policies without issue.
rootCause: Surface Hubs cannot be managed via Auto Enrollment/Azure AD joins. The process must happen in two steps:1. The Out of Box Experience, where the Azure AD join happens2. Manual enrollment of the device to be managed via MDM.This is by design.
solution: To resolve this problem, be sure the Surface Hub users are not being targeted for being managed by Intune under Windows Azure -> Microsoft Intune -> Configure properties. If these users are being targeted, the Surface Hub devices will automatically try to autoenroll into the MDM when joining Azure Active Directory. This is not supported per https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/itpro/surface-hub/manage-settings-with-mdm-for-surface-hub.If you want all users to be managed by Intune (except for the Surface Hubs) utilized either a static or dynamic user group that excludes the Surface Hub users.

symptom: Computers running the full Microsoft Intune client may experience one or more of the following symptoms:PCs suddenly stop reporting to the Microsoft admin console. PCs experience high CPU utilization.Application installations stall when installed through the Intune portal.The Microsoft Intune Center triggers the following error:An error occurred while updating your computer. Error found: Code 0x800705b4.The status field under Intune Admin Console > Groups > All Devices displays the following: One or more agents that are installed on this computer have errors. The information for this computer may not be accurate or up to date.Error 0x80cf200b when checking for updatesThese issues primarily affect clients that are running Windows 7 and 8.x, because of the large number of superseded updates that are available for Windows 7 and 8.x. Windows 10 and later operating systems do not have as many available superseded updates and therefore are not as susceptible to this issue.
rootCause: This problem may occur if superseded updates have not been declined for an extended period. During certain processes, such as installing an application, Windows checks all superseded updates in sequence so that the updates and their successors can be correctly mapped. If the list of superseded updates grows too large, this checking task may cause high CPU utilization because of the processing load and time required and will prevent the machines fro reporting to the Intune Admin Console.
solution: To resolve this issue, follow these steps:Log on to the admin console at https://admin.manage.microsoft.com.Select Updates, and then click All Updates.Use the filter option on the upper toolbar to filter for superseded updates.Decline all superseded updates that may apply to Windows 7 or to applications (for example, Microsoft Office) that were installed on the affected clients. Restart the affected clients.Additionally, if you're running Windows 7, make sure that you have the following update installed:3050265 Windows Update Client for Windows 7: June 2015

You may have a customer ask if Bitlocker information can be found for an Intune user or device. If so then the details are below.
The Intune Admin console migration is starting with the Intune 1612 (December 2016) Deployment. This Article will be updated as the migration moves forward.
Company Portal in infinite loop. Device looping back to login page. Company Portal logs show The signed in user is not assigned to a role for the application
rootCause: Check to see if Company Portal log shows this message The signed in user is not assigned to a role for the application. If yes, then the option is set in Azure for the Microsoft Intune application user assignment required is set to yes. This option is only used for 3rd party applications and so will cause login to Intune Company Portal to fail
solution: Navigate to Azure portal and set user assignment required to NO. If the option is not available then escalate issue per process.

symptom: Users may get This page cant be displayed error on Windows 8.1 and Windows 10 when trying to get to HTTPs websites when the Intune full client is installed on the device
rootCause: Azure AD Cloud App Discovery agent is installed with the default option of Deep Inspection enabled. This is unrelated to the Intune Full client being installed on the device.
solution: Ensure that the Deep Inspection feature of Azure AD Cloud App Discovery is changed to off before installing the Azure AD Cloud App Discovery agent.

symptom: updates.log shows Agent sync failed, error = 0x80CF000E. WARNING: Updates client failed Searching for update with error 0x80cf000e
rootCause: MDM authority is not set to Intune/SCCM. Agents may not be allowed to sync if the authority is not set to Intune or SCCM
solution: If authority is set to Office and customer has an Intune subscription then see this article to enable coexistence

symptom: Android For Work Profiles are not synchronizing. SCCM Console shows Sync Status Failed
rootCause: No Applications are approved in Play for Work. Connectivity issues shown in the dmpdownloader.log
solution: Make sure at least one app is approved in Play for Work. Make sure the sync was initiated from the SCCM console, not from the Intune console. Make sure SCCM connectivity with Intune is active.

symptom: Customer states that Intune Enrollment is not workingCustomer states when they attempt to enroll via Company Portal they get error:"Could not sign in. You will need to sign in again. If you see this message again, please contact your IT admin�
rootCause: Tenant Endpoint defectDiagnostic Description: The test determines if the account has published endpoints.Action Needed: If false, the service endpoints for the account has not yet been published. If the account should exist and the endpoints have not been published for a long period of time, please escalate.Error Message: Account has no published endpoints
solution: Resolved the defect on the back end of the tenant via Ops procedureCustomer verified they were able to enroll again.

symptom: When you attempt to save the configuration profile with the .csv files for ios applications you may receive a generic failed error on portal.azure.com
rootCause: The format has changed in the new portal
solution: The format has changed in the new portal and must be in this format:<app url>, <app name>, <app publisher> "AppStoreUrl must be a valid URL or be empty."

symptom: Customer would like to know if Intune has an RSS FeedCustomer would like to know if yes, how do you enable and configure it

When a customer is seeing Intune SilverLight Dashboard errors for devices showing as "currently busy" or "Account is not unique".When a customer is seeing Intune SilverLight showing in the Mobile Device Inventory Report entries for "Enabled without bypass code (device is not supervised)"This is and advisory to help the customer understand them.
rootCause: These can be caused for the following reasons:a. The device already has the Email profile manually on the device for the same serverb. The device may be updating the iOS or Email Applicationc. Activation Lock is enabled automatically when a user opens the Find My iPhone app on a device.
solution: [When you see the following errors in the Intune SilverLight Dashboard for devices]An error occurred:0x87D11388 : iOS device is currently busySource Policy: Email Profile - iOSor An error occurred:0x87D1791D : Account is not uniqueThese can be found in the article:https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/troubleshoot/troubleshoot-company-resource-access-problems-with-microsoft-intune These can be caused for the following reasons:a. The device already has the Email profile manually on the device for the same serverb. The device may be updating the iOS or Email ApplicationAction Plan to resolve these two errors========================================Contact the end usersHave them remove all Email ProfilesAllow the Intune Email profile to be pushed downRe-add any personal Email profile[If you see in the "Mobile Device Inventory Report" that Activation Lock State set to Enabled without bypass code (device is not supervised)]Activation Lock State: Enabled without bypass code (device is not supervised)https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/deploy-use/help-protect-ios-devices-with-activation-lock-bypass-for-microsoft-intune Activation Lock is enabled automatically when a user opens the Find My iPhone app on a device. After it is enabled, the user's Apple ID and password must be entered before anyone can: Turn off Find My iPhoneErase the deviceReactivate the device While Activation Lock helps secure iOS devices and improves the chances of recovering a lost or stolen device, this capability can present you, as an IT admin, with a number of challenges. For example: A user sets up Activation Lock on a device. The user then leaves the company and returns the device. Without the user's Apple ID and password, there is no way to reactivate the device.You need a report of all devices that have Activation Lock enabled.You want to reassign some devices to a different department during a device refresh in your organization. You can only reassign devices that do not have Activation Lock enabled. To help solve these problems, Apple introduced Activation Lock bypass in iOS 7.1. This lets you remove the Activation Lock from supervised devices without the user's Apple ID and password. Supervised devices can generate a device-specific Activation Lock bypass code, which is stored on Apple's activation server. Supervised mode for iOS devices lets you use Apple Configurator to lock down a device and limit functionality to specific business purposes. Supervised mode is generally only for corporate-owned devices. You can see which devices are using Activation Lock in two ways: Run the Mobile Device Inventory Reports. This report displays the Activation Lock Status and Supervised columns to indicate the state of devices. The values for Supervised are Yes or No, and the values for Activation Lock Status are: Enabled with bypass codeEnabled without bypass code (device is not supervised)Enabled without bypass code (device cannot be reached)Not enabled Action Plan for these entries========================================Contact the end usersHave them disable the Active Lock

symptom: Customer needs to know if there is a RSS Feed for Intune and how to configure it for monitoring health.
rootCause: Advisory
solution: There was only one place for Intune to configure an RSS feed, via the Office 365 Service Health Dashboard:https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/undocumentedfeatures/2015/10/12/subscribe-to-the-office-365-service-health-dashboard-rss-feed-in-outlook/ However this site has been depreciated and replaced with https://portal.office.com/adminportal/home#/servicestatusPlease keep in mind that this is for Outages and Alerts not shown in the SilverLight Console 1. Open a browser in private mode2. Navigate to https://portal.office.com/adminportal/home#/servicestatus3. Sign in with your tenant global admin account4. Navigate to Health->Service Health Here you can review if a Service is healthy, under Advisory or if there is an Incident.But you cannot configure for RSS Feeds any longer like you used to be able to. The only remaining RSS feed out there from us on the Intune MDM Application is a news feed that is here:https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/enterprisemobility/feed/?product=microsoft-intune%2f Which you subscribe to via the following steps: 1. Open a browser2. Navigate to https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/enterprisemobility/feed/?product=microsoft-intune%2f3. Click Subscribe If you have Outlook (Full not Mobile App) you can add the RSS Feed to your Outlook via the following steps 1. Open Outlook2 Navigate to RSS Feeds3. Right click RSS Feeds in Outlook->Select Add new RSS Feed4. Paste the URL: https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/enterprisemobility/feed/?product=microsoft-intune%2f5. Click Add button

solution: Disable and Re-enable Device WritebackReference Article: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/connect/active-directory-aadconnect-feature-device-writebackAction Steps:==========================================1. Run the Azure AD Connect Installation Wizard2. Navigate to the Customize Synchronization Options->Optional Features3. Uncheck Device writeback4. Complete the Wizard5. Run the Azure AD Connect Installation Wizard6. Navigate to the Customize Synchronization Options->Optional Features7. Check the Device writeback8. Click Next9. On the Writeback page, select the domain required10. Complete the Wizard11. Verify you can enroll device

symptom: An Apple iPhone is successfully managed by Intune, however when connected to a computer a message similar to the following is generated:"This iPhone is supervised by another computer and cannot be used with this computer."
rootCause: This occurs when using Apple�s Device Enrollment Program (DEP) and the policy Enable additional Apple Configurator management is set to Disallow. When set to Disallow, this will prevent a DEP device from syncing files.
solution: To fix this problem, complete these steps:1. Open the Intune portal at http://manage.microsoft.com/2. Open the Policy node and go to Corporate Device Enrollment.3. Under Device Enrollment Program Settings, find the setting titled Enable additional Apple Configurator management.4. This is probably set to Disallow. If so, change it to Allow and save the policy.5. Lastly, complete a factory reset on the device to set the policy. Apple dictates that a wipe is required so we need to make sure that is done.Once this is complete you should no longer get that message.

symptom: Global Administrator is attempting to deploy Software or Application and they receive the following error:
rootCause: This occurs because the Global Administrator does not have an Intune License
solution: Assign an Intune License to the Global Administrator attempting to deploy Software or Application

symptom: Customer wants advisory for the following two areas:1. Recommended practices when pushing applications or application updates2. Potential issues when pushing applications or application updates
rootCause: Advisory
solution: This advisory is written to address not only the two questions below, but application deployment and upgrade recommendations for Intune and Configuration Manager alike that can be used over time.When using this advisory, you can update the link section at the end to reflect the customers MDM Authority tool set to go with the advisory.1. Recommended practices when pushing applications or application updates2. Potential issues when pushing applications or application updatesWhen it comes to recommended practices for pushing applications or upgrading applications in your environment, there is no official documentation that will spell it all out 100% for you; its kind of an art.There are no Application Management Best Practices for Configuration Manager Current Branch articles out there as of yet, eventually an engineer will put one together and I may even be the one to go ahead and just do it, but currently it has not been published.So, what are the recommended practices when pushing an application or an application update to iOS devices, to be honest the same as any deployment of an application to a client or mobile device; they fall into the following areas:a. Planningb. Testingc. Pilotd. Verificatione. Deploymentf. Monitoringg. Follow uph. Recap[Planning]I suggest to start with planning because no matter if you are rolling out something new vs rolling out an update to something old, you need to know your end user device base and ask yourself the following questions:a. What devices are currently known to be having issues that could be included in my deployment (Examples: Users or devices with known problems)?b. What environment issues are currently know that could impact my deployment (Examples: Time zone differences, devices being on or off, other roll outs)?c. What environment restrictions could impact my deployment (Example: locations with slow internet and the application is large)?d. What application properties could impact my deployment (Examples: size, license, dependencies of the application, vendor documents)?e. What are the latest Configuration Manager changes for the version I am on, are there updates that could add features to aid in my end goal?When you take these things and you look at the data, you get a more rounded picture of the process, a roadmap to what lies ahead and a plan to avoid pitfalls and allow your numbers to move in a fluid and progressive arch for deployment success and reporting. You reduce the number of delays and follow ups that will be required during the deployment by identifying things right at the start before you even create a collection, application deployment to the collection pulling the trigger.By knowing and reviewing the product you use for the deployment, in this case Intune or ConfigMgr, you build an better understanding of your tools, including options and limitations. Building this mental muscle memory allows you to be familiar with your tools, understanding their capabilities and setting realistic expectations of your deployment and roll outs for your end users, managers and company alike.After you have done your planning, this leads to testing, you may say oh I have used this application before or deployed it, but software is ever changing and vendors change things in applications that are subtle at times during planning phase you should have reviewed the vendors documentation. I have found that more often than not problems can be defeated before they are allowed to start with simple testing, sometimes vendors have bad days as well and corners are cut or missed; don't let their mis-steps be your deployment's downfall.[Testing]Start out by manually running the install with no modifications just as it came from the vendor. Why do this you may be asking, while knowing silent switches and command lines are great truly being prepared for deployments is understanding what good looks like. Defining that further I mean, by manually installing an application you will see its UI and generally get a good feel of what the application does during its installation.There are even mobile apps out there that do not always play nice, and when dealing with computers and mobile devices getting to see the UI interaction first hand can be a important tool in assuring that the deployment goes smoothly.This plays simply a fact no matter if you are deploying customer applications, store applications, even deep linked applications because again software and vendors are always changing (not excluding the OS that you are targeting).Once you see how the default install acts, now you can start with modifying it, there are different degrees of modification that are supported by the vendor (when dealing with mobile apps, often there is very little to none truly).However when ever you modify an application always adhere to vendor support and documentation, this will keep you from getting into a pickle when calling support during a roll out because of issues that could arise only to find out you broke your support by using non-sanctioned modifications to the applications.No matter if you are deploying a mobile application, a full client application or a custom application; it is again important to check the vendor documentation or developed documentation. Starting to see a theme?, good as repro of a process is important when it comes to deployments, while no deployment may be the exact same if your process is, then your success can be too.When upgrading an existing application and using Configuration Manager, look at how you deployed your original application, it may have been a while since you touched it. Sometimes looking at the past can bring experience to the present, you may find that while its a deep link deployment of the original the new version due to license change for the application is now an offline deployment. This is something that should have been covered in planning but no matter it�s always good to check, check and double check.Know your tools, something that was already spoken off in planning as well, but cannot be stressed enough. Knowing the capabilities in important as it effects your ability to create, understand and successfully deploy scalable environments that will support your growing client base's needs.Practice, when in between production deployments, create a testing ground and play; when I say this I mean it... play.Create applications and test collections that you can deploy to test devices and practice building your deployment and support along with troubleshooting muscle memory, don't just test when there is a production need.When testing make sure you keep notes, follow up with issues found during the testing phase sooner rather than later as once you rule them out they are very less likely to raise their heads during production roll outs.If you have a toolset like Configuration Manager that allows you to perform Simulations of deployments, use them they are great way to get tactical data for possible issues that can arise from deployments.Always take the supported and documented routes of deployments, tools and processes when available, if you find yourself looking down a dark alley on an application deployment with a gut feeling of I am not sure if this will work; then set the expectation realistically and take the hard road and delay the deployment if possible to get this back on the right track. Being proactive in testing rather than re-active in deployments always can bring you success.[Pilot]Now there are a lot of people out there that state, I did all this testing why do another phase of it? The simple answer is testing often occurs in a clean room, meaning the devices are fresh builds and not the dirty, scabby devices of the real world where the matrix has been stripped away and the beauty is gone. These devices have flaws, bugs, gaps in performance and possibly even commitment issues for deployments (sorry thought it would bring a little humor to the write up). By taking a cross section of your environment, you can create a pool to pilot your deployment of a new or upgrade for an application in production without it being officially the production deployment; a pre-flight after testing phase.This lets you take what you learned in testing to the next level, bringing in the dirty and chaos to your clean room to put what you learned from testing into practice for the big deployment. To create a good healthy pilot I use the following equation:a. Number of devices for production deploymentb. Take 1/3 of this and device in halfc. This is your deployment pilot poolNow depending on how large a may be, this equation may have an adjustment to be made to it, example never use less than 10 devices if possible in your pilot pool; yet at the same token if your deployment pool is over 50 devices you may want to go ahead and hard line it at 50 as getting too large of a pool can also leave impacting your environment if something goes amiss that you did not catch in testing during the pilot.Always make sure your end users are aware they are in a pilot and that they are ok with it, people whom are not should be removed as your goal is to not impact the environment or also have flawed pilot results.Sometimes pending on the end users, example iOS devices where users have to approve installs it is actually good to keep a problem user or two for the social aspect in the pilot as they can represent what can happen when the device is willing for the deployment of an application or upgrade but the end user is not.Like in testing, assure you document everything learned in pilot and use this to your advantage for audit trail and preparation for a deployment to production.[Verification]Now that you have done planning, testing and a pilot, there is more allow some time to pass after the pilot to let the end users use and burn in the application you upgraded.Sometimes deployments are brought crashing down not by the action of deploying the application itself but the bugs in the application itself and a pilot is a great testing field prior to deployment to production to find them.Follow up with the end users, document their experience and look for the following:a. Has anyone had to uninstall and re-install the app since the pilot deployment?b. Has anyone had crashes, errors or issues with the app since the pilot deployment?c. Has anyone had network issues or hardware issues since the pilot deployment?Now I am sure there will be some people who always have an issue, but vet them out and it helps you identify false positives vs real issues that could be a serious impact if this product is rolled out.[Deployment]When it comes to deployment to production, assure to do it by the numbers at this phase you should be ready since you have now completed:a. Planningb. Testingc. Pilotd. VerificationAssure to adhere to company security and policies for your deployments, never worth the risk to step around those.Assure that you before pulling the trigger to deploy the application or upgrade to an application to take a last inventory of the following for a fresh perspective on your environment:a. What devices are currently known to be having issues that could be included in my deployment (Examples: Users or devices with known problems)?b. What environment issues are currently know that could impact my deployment (Examples: Time zone differences, devices being on or off, other roll outs)?c. What environment restrictions could impact my deployment (Example: locations with slow internet and the application is large)?d. What other application, software updates or virus protection changes could impact my deployment (Example: was there a change to iOS)?e. What are the latest Configuration Manager changes for the version I am on, are there updates that could add features to aid in my end goal?Assure that you communicate that the deployment is coming in a timely and repeated manner for your end users, especially if you have devices like Apple iOS that require end users to approve an install.Assure that they understand the purpose and importance of the application or upgrade deployment, social engineering can be one of your best tool in your kit.[Monitoring]Once you have pulled the trigger on deploying the application or upgrade deployment, monitor and set a cycle of monitoring and reporting.This will get you into the habit of watching for any issues pro-actively not re-actively and can assist you in cutting off problems or trends before they can impact your environment.Make notes as you monitor the deployment, this will aid you in future deployments and use the tools that are available to monitor, generate reports allowing you the most insight you can get to your deployment.[Follow Up]Just like after deployment, touch base with your end users and follow up.Assure that there is no issue out there lurking, this will also aid you in getting those whom are on operating systems like Apple to upgrade simply by being on the phone with them and giving them support where they may have been holding off on approving an installation due to uncertainty or liking it how it was. This lowers your numbers for success and by calling and following up, you raise them back up and build confidence in your end user customer base for future deployments.[Re-cap]At the end of a deployment, review all your notes from planning to recap, use these to aid in improving your process for the next go round.Also the notes taken during the process if you have to open a support case with a vendor allow you to have data prepped and ready for your support decreasing your time to solution.[Support Links for your advisory]Here are article links that are for your service request's specific advisory that will suppliment what I have written above.

symptom: Customer states they have an Intune license with MDOP associated with it but cannot get to the old download site or do not know the site.
rootCause: In 2015 there was a change to the process and the old site was retired, the MDOP license is no longer available to new licenses for Intune so you will want to use the following process to validate if they have the MDOP license truly and if they do how they can now download their ISOs and Product Keys.
solution:
[Verification]

1. Open the
Portal.Office.com Admin Portal

2. Select Billing \
Subscriptions

 

� Note if you do not
show a subscription labeled "Microsoft Intune With Microsoft Desktop
Operating System" you do not have Software Assurance aka MDOP.

� Should look like
this:



 

[Downloading]

Action Steps  - Downloading ISOs

Assure the tenant
you are attempting to download ISOs from has the "Microsoft Intune with
Microsoft Desktop Operating System" subscription. Also assure that when
attempting to download ISOs, you are using a global admin account from the
actual tenant.

 

1. Launch an
InPrivate Browsing session of Internet Explorer and navigate directly to https://www.microsoft.com/online/downloads/Catalog.aspx.


2. User may be
prompted with two login options. Choose the Microsoft Online Services ID User
and select continue. 

3. Enter a valid
Global Admin login to access the benefit (same login or account used before
when using the SA benefit).4. Search for ISO, select and download product keys are listed next to the ISOs  in search results.

solution: This issue is resolved in the next iOS SDK/Wrapper.

symptom: Blocking Applications (whitelist/blacklist) on iOS Devices with Microsoft Intune requires devices to be in Supervised Mode
rootCause: To block the installation of applications on iOS devices it is required for devices to be in Supervised Mode.  This requirement for Supervised Mode comes from Apple and is documented by them on the Configuration Profile Reference that is documented here: https://developer.apple.com/library/content/featuredarticles/iPhoneConfigurationProfileRef/Introduction/Introduction.htmlTo blacklist/whitelist applications we are calling the Restrictions Payload and specifically the following:blacklistedAppBundleIDs Array of strings Supervised only. If present, prevents bundle IDs listed in the array from being shown or launchable. Availability: Available in iOS 9.3 and later.whitelistedAppBundleIDs Array of strings Supervised only. If present, allows only bundle IDs listed in the array from being shown or launchable. Availability: Available in iOS 9.3 and later.As noted the use of the blacklistedAppBundleIDs or whitelistedAppBundleIDs specifically calls out "Supervised only"
solution: The resolution at this time requires that the device(s) be placed in Supervised Mode by being prepared by the following channels:Apple Configurator: https://support.apple.com/apple-configuratorApple Device Enrollment Program: https://www.apple.com/business/dep/

symptom: This document describes how to use Fiddler to capture a network trace, including instructions that support engineers can send to customers when capturing network trace data is needed to resolve a case.
solution: Fiddler is a free 3rd party tool that customers can download and use if they agree to do so. Fiddler is not owned or supported by Microsoft, however it is very robust and easy-to-use. Below are the instructions for downloading and configuring Fiddler to capture network traffic. These instructions can be sent to a customer when requesting network trace data.======================================================================Download and install the Fiddler tool from http://www.telerik.com/download/fiddler.Clear the web browser cookies and cache, then close all web browser windows.Open a new Internet Explorer web browser session using the Start InPrivate Browsing option.Logon to https://portal.azure.com.Open Fiddler. Tracing will automatically start when Fiddler is opened. Make sure that HTTPS is enabled under Tools -> Options -> HTTPS.Reproduce the problem in the web browser session opened in step 3 above.Once the problem is reproduced, press F12 to stop tracing.Save the trace file by clicking on File -> Save -> All Sessions. Give your file a name that will make it easy to identify.Send the saved .SAZ back to me.IMPORTANT: If you have the Decrypt HTTPS traffic option enabled, your password may be captured in the trace file. If you logged in after starting Fiddler tracing, remove the password related traffic from the trace before saving it to send to us. You can remove the trace entries that might include your password using the following steps:a) In the left-hand window, locate the line entry whose host is login.live.com/ppsecure/post.srf?....b) Right-click that line and select Remove -> Selected Sessions======================================================================NOTE: If you need to use Fiddler to capture traffic for the Windows PC based Company Portal application, you must use the AppContainer Loopback Exemption Utility in Fiddler to exempt all apps. In the case of the Intune Company Portal App as well as all Modern Apps, the login goes through Windows Authentication Broker so if the apps are not exempted then no traffic will be captured. To exempt all apps, complete the following inside the Fiddler application:1. Click the WinConfig button on the toolbar. This will start the AppContainer Loopback Exemption Utility.2. From the AppContainer Loopback Exemption Utility, click the Exempt All button, then click the Save Changes button.3. Once saved, restart Fiddler and complete the steps above (replacing IE11 with the app in question) to reproduce the problem and collect a network trace.

symptom: The information below can be used when troubleshooting enrollment failures with iOS corporate owned device that are part of Apple's DEP program.
solution:
DEP enrollment failures can be caused by many different reasons. Note that you will need access to Intune Kusto which you should have already assuming you are in the Intune Support All security group: Important information to collect:Intune Account IdDevice Serial NumberFirst determine how far along in the enrollment process the customer gets before encountering the problem. If they do not get to the DEP Login page, run the following Kusto query to determine if the device is set up for DEP correctly:DepFeatureTelemetry | where serialNumber contains"F4GT54YPHG71" or serialNumberscontains "F4GT54YPHG71"A Common exception is shown below which indicates that the profile assignment failed. This is typically caused because Apple has not created a record for the device yet. This can happen if you try to enroll a DEP device immediately after adding the information to Intune. Please wait a few minutes before attempting to enroll the device after adding it to Intune.Microsoft.Management.Services.Common.NotFoundException: HTTP
request failed: NotFound - Not Found, request URI: https://mdmenrollment.apple.com/profile/devices   at
Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessAppleSyncService.DataAccess.Clients.ProxyBase.ThrowOnError(IHttpResponse
httpResponse, Uri requestUri)   at
Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessAppleSyncService.DataAccess.Clients.RestProxyBase.ParseHttpResponse[T](Uri
resourceUri, IHttpResponse response)   at
Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessAppleSyncService.DataAccess.Clients.RestProxyBase.<PerformHttpOperationMethod>d__14`2.MoveNext()   at Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessAppleSyncService.DataAccess.Clients.RestProxyBase.<PutObjectAsync>d__12`2.MoveNext()   at
Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessAppleSyncService.DataAccess.Clients.DepClientProxy.<PutObjectAsync>d__6`2.MoveNext()   at Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessAppleSyncService.DataAccess.Clients.DepProfileClient.<AssignProfileAsync>d__3.MoveNext()   at
System.Runtime.CompilerServices.TaskAwaiter`1.GetResult()   at
Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessAppleSyncService.Behaviors.DepProfileBehavior.<AssignProfileAsync>d__5.MoveNext()   at
Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessAppleSyncService.Behaviors.DepProfileSyncBehavior.<SyncProfileAssignmentInternalAsync>d__13.MoveNext()   at
Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessAppleSyncService.Behaviors.DepProfileSyncBehavior.<SyncProfileAssignmentAsync>d__11.MoveNext()If they get to the DEP Login page but it either just spins or keeps asking for credentials, make sure they're using the format user@domain.com for the username. When the authentication
step of DEP enrollment is failing, you can run the following query to find these auth failures
during DEP Enrollment.  Make sure to
adjust the time appropriately in both places. This should give you the exception that is causing the problem.IntuneEvent

    | where env_time > ago(10d)

    | where Col1 contains "<AccountID>" 

    | where EventUniqueName == "PostReportDeviceInfo" 

    | project ActivityId, env_time 

    | join (IntuneEvent | where env_time > ago(10d) | where EventUniqueName
== "ADALAuthenticationException") on ActivityId 
One common exception stacks and what it means is below. This is typically seen if they entered an invalid username in the username
field instead of using user@domain.com.Microsoft.IdentityModel.Clients.ActiveDirectory.AdalException:
unknown_user_type: Unknown User Type   at
Microsoft.IdentityModel.Clients.ActiveDirectory.AcquireTokenNonInteractiveHandler.<PreTokenRequest>d__5.MoveNext()   at
Microsoft.IdentityModel.Clients.ActiveDirectory.AcquireTokenHandlerBase.<RunAsync>d__55.MoveNext()   at
Microsoft.IdentityModel.Clients.ActiveDirectory.AuthenticationContext.<AcquireTokenCommonAsync>d__52.MoveNext()   at
Microsoft.IdentityModel.Clients.ActiveDirectory.AuthenticationContextIntegratedAuthExtensions.<AcquireTokenAsync>d__0.MoveNext()   at
Microsoft.Management.Services.IosDiscoveryService.AdalAuthenticator.<AuthenticateAADUser>d__3.MoveNext()

ErrorCode:
unknown_user_typeIf this is not the issue, get a full trace of the DEP Session for the auth failure by running this query along with the ActivityId:IntuneEvent    | where ActivityId == "<ActivityId>"To get more detailed
information on any of the exceptions, you need to look at EvoSTS logging.  To get that, the ActivityId for the row with
the exception maps to the Correlation Id in the EvoSts logs. So copy that then
run the EvoSTS query below. This should return at
least 1 row, if not then move onto next query with CorrelationId, instead of
RequestId.unioncluster("estsam2").database("ESTS").PerRequestTableIfx, cluster("estsbl2").database("ESTS").PerRequestTableIfx, cluster("estsby1").database("ESTS").PerRequestTableIfx, cluster("estsch1").database("ESTS").PerRequestTableIfx, cluster("estsdb3").database("ESTS").PerRequestTableIfx, cluster("estshkn").database("ESTS").PerRequestTableIfx, cluster("estssin").database("ESTS").PerRequestTableIfx, cluster("estssn1").database("ESTS").PerRequestTableIfx
| where env_time > ago(1d)| where CorrelationId
==  "<ActivityIdFromAbove>"
| project env_time, RequestId,
CorrelationId, ErrorCode, Call, IsInteractive, ApplicationId, ResourceId, ClientIp, MfaStatus, DeviceId, EventData, MultiCAEvaluationLog, ConditionalAccessAppId1Decision, ConditionalAccessAppId2Decision  , UserPrincipalObjectID, Client, ClientInfo, UserAgentThe event message will
usually tell you what is wrong here. Interpreting it takes some skill so don't be afraid to ask for help. To get more details
on what is returned from the above query grab the RequestId and run this query:unioncluster("estsch1").database("ESTS").DiagnosticTracesIfx,cluster("estsby1").database("ESTS").DiagnosticTracesIfx,cluster("estsbl2").database("ESTS").DiagnosticTracesIfx,cluster("estssn1").database("ESTS").DiagnosticTracesIfx,cluster("estsam2").database("ESTS").DiagnosticTracesIfx,cluster("estsdb3").database("ESTS").DiagnosticTracesIfx,cluster("estshkn").database("ESTS").DiagnosticTracesIfx,cluster("estssin").database("ESTS").DiagnosticTracesIfx
| where env_time > ago(1d)| where RequestId == "<RequestId>"
| project env_time,
CorrelationId, Result,
Call, MessageThis will give you
the detailed traces. For Conditional
Access you can look for  the
Exception.  Then look at the rows just
previous to the exception and you should see something like this:Multi CA Policy evaluation log:

{"Id":"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000","Eval":"NotSet","Val":"DefaultDummy"}

{"Id":"38df2d10-2ba4-4306-aab7-45cf403d900f","Eval":"Applies","Val":"Valid","Data":{"App":"Satisfied","Plat":"Satisfied","User":"Satisfied","Loc":"Satisfied","ClientType":"Satisfied"},"Chlgs":["Block"],"AllChlgs":["Block"]}

{"Id":"e51b1a91-0d4a-47fa-95c8-cce0fe71eee6","Eval":"NotEnabled","Val":"Valid"}

{"Id":"166d0d1d-75b8-471c-93d1-636e617a40d9","Eval":"DoesNotApply","Val":"Valid","Data":{"App":"NotSatisfied"}}Look for the
"Eval":"Applies" 
this is the policy or policies blocking access.  If none are in the Apply state then
determining why it is blocked can be tricky, please ask for help.The following is
information on the Client that is authenticating to AAD. You might need to go to
Evo STS and here is some useful information for that:AdalClientId = 9ba1a5c7-f17a-4de9-a1f1-6178c8d51223 IntuneServicePrincipalId = 0000000a-0000-0000-c000-000000000000
IntuneEnrollmentServicePrincipalId = d4ebce55-015a-49b5-a083-c84d1797ae8c

symptom: Mobile devices do not receive configuration policies such as password requirements and/or other device restrictions policies when devices are managed through Configuration Manager
rootCause: Configuration baseline deployment is not configure to Remediate (Baseline Deployment shows Action = Monitor)
solution: 1) Launch the Configuration Manager console and navigate to: Assets and Compliance -> Compliance Settings -> Configuration Baseline2) Select the Configuration Baseline that contains the configuration items that you are trying to deploy 3) In the bottom pane select the "Deployments" tab then right click the deployment and select "Properties"4) Verify that the option "Remediate noncompliant rules when supported" is selected5) Force a new sync on the device and confirm that the device can now receive the respective configuration policies6) To confirm the status of the deployment in Configuration Manager console, simply right click on the Configuration Baseline deployment and select "View Status"

symptom: Users are unable to launch the "All apps" link in the Company Portal in order to self-service applications. The following error is shown:This link cannot be opened in Safari. Safari might be restricted in Settings or your IT admin might have blocked access
rootCause: A configuration policy is configured to either disable or hide Safari on iOS supervised devices. This is due to the January (2017) changes to the Company portal application for iOS where links inside of the Company Portal app for iOS, including those to documentation and apps, will open directly in the Company Portal app using in-app view of Safari. As documented here:https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/whats-new/whats-new-archiveIn previous versions of the company portal if Safari was blocked or hidden, it would allow the links to be opened in the Microsoft Intune Managed Browser when the application was deployed.
solution: Need to ensure that Safari is not being disabled or blocked in the General Configuration Policy (iOS 8.0 and later):1) Check the General Configuration Policy (iOS 8.0 and later) under the "Browser" section and make sure that the setting "Allow Safari (iOS 8.0 and later)" is either not configured or set to "Yes"2) Under the "Show or Hide Apps" section make sure that Safari is not listed as one of the hidden applications3) Once confirmed that Safari is no longer disabled or hidden in the General Configuration Policy (iOS 8.0 and later), refresh the policy on the iOS device and confirm that the "All Apps" link in the Company Portal launches successfully

symptom: Whether a device is a desktop, laptop or tablet is not the primary deciding factor regarding whether to enroll using the Full PC Intune client or the MDM agent. Due to the current differences in the available feature sets between the two choices, each organization will want to carefully consider which choice meets their needs
solution: To manage Windows PCs, you have two choices:+ Enroll the device orInstall the Intune software client.Decide which method to useEnrolling Windows PCs in MDM to get the most functionality. However, the Intune software client might be more suitable for your needs when:The PC is running Windows 7You want to manage Windows software updates and license usage                                  More Information                                    Information taken from https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/pc-management-comparisonOrganizations can use Microsoft Intune to manage Windows PCs either as mobile devices with mobile device management (MDM) or as computers with the Intune software client. Microsoft recommends that customers use the MDM management solution whenever possible. To help you better understand the differences between these options, however, the following chart compares the two management options.Capability / ScenarioWindows as ComputerIntune software clientWindows as Mobile DeviceMDMOperating systemsWindows 10, Windows 8+, Windows 7, Windows VistaWindows 10+Intune Portal supportSilverlight consoleAzure portalConditional AccessNot availableAvailableWhat is Conditional Access?Bulk enrollmentNot availableAvailableBulk enrollment for Windows devicesDevice profilesNot availableAvailableWhat are Microsoft Intune device profiles?Agentless enrollmentNot availableAvailableEnroll Windows devicesSoftware update managementWindows Updates and Microsoft app updatesKeep Windows PCs up-to-date with software updatesMicrosoft Store for Business for both Windows 10 and Microsoft apps updatesConfigure Windows Update for Business settingsSoftware license managementAvailableManage license agreements for Windows PC softwareMicrosoft Store for Business (.appx apps only)Manage apps purchased from the Microsoft Store for BusinessInventoryAvailableView hardware and software inventory for Windows PCsAvailableHow to monitor app informationWhat is device managementWindows Firewall policyAvailableHelp protect Windows PCs using Windows Firewall policiesAvailableWindows Defender FirewallAnti-malware protectionEndpoint ProtectionHelp secure Windows PCs with Endpoint ProtectionWindows DefenderEnable Windows DefenderRemote assistanceTeamViewerRequest and provide remote assistance for Windows PCsTeamViewerUse TeamViewer to remotely administer Intune devicesApp deploymentNot available for Microsoft Store for Business,.exe, .appx, and multi-file .msi onlyAdd apps for Windows PCs that run the Intune software clientAvailable for Microsoft Store apps and line-of-business appsHow to add Windows store appsHow to add Windows line-of-business (LOB) appsApp protectionNot availableAvailableWhat are app protection policies?Health attestationNot availableAvailableAdvantages of MDM Windows PC managementWindows PC management with modern mobile device management has the following advantages:Scalability - MDM management scales with Intune cloud management. The Intune software client is limited to 7000 PCs.Simplicity - Uses modern management capabilities included in the operating system without relying on a downloaded software clientConsistency - Your Windows PCs are managed like all other mobile devices in your organization

symptom: When a tenant was set to Intune as the MDM Authority, Device Categories were created and used.After some time, the MDM Authority was reset from Intune to SCCM (Configuration Manager).These Device Categories that were present prior to the reset cannot be viewed or managed within the Configuration Manager console and cannot be deleted from the Intune portal.
solution: These Device Categories are not manageable from external interfaces and require a CRI (IcM) to be filed to request that an internal operation is run to remove the Device Categories from the tenant.After the internal ops procedure is run, the customer may add and/or remove new Device Categories via the Configuration Manager (SCCM) console.
