This could simply be because the policy is not compatible with the OS version and device type or that the policy is incorrectly targeted to the user or device. Another way you can resolve this is by following the troubleshooting steps we�ve listed below.

When using Intune on premise Exchange conditional access when you try to use a device access rule like the one in the screen shot below to allow Outlook App access.   Even with Exchange  The Outlook App devices are blocked.
rootCause: This is because Intune on premise Exchange conditional access does not support the Outlook App for IOS and Android.   Any Outlook Apps that are detected will be blocked by the Intune Service.  Any Outlook entry that is allowed by Exchange on-premise device access rule will blocked.
solution: If you have users that need to use the Outlook App you can not target them for conditional access.   Or you can enable hybrid modern authentication for Outlook IOS and Android.   See the following link for more information https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/Exchange/clients/outlook-for-ios-and-android/use-hybrid-modern-auth

symptom: Devices added in the Apple Device Enrollment Program are not showing in the Intune portal after Sync completes.When you check Rave->Tenant->Applications->Intune->Troubleshooting->Apple Dep->Token->Token Specific Profile Sync History or Token Specific Profile Sync Failure History and you see the following response:Example:2018-09-02 13:14:36.7606181   AMSUA0201      IntunePE            StatelessMTAppleDepSyncService            Dep       6.1808.9.92300 4898fbe1-9b70-4409-b9fd-f3cca3a7aef5              DepFeatureTelemetry    SyncMultiTokenDevicesToAppleFailureEvent       Full        4bf3add6-2ea1-451f-a8bb-c7063e6694f0   tokenId;exceptionStatusCode;    4bf3add6-2ea1-451f-a8bb-c7063e6694f0            429       Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessMTAppleSyncService.Exceptions.AppleDepException: HTTP request failed: 429 - Too Many Requests, request URI: https://mdmenrollment.apple.com/session   at Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessMTAppleSyncService.Clients.ProxyBase.ThrowOnError(ILoggerContext context, IHttpResponse httpResponse, Uri requestUri)   at Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessMTAppleSyncService.Clients.RestProxyBase.ParseHttpResponse[T](ILoggerContext loggerContext, Uri resourceUri, IHttpResponse response)   at Microsoft.Management.Services.StatelessMTAppleSyncService.Clients.RestProxyBase.<GetResourceAsync>d__14`1.MoveNext()
rootCause: This is caused due to an API change that Apple made in July of 2018 and this change modified the thresholds in regards to number of sync's and devices sync'd for tenants.Currently these numbers cannot be shared, but the result is that once the threshold is reached Apple throttles the tenant and places in in a "Timeout" box presenting the 429 response and preventing the devices from Syncing into Intune service correctly.
solution: A IcM was filed for this https://icm.ad.msft.net/imp/v3/incidents/details/78656126/home and Microsoft worked with Apple but after delayed headway, a work around from our side was put into place. This workaround increased the batch size limit from 100 to 200 in DF and PE, added some additional logging, adjusted the use of session tokens when doing DEP sync's with the Apple Service and comes with a Flight tag (this code change was made on 9.13.18), if your customer is still getting the "429 - Too Many Requests, request URI: https://mdmenrollment.apple.com/session" assure they have the flight tag on their tenant:1. Open Rave2. Enter the tenant3. Select Applications->Intune4. Select Data->Show More5. Verify you see Flighting Tags "IncreaseDEPSyncDeviceLimit" and "IncreaseDelayBetweenDEPSync"If either of these are missing, file an IcM to have the Flight Tags added, once added the tenant upon its next sync will start working again.If both tags are present but the tenant is still getting the 429 messages and is a very large tenant, open an IcM as further adjustments to code may need to occur.

symptom: Monitoring Agent (x64) fails to install with 0x80070643Agtinstaller.log shows that monitoring agent failed to install with 0x80cf200bDeployed updates fail with 0x80cf4017
rootCause:
Intune is not compatible with other Microsoft Monitoring Solution agents, . this is because the Intune service uses the same base Monitoring agent.Some known examples include(but are not limited to):OpsMgr 2012 or OpsMgr 2012 R2Microsoft
System Center 2012 Service ManagerMicrosoft
System Center 2012 R2 Service ManagerAzure Operational Insights
solution: To resolve the error above, remove any other versions of Microsoft Monitoring Agents from Add/Remove Programs or Apps and Features, then rebootOpen the Microsoft Intune Center and click Check for Update to allow Intune to download the Monitoring Agent as an update and reattempt the install.

symptom:
DEP devices no
longer tagged as Supervised after doing a wipe and re-enrolling, this effects only Intune Hybrid SCCM and not stand alone Intune.

 

Scenario: 

1.          
A DEP device enrolls as supervised device (This is a new device that has never
been enrolled before.  IOSDeviceInfo will be set to IsSupervised = true.) 

2.          
The user resets this device.

3.          
The user re-enrolls the device Resulting that Supervised options are greyed out in the ConfigMgr Console and un-usable.
rootCause:
This is a known
issue that is under long term development for solution, the issue was original
fixed in 1802 but has re-occurred multiple times in two different variations.
The issue is that in the Intune side the IsSupervised=true state still exists
for the device in the IOSDeviceInfos table and since present, the value is not
sent during this enrollment and table \ row creation in Intune Hybrid SCCM
configurations. This is due to the detection method code in the
"CacheManager.cs" for check in code and it not sending data it
already considers known\sent.

 

BUG Tracking: https://msazure.visualstudio.com/Intune/_workitems/edit/2789750
solution:
Use one of the two
work arounds based on break scenario.

NOTE:  Assure to remind your customer to always
before making ConfigMgr DB changes\updates to assure they have a current\valid
SQL and ConfigMgr backup.Work Around I - If
  the Row PropertyName = IsSupervised is present, but NULL.NOTE: A cleaner singe SQL Query
version is in works between Charlesa and Karanr that will flow more like Work
Around II. Once done I will update this article, but did not want to slow the release of info.1.        Open
SQL Management Studio2.        Select
the CM DB3.        Add
the following query to determine the impacted devicesselect mcr.uniqueusername,
ud.CloudUserID, serialnumber as deviceserial, machineid, name, smsid,
activationlockstate, activationlockbypassstatetimestamp,
activationlockbypassstate, issupervisedfrom
vSMS_CombinedDeviceResources cdr join v_usermachinerelation mcr
on mcr.machineresourceid= cdr.machineidjoin user_disc ud on
ud.Unique_User_Name0=mcr.UniqueUserNameWHERE clienttype = 3 and
ClientEdition = 8 and isactive = 1 and architecturekey=5 and deviceowner=1 and
issupervised IS NULL4.
Execute the query.5.
Export the data into a CSV.6.
Add the following query to update each of the devices impacted - Update the DeviceID1, DeviceID2, DeivceIDX, ect. update
[CMSD_LQQ].[dbo].[MDMDeviceProperty]set PropertyValue = 'True'where PropertyName =
'IsSupervised'and DeviceID in
(�<DeviceID1>�, �<DeviceID2>�, �<DeviceID X>�)7. Execute the query.8.   Verify the Supervised
options are now active in the ConfigMgr console for the devices.
  
 


Work around
  II  - If the Row PropertyName =
  IsSupervised is missing.Work around II  - If the Row PropertyName = IsSupervised is
missing. (Thanks Karanr for the great query).1.        Open
SQL Management Studio2.        Select
the CM DB3.        Add
the following queryCREATE
TABLE #DEPFix (ID INT IDENTITY (1, 1),DeviceID uniqueidentifier);INSERT
INTO #DEPFix SELECT smsid from vSMS_CombinedDeviceResources cdr join
v_usermachinerelation mcr on mcr.machineresourceid= cdr.machineid join
user_disc ud on ud.Unique_User_Name0=mcr.UniqueUserName WHERE clienttype = 3
and ClientEdition = 8 and isactive = 1 and architecturekey=5 and deviceowner=1
and issupervised IS NULL DECLARE
@SITENUM intSELECT
@SITENUM=SiteKey from Sites where SiteCode='<SiteCode>'DECLARE
@COLO uniqueidentifierDECLARE
@INSTANCE int = 1WHILE
@INSTANCE <= (select MAX(ID) from #DEPFix)BEGIN      SET @COLO = NULL      SELECT @COLO=DeviceID from #DEPFix WHERE
ID = @INSTANCE      INSERT INTO MDMDeviceProperty (DeviceID,
PropertyName, PropertyValue, LastUpdateTime, SiteNumber) SELECT @COLO,
'IsSupervised', 'True', GETDATE(), @SITENUM       SET @INSTANCE = @INSTANCE + 1ENDDROP
TABLE #DEPFix4.        Update
the query to replace <SiteCode> with your ConfigMgr SideCode, example
SELECT @SITENUM=SiteKey from Sites where SiteCode='PS1'5.        Execute
the SQL Query.6.        Verify
the Supervised options are now active in the ConfigMgr console for the devices.

symptom:
When attempting delete an Autopilot Deployment Profile, the following error is returned:

Cannot delete <AutopilotProfile Name>

The profile is assigned to groups.  You must unassign all groups from this
profile before you can delete it.
rootCause: This issue can occur for one of the two following reasons:1.  There is an active group still assigned to the Autopilot Deployment Profile.2.  There is a  Group that was assigned to the Autopilot profile that has been deleted from Azure Active Direction before removing the Assignment from the profile and the link to the group remains within the Assignment and cannot be removed. Note that in this scenario, you can follow the workaround below to delete the profile immediately, or if the customer is willing to wait, the link to the deleted group should clear in 7 days and the Admin can manually delete the profile from within the portal then.
solution:
Execute the following procedures to remove the group assignment if the group has not been deleted from Azure Active Directory.

1.      
Logon to Azure Portal.

2.      
Select Intune.

3.      
Select Device
Enrollment � Windows enrollment > Windows Autopilot deployment profiles.

4.      
Select the Autopilot deployment profile.

5.      
Select Assignment.

6.      
Remove all groups assigned.If the Group has been deleted from Azure Active Directory however, the process is a bit more detailed to remove the profile.  Please follow the procedures below to gather the pertinent information to execute the deletion via Graph Explorer.

Execute the following procedures to find the
AutopilotProfileID:

1.      
Logon to Azure Portal.

2.      
Select Intune.

3.      
Select Device
Enrollment � Windows enrollment > Windows Autopilot deployment profiles.

4.      
Select the Profile in question.

5.      
Copy the ID from the Address bar. 

Execute the one of following procedures to capture the GroupID for the Group assigned to the
profile but has been deleted from Azure Active Directory.

I.                  Execute the following procedures to ascertain
the GroupID via F12 Tracing.

a.      
Login to the portal here.

b.      
Navigate to the step just
prior to where the issue is happening.  Press F5 or refresh.

c.      
Press F12 to start the browser
Developer Tools (IE and Edge)

d.      
Click on the Network, stop the
tracing and click on the clear session button

e.      
Start the session and
reproduce the issue

f.       
Stop the trace.

g.      
 In the
trace, look for the GET request starting Name groups.  Grab the URL and copy the GroupID.



II.       Execute the following procedures to ascertain
the GroupID via Graph Explorer.

a.      
Connect and logon to Graph
Explorer with a Global Admin for the Tenant.

b.      
Run the following Query to capture details on
the profile.https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/deviceManagement/windowsAutopilotDeploymentProfiles/<AutopilotProfileID>Where:  <AutopilotProfileID> = the AutopilotProfileID gathered above.

c.      
Run the following Query to capture details on
the Assignment

https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/deviceManagement/windowsAutopilotDeploymentProfiles/<AutopilotProfileID>/assignmentsWhere:  <AutopilotProfileID> = the AutopilotProfileID gathered above.

d.      
The output should be similar to the
following.  Capture the GroupID from the
output.  Execute the following query to delete the
Assignment.



https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/deviceManagement/windowsAutopilotDeploymentProfiles/<AutopilotProfileID>/assignments/<AutopilotProfileID>_<GroupID>Where:  <AutopilotProfileID> = the AutopilotProfileID gathered from Intune Portal above.                <GroupID> = Group ID gathered from F12 Trace or Graph query above.Execute the following query to delete the
Profile.

https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/deviceManagement/windowsAutopilotDeploymentProfiles/<AutopilotProfileID>/ Where:  <AutopilotProfileID> = the AutopilotProfileID gathered from Intune Portal.

symptom: When Jamf is integrated with Microsoft Intune, if the Create Mobile account at Login option is enabled on a Mac device, the Intune Company Portal app fails to launch through the Self-Service Portal and enrollment fails when using an Active Directory user account with a mobile profile.
rootCause: Currently this behavior is by design.
solution: To work around this issue, log on using a local account and register the device, then once registration/enrollment is successful, log out and log back in using the AD account with a mobile profile.

symptom: Unable to delete Autopilot devices - Some devices failed to delete message when devices have been uploaded via CSV.
rootCause: Code Flaw on the DDS service side with registrations in ADRS, junk being left behind in ZTD due to code return.
solution: DDS has disabled signaling to ADRS on deletion. If device is deleted and registered again, registration will update ADRS with latest ZTDId. Fixed as of 10.4.18.

symptom: Information needed to troubleshoot Jamf Registration and Compliance Issues - collect tenant name, UPN, AAD Device ID, AAD AccountId, ASU, screenshots, Company portal logs, compliance policy details, Jamf version, Jamf case number.
Customer receives notification in Jamf console: Unable to connect to Microsoft Intune. Check your Microsoft Intune Integration configuration.
rootCause: Customer Jamf license had expired.
solution: Customer should contact Jamf for assistance.

symptom: Could not retrieve the access token for Microsoft Graph API. Check the configuration for Microsoft Intune Integration error message when trying to confirm settings from JAMF console.
rootCause: Customer is using a proxy and had required ports blocked
solution: The following ports need to be accessible for Jamf and Intune to work properly: Intune - Port 443 Apple - 2195/2196/5223 (Push notifications to Intune) Jamf - port 80, 5223

symptom: To test if Jamf is communicating with Microsoft Intune, do the following: 1. In Jamf Pro, navigate to Settings > Global Management > Conditional Access > Test macOS Intune Integration and click Test. 2. Below is an example of a successful connection test.
rootCause: N/A
solution: If connection test fails, the customer should contact Jamf support to troubleshoot.

symptom: Customer is prompted with a keychain signing message for TEAMS, Outlook (O365 apps) after enrolling into Jamf with Intune integration.
rootCause: The workplace join key is put down by AAD teams code which the Intune company portal runs.
solution: This is expected since an App is trying to access a keychain item and the user is being prompted to allow access.

symptom: Unable to delete Auto pilot devices. Some devices failed to delete message when devices that have been uploaded via CSV.
rootCause: Code Flaw on the DDS service side with registrations in ADRS, in short junk being left behind in ZTD due to code return.
solution: Verification your case is a match. DDS has temp disabled the signaling to ADRS that the deletion happened. If the device is deleted and registered again, registration will update ADRS with the latest ZTDId. Customer should be able to delete without issue now. As of 10.4.18 - DDS states this is fixed.

symptom: When troubleshooting issues with Jamf integrated with Intune, collect the following information: Tenant name, UPN and AAD Device ID, AAD AccountId, ASU, Full screenshots of device from Azure portal, Full screenshots of device from Jamf Pro, Company portal logs, Screenshots of any errors, Full screenshots of Intune compliance policy and corresponding Jamf policy, Version of Jamf Pro, Jamf case number.
Customer receives the following notification in the Jamf console: Unable to connect to Microsoft Intune. Check your Microsoft Intune Integration configuration.
rootCause: Customer Jamf license had expired
solution: Customer should contact Jamf for assistance.

symptom: To troubleshoot Jamf compliance: 1. Collect the following information a. AAD Device ID b. AAD AccountId c. ASU Note: Compliance sync can take from 1 hour plus depending on load. Check portal.manage.microsoft.com (IW portal) on the Mac device to see the latest compliance information. This will have the most up-to-date information. 2. Run the following query in Kusto // Col1 contains userId and deviceId, // Col2 is ComplianceState: 1- Compliance, 2 - not compliant // Col4: if Col2 is 1, "NoRuleDetailNeeded" // if Col2 is 2, the rule that's out of compliant. it has RuleId - name of the rule, ExpectedValue - the value in the policy, ActualValue - the value send by Jamf IntuneEvent | where SourceNamespace == "IntunePE" and env_time >= datetime (2020-07-13 20:18:30.3715063) | where EventUniqueName == "ComplianceReportHelperComplianceReport" | where Col1 has "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-018fb3120fe9" | project env_time , env_cloud_name , ColMetadata , Col1 , Col2 , Col3 , Col4 , AccountId , ActivityId , ScenarioInstanceId , ScenarioType NOTE Column 4 lists compliance policy information Query results example showing expected value mismatch: RuleDetail:RuleName:;RuleId:MacOSCompliancePolicy.OsMinimumVersion_IID_xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-5bd9c336ce03;SettingId:PlatformOSVersion_ macOSMinimumVersion;ExpectedValue:10.14 ; ActualValue:10.13.6 ;ErrorType:0;ErrorCode:0;InstanceSource:007d2fff-e0dd-4b28-8595-cec005efe5cd If there is no compliance mismatch you will see - NoRuleDetailNeeded 3. Run the below query in kusto to get the overall Jamf State of the device, apart from the compliance evaluation messages we have traditionally been looking at: IntuneEvent | where env_time between ( datetime (2021-03-24 08:20).. datetime (2021-03-24 09:30)) | where env_cloud_name == "AMSUA0401" | where EventUniqueName == "PartnerDeviceConverted" | where DeviceId == "<DeviceID>" Example output State=1,OSDescription=macOS,OSVersion=11.2.0,LastUpdateTime=2/11/2021 5:39:33 AM,DeviceManagementState=0 State=0,OSDescription=macOS,OSVersion=11.2.0,LastUpdateTime=2/11/2021 5:39:33 AM,DeviceManagementState=0 Comment: The above result shows Jamf is sending us both state 0 and state 1 information at the same time. Key Table: "State":"2" = Unresponsive "State":"1" = Deactivate "State":"0" = Active
To get the Jamf policy from a Mac: 1. From the Mac, click the Launch Pad 2. Type in "ter' for Terminal 3. Type in "sudo jamf policy"

To troubleshoot if a device is not syncing inventory information with Intune: 1. Have the customer confirm the state of the device in Jamf 2. Confirm that the user has signed into the Self Service portal within the past 24 hours, if not, have them sign into the portal. 3. You can view the device state by running the following Kusto query: // not all data is shown due to privacy concern - you will see state of device reported IntuneEvent | where SourceNamespace == "IntunePE" | where env_time > ago (2d) | where EventUniqueName in ( "PartnerDeviceConverted" , "PartnerUdaConverted" , "PartnerDeviceAttributeConverted" ) | where DeviceId == "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-4895241530d9" | where ServiceName contains "PartnerDevice" | project env_time , ComponentName , EventUniqueName , ColMetadata , Col1 , Col2 , Col3 , Col4 , AccountId , ActivityId 0 == Active/Calculated activated in Jamf Pro (new or coming back from unresponsive after gatherAADInfo and Jamf policy check-in) 1 == Deactivated (deleted/retired from Jamf Pro) 2 == Unresponsive (time of last recurring check-in is more than 24 hours ago) 4. Check the State of the device. If it's " 2 ", customer needs to confirm that the device is still active in Jamf.

Devices check in with Jamf Pro every 15 minutes. If a device doesn't check in within a 24 hour period, Jamf will mark the device as unresponsive. Every registered Mac also has an Azure token. This token is refreshed every 12 hours, if it tries to refresh but is unable to for 24 hours or more, Jamf will mark the device as unresponsive. If the Azure token expires, users are prompted to log into Azure. Refresh tokens are generated every 7 days. Note: Enrolled users must log into the Self Service portal to correct the non-responsive state. It cannot be a user who has just work-place joined the account.
After setting Intune enrollment restrictions to require a minimum iOS version of 11.3.1, devices running iOS 11.3.1 fail to enroll and generate the following error: Oops, an error occurred
rootCause: This is a known issue with iOS 11.3.1. The issue was addressed in iOS 11.4 so this should be the only version affected.
solution: To work around this problem, configure the iOS platform enrollment restriction to 11.3 or later.

symptom: Ensure that the Company Portal app is being launched from the Jamf Self-Service portal. The Company Portal must be launched from Jamf Self Service to begin device registration. IMPORTANT : Launching the Company Portal app manually (e.g. from the Applications or Downloads folder) will NOT register the device. If an end user launches the Company Portal app manually, they will see a warning, 'AccountNotOnboarded ". If the device has been enrolled into Jamf before, have the customer follow the steps on "How to unenroll a Mac from Jamf " and attempt enrollment again. Previously enrolled devices that have not been properly unenrolled exhibit various issues when attempting to enroll the device again.

To unenroll a Mac from Jamf registered with Intune: On the Mac, launch Terminal Type in �sudo JAMF removemdmprofile� Type in �sudo JAMF removeFramework� On the JAMF Pro server delete the machine�s inventory record Delete device from AzureAD Delete the following files on the machine if they exist: /Library/Application Support/com.microsoft.CompanyPortal.usercontext.info /Library/Application Support/com.microsoft.CompanyPortal /Library/Application Support/com.jamfsoftware.selfservice.mac /Library/Saved Application State/com.jamfsoftware.selfservice.mac.savedState /Library/Saved Application State/com.microsoft.CompanyPortal.savedState /Library/Preferences/com.microsoft.CompanyPortal.plist /Library/Preferences/com.jamfsoftware.selfservice.mac.plist /Library/Preferences/com.jamfsoftware.management.jamfAAD.plist /Users/<username>/Library/Cookies/com.microsoft.CompanyPortal.binarycookies /Users/<username>/Library/Cookies/com.jamf.management.jamfAAD.binarycookies com.microsoft.CompanyPortal com.microsoft.CompanyPortal.HockeySDK enterpriseregistration.windows.net https://device.login.microsoftonline.com https://device.login.microsoftonline.com/ Microsoft Session Transport Key (public AND private keys) Microsoft Workplace Join Key (public AND private keys) Remove anything from keychain on the machine that references Microsoft, Intune, or Company Portal including DeviceLogin.microsoft.com certificates Remove JAMF references except for JAMF public and private key (removing the public and private key will break enrollment) Go through the entries one by one and delete the following shown below, if they exist. Kind: Application password ; Account: com.microsoft.workplacejoin.thumbprint Kind: Application password ; Account: com.microsoft.workplacejoin.registeredUserPrincipalName Kind: Certificate ; Issued by: MS-Organization-Access Kind: Identity preference ; Name (ADFS STS URL if present): https://adfs[DNSName].com/adfs/ls Kind: Identity preference ; Name: https://enterpriseregistration.windows.net Kind: Identity preference ; Name: https://enterpriseregistration.windows.net/ Reboot Mac Uninstall Company Portal from machine Go to portal.manage.microsoft.com and delete out all of the instances of the Mac device. Wait at least 30 minutes and perform step 12. Re-enroll computer in JAMF Pro Re-open Self Service and start Registration policy
Customer enrolls macOS devices into Jamf and registers with Intune for Partner Device Management integration but upon launching the company portal app, device shows "Not registered"
rootCause: From CP app logs: Line 7783: 2018-07-25 21:41:25.116 INFO com.microsoft.ssp.application TID=1 WelcomeViewController.swift : 253 ( startLogin ()) Portal launched without WPJ only arg while account is under partner management
solution: Company Portal (CP) should not be launched directly in JAMF cases, only JAMF should be launching CP with partnermanaged flag. You can confirm the Company Portal was launched correctly in Registration mode only by searching the logs and finding [-r] To change the registration source from Intune to Jamf: Remove the macOS device from Intune . To avoid further complications for devices that aren't fully removed from Intune, see Cause 6 below. On the device, use Jamf Self Service to open the Company Portal app, and then register the device with Azure AD. This task requires you to have already completed the following tasks: Deploy the Company Portal app for macOS in Jamf Pro Create a policy in Jamf Pro to have users register their devices with Azure AD When the portal opens, the first screen you see prompts you to sign in. Use your work or school account The Company Portal confirms your account information and shows your Device Enrollment and Device Compliance statuses. Yellow triangles highlight the actions you need to take to secure your macOS device for school or work. Click Begin to start enrollment. If prompted, type in your computer's sign-in information. It might take a few minutes to register your device. You'll receive a message after the registration is completed to let you know you're done.

Customer
receives the following message when trying to register a Jamf enrolled device
with Intune: "Invalid command line input.
Registration-only command line flag (-r) can only be used when partner
management is enabled in Intune. Please contact your IT admin".
rootCause:
After
upgrading to 10.7.1 customer found that the Intune integration was disabled due
to disabling it on the customer�s Test and D/R servers.  Jamf Pro Server
sends a pulse to the Intune servers when this is unchecked, telling Intune that

in a "partner device management

symptom: Apple Business Manager is supported with Intune.It is not specifically called out in our documentation, at this time, as it uses the current VPP and DEP technology, so support should be approached in the same manner.The only feature that Apple Business Manager adds that is not supported, at this time, is Managed Apple ID's.Apple Business Manager, and more, is detailed in this session from Ignite 2018 and can be shared with customers: https://myignite.techcommunity.microsoft.com/sessions/64340?source=speakerdetail#ignite-html-anchor

OneDrive KFM (Known Folder Move) allows you to redirect common Windows folders (Desktop, Documents and Pictures) to the users personal OneDrive. The deployment with Microsoft Intune allows you to trigger or automate the OneDrive KFM configuration for your end users.
rootCause: Protect Critical Data With OneDrive & Known Folder Move (KFM)https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/nzedu/2018/06/29/protect-critical-data-with-onedrive-known-folder-move-kfm/
solution: To setup OneDrive for Business KFM, you will need to perform the following actions:1. Make sure OneDrive Groove client is installed, with version higher than 18.100.x2. Create a Windows 10 Custom Configuration policy from Intune with the following settings:a. One Policy with the ADMX:Name    ADMX setupDescription         ADMX   OMA-URI            ./Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/OneDriveNGSC/Policy/OneDriveAdmx     Data type            String    Value    Content from the OneDrive ADMX that can be found here: https://github.com/nicolonsky/Techblog/blob/master/OneDriveKFM/OneDrive.admxb. One Policy for the Silent ConfigurationName    Silent     Description         Silent                     OMA-URI            ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/OneDriveNGSC~Policy~OneDriveNGSC/SilentAccountConfig                Data type            String    Value    <enabled/>c. One Policy for blocking KFM WizardName    NoWizard    Description         NoWizard                    OMA-URI            ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/OneDriveNGSC~Policy~OneDriveNGSC/KFMOptInNoWizard                Data type            String    Value    <enabled/><data id="KFMOptInNoWizard_TextBox" value="DIRECTORYID"/><data id="KFMOptInNoWizard_Dropdown" value="1"/>DIRECTORYID can be copied from Azure Active Directory > Properties, or go directly to this link:https://portal.azure.com/#blade/Microsoft_AAD_IAM/ActiveDirectoryMenuBlade/Properties d. One Policy to prevent users to roll back the settings:Name    KFMBlockOptOutDescription         KFMBlockOptOutOMA-URI            ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/OneDriveNGSC~Policy~OneDriveNGSC/KFMBlockOptOut  Data type            String    Value    <enabled/>

symptom: Customer complains that Compliance is not updated on MDM enrolled devices with Intune Hybrid and in dmpdownloader.log you see the following errors:ERROR: DmpDownloader:ProcessComplianceMessage: Failed to send MDM SSB Compliance Message.. Exception: System.Data.SqlClient.SqlException (0x80131904): ERROR 50000, Level 16, State 1, Procedure spRethrowError, Line 43, Message: ERROR 50000, Level 16, State 1, Procedure spGetSSBDialogHandle, Line 59, Message: Route is not defined for target site with service name ConfigMgrMDM_Compliance_SiteMET.~~
rootCause: Issue can be encounter in scenarios where Service Connection Point role is not on the same server with the Site that manages the MDM devices and SQL permissions are not setup correctly. Or we are missing the sys.routes from SQL database.
solution: Before taking any actions to SCCM SQL database, please make sure you have a proper backup.Proceed to check the following:1. Run select * from sys.routes on SQL database and confirm that you have setup correctly the routes for the Service Name were the error is present. -In this case ConfigMgrMDM_Compliance_SiteMET.2. Reconfigure the routes using: spUpdateSSBRoutes @TargetSiteCode='MET', @SSBPort=4022-where MET=site code from the customer side, and confirm that SQL port is the standard one 4022, otherwise adjust it.3. Restart SMS_EXECUTIVEMake sure that the computername where SCP role is installed has sysadmin rights on the SQL database CM_SIteCode

symptom: Delete
This walkthrough will show you how to configure and enroll a COSU device with the QR code methodWhat you'll needAndroid 7+ deviceOpen Wi-Fi Network
rootCause: Education
solution:
If you haven't already...Onboard to Google
 Approve any applications from
     the Managed Play Store that you need to be in the
     Managed Home Screen experience (including the Managed Home Screen & Android Device Policy)
 
  Navigate to Intune > Client Apps
      > Setup �
      Managed Google Play and click Sync
 
 Create an Assigned or Dynamic
     Device Group that will be used for the deployment
 
  If using Dynamic
      Device Group - set the membership rule to Add devices where / enrollmentProfileName / Equals / InsertCOSUEnrollmentProfileNameHere
 
 
  I�ll be using
      Dynamic Device Group - COSU_Dynamic_Device_Group
  My rule will be Add devices
      where / enrollmentProfileName / Equals / COSU_Enrollment_Profile
      � see below
 


 


 Create COSU Enrollment
     Profile
 
  Navigate to Intune > Android
      Enrollment >
      Kiosk and Task Device Enrollments > Create
  Name the profile what you
      chose in your Dynamic Membership Criteria
 
 
  e.g.
      COSU_Enrollment_Profile
 
 Create Kiosk Profile
 
  Navigate to Intune > Device
      Configuration >
      Profiles >
      Create Profile
  Name it what you�d like
  Platform � Android
      Enterprise
  Profile Type � Device Owner
      Only � Device Restrictions
  Navigate to the Kiosk node
      and select Multi-app kiosk
  Click Add � the list of all
      your apps will appear on the right
  Add your apps (I recommend
      the Android Device Policy in addition to whatever you want) & do not add the Managed Home Screen
 
 
  If you have Web
      Apps, be sure to include a browser
 


  



I also recommend
adding a password just to see what happens with the Android Device Policy App

 

 


 Deploy all your apps as Required to your Dynamic Device Group
 
  If you have Web
      Apps, you do not need to deploy them
 
 Factory Reset your device
 Wait for OOBE to begin and
     then tap white space ~5 times until you�re prompted to download QR Reader
 Connect to open Wi-Fi network
 Wait for the QR Reader to be
     installed (your screen will just be a camera)
 Use the QR Reader on your QR
     Code attached to your COSU Enrollment Profile
 Enroll the device and wait 
 
  If you required a
      PIN, go manually set it before the Managed Home Screen experience begins
      otherwise the policy will show as failed
 


 

 

Pro-Tip

 


 Go to the Android Device
     Policy App > click the vertical ellipses > device details > click
     Model 7ish times
 
  This will enable
      debug items
 
 Go back and click the
     vertical ellipses again
 Check �All policies�
 
  This gives you
      the good stuff and is as close as you can get to �logging�
 


 

Caveats (some repeat information)

 


 Don't add the Managed Home
     Screen app to the Multi-App Kiosk profile.
 If using web links, you do
     not need to deploy these to the device groups - only store apps are
     required to be deployed.
 If requiring a PIN, you will
     not be prompted. The setting is enforced, but you will need to configure
     the PIN "manually".
 To get out of the Managed
     Home Screen experience, all you need to do is remove the Managed Home
     Screen app deployment. You can make adjustments to the Multi-App Kiosk
     Mode profile as needed, and then redeploy the Managed Home Screen app.
 To get out of COSU, you need
     to factory reset the device.
 It is recommended for
     troubleshooting to include the Android Device Policy app in your Multi-App
     Kiosk profile so we can confirm what policies Google is sending the
     device.
 There is no direct
     communication between Intune and the device, so troubleshooting will be
     limited. Three main points that will help us troubleshoot:
 
  Confirm configuration is
      correct
  Download Policy Body from
      Rave to show what settings are saved in Graph
  Compare what we see in the
      UI, Rave, and the Android Device Policy App
  Device names will look like
      this: 30a97bfb2327b18d_AndroidEnterprise_10/2/2018_10:34 PM - we cannot
      change this as of today
  Compliance will show as Not
      Evaluated because COSU does not support Compliance Policies.

symptom: When attempting to deploy a Line of Business (LOB) app to Android devices, the deployment fails to install on the devices and generates error 0xC7D14FB5.
rootCause: This can occur if the app is being assigned as a Required app to a user group. This is expected behavior.
solution: To resolve this issue, assign the app as a Required app to a device group instead of a user group. To assign the app to a user group, the Android LOB app must be assigned as an Available app. This is by design.

symptom: Devices in an Azure Dynamic Group are being removed/added constantly. For example, everyday you might see a different number of total devices in the group even though no new devices have been added, nor should any new devices appear. In this case, the rule to add members to the  group was:(device.deviceOSType -eq "Windows") -and (device.deviceOwnership -eq "Company")Note that this only happens on devices that are hybrid and synced from an on-premise environment.
rootCause: This happens because of a race condition between Intune and AD Connect. What happens is this:1. The computer object in the local AD gets the info from the OS.2. AD Connect syncs the attributes with the transformation rule.3. Intune overwrites the attribute on Azure, and on the next sync cycle AD Connect updates it again.
solution: To work around this problem, the rule was changed:BEFORE(device.deviceOSType -eq "Windows") -and (device.deviceOwnership -eq "Company")AFTER(device.deviceOSType -contains "Windows") -and (device.deviceOwnership -eq "Company")

symptom:
You had set up JAMF-Intune integration
appropriately and registered MacOS devices within Intune. You could see
machines registered correctly on the company portal, and was working as
expected for several days. A few days later, you found that Device
configuration checks would not show as compliant. You re-registered from JAMF
Pro portal. When you view the Azure portal, you saw the device in Azure Active
Directory devices but not in Intune devices. It appeared to not register the
Serial Number in Intune, causing an error. JAMF noted this problem and had also
opened a Microsoft ticket under PI-006105.
rootCause:
During the investigation, JAMF released a new
version of 10.6.2 which contained a fix for the issue.
solution:
After
  Jamf released 10.6.2, you had to do the following steps to get the client to
  re-register: 1. Delete the Jamf Client from the MAC 2. Uninstall the Intune
  Company Portal 3. Delete the MAC device (unmanage) from the JAMF console 4.
  Delete the Mac device from Azure. 5. Once you reinstalled the JAMF client,
  Company Portal, then ran the Azure Join, this resulted in the device
  appearing correctly in Azure AD Devices and within Intune Devices; however,
  the previous Mac device instance was still present. You attempted to delete
  the previous instance of the device from the list of devices, but this device
  still came back. After researching this further with our Intune engineering
  team for our Mac OS deletion, I got the following confirmation from them:
  Based on the trace and the screenshots, you are performing a delete from the
  list of devices which we term a �bulk delete.� A bulk delete of a Jamf device
  is currently not expected to work. We should be adding this in our 1809
  release. We did add a delete option in our 1808 release that should enable
  the delete button within the device details view to allow a Jamf device to be
  deleted. 1808 is actively rolling out to customer tenants now so you can test
  this in case your tenant has the full 1808 release by selecting the device to
  open the device details then seeing if you have a delete button available.
  When you waited a few days for the 1808 release to complete on your tenant,
  you reported that you were able to successfully delete the device via device
  details.

symptom: When the Managed Browser and Edge Browser are targeted by an App Proxy redirection.Typing in the Internal URL generates a 503 or similar instead of redirecting to the Azure App proxy External URL . This error can be easily seen when using fiddler.
rootCause: The Azure App proxy is not configured correctly or there may be issues with assignments (User/Group)
solution: First thing to check is whether the policy is successfully applying to the device (via Rave/Kusto)Next, engage CXP to pull the blob from the app (Needs a test account from the customer).In the blob, we will see the URL pairings that are being pushed by the Azure App Proxy configuration.Unless we receive the right pairings (Internal URL:External URL), a redirect will always generate a 503 error.To resolve, verify that user is targeted by the application and the Azure App Proxy is configured as expected following these instructions. if necessary, target a user directly to bypass issues related to grouping. Note: Nested groups are not supported.https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/manage-apps/application-proxyFor configuring the Managed Browser:https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/app-configuration-managed-browser

symptom:
After creating and assigning an iOS update policy to prevent
software update installations during a specified day/time range, the policy
settings appear to be ignored and any available iOS updates are immediately
installed on a targeted device. 

For example, when the policy is configured as shown in the
screen shot below, the assumption is that the software update will be blocked during
the specified days and time period. However, software updates are not blocked,
and updates remain visible on the device.
rootCause:
This is a known issue with the current release of Intune and
iOS 11.3.1 and later.Note: The Delay visibility of software is only supported on iOS 11.3 and later.
solution:
UI was updated 10/2018 in the Intune portal to make the functionality more clearAs a workaround, if you wish to delay visibility of updates
to end users, do not create an update policy. Instead, upload a Custom Device
Configuration policy with the following key-value pairs set:<key>forceDelayedSoftwareUpdates</key><true /><key>enforcedSoftwareUpdateDelay</key><integer>30</integer>

symptom: Intune Service allows iOS device to be enrolled a second time while already enrolled actively with different UPN. Just sign out of the CP app with first UPN it was enrolled with and then sign in with another and you can enroll a again, and both devices are showing as enrolled and active under different UPNs at the same time in the service. This results in what the customer's perceive as a duplicate in the portal buts is more serious and affects management of the device.How to verify for your customer if reported via Service Request:1. Enroll an iOS device with a UPN as BYOD2. Once enrolled:a. Verify the device shows in the Intune Portal and Rave under the UPN as successfully managed and compliant.3. Sign out of the Company Portal App on the iOS device that is enrolled.4. Sign into the Company Portal App with another UPN (not same as one used to enroll the iOS device)5. Enroll the device a second time, with out removing the previous enrollment (wizard will walk you through like was not enrolled).6. Verify  the device shows in the Intune Portal and Rave twice under two UPNsExample 1:Example 2:Example 3: You will see both in Rave as well, by the following steps:1. Open Rave2. Open the tenant in question in Rave3. Select Troubleshooting->Devices->Serial4. Search for the serial of the device that has been enrolled multiple times with the offending behavior (code flaw)5. You will see the entries and that they are showing as activeExample 1:Example 2:Example 3:
rootCause: Expected behavior due to past release code change.
solution: Currently IcM has been filed and pending investigation: https://icm.ad.msft.net/imp/v3/incidents/details/87038752/home Per Karan Rustagi this has been filed as a DCR to add previous experience behavior back, preventing second enrollment with out retirement first.CxE\Devs state: [Karan Rustagi] Karan Rustagi (karanr) Other at 10/12/2018 11:58:04 EDT Transferring back to queue for Myra as it's a DCR Karan Rustagi (karanr) Edited at 10/12/2018 11:57:00 EDT Intune Service allows iOS device to be enrolled a second time while already enrolled actively with different UPN. Just sign out of the CP app with first UPN it was enrolled with and then sign in with another and you can enroll a again, and both devices are showing as enrolled and active under different UPNs at the same time in the service. Expected behaviour: Block second enrollmentKaran Rustagi (karanr) Edited at 10/12/2018 11:39:56 EDT Ele == Ele Ocholi Karan Rustagi (karanr) Edited at 10/12/2018 11:39:01 EDT Chatted with Ele about this after speaking with Brian. In previous versions, new enrollments in this scenario may have been blocked but likely a change on the enrollment service side allows it now. Will reach out to Charles to understand customer's ask. We may have to take this as a DCR.

symptom: If you have a customer who requests Root Cause Analysis (RCA) for a tenant specific issue, please see the current policy information below.

Error
during sign in to Company Portal - "Could not sign in. You will need to
sign in again. If you see this message again, please contact your IT
admin"

ADFS certificate
prompt at portal.manage.microsoft.com
rootCause:
The
Intune Company Portal app only supports Forms Based Authentication
solution:
This
error is occurring because the ADFS server is asking for a certificate-based
authentication (CBA) and the Intune Company Portal has no mechanism to handle
this authentication type

You
can also verify this on any device by using either test@contoso.com(Replace
Contoso with customer domain), however if you type in
test@contoso.onmicrosoft.com you are properly redirected to the password screen
as it does not rely on the ADFS server.

 

This Blog outlines how to change the ADFS Proxy Authentication type to Forms Based Authentication (FBA)

https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/josrod/2014/10/15/enabled-forms-based-authentication-in-adfs-3-0/

symptom: JAMF-Intune integration: After registering MacOS devices, device configuration checks show non-compliant after a few days. Re-registering from JAMF Pro shows device in Azure AD but not in Intune. Serial Number not registered in Intune causing errors.
rootCause: Bug in JAMF versions prior to 10.6.2 causing device registration issues with Intune.
solution: Upgrade JAMF to 10.6.2+. Steps: 1) Delete JAMF Client from MAC, 2) Uninstall Intune Company Portal, 3) Delete MAC device from JAMF console, 4) Delete Mac device from Azure, 5) Reinstall JAMF client and Company Portal, then run Azure Join. Note: Bulk delete of JAMF devices not supported until 1809 release; use device details delete button (available in 1808+).

symptom: Managed Browser and Edge Browser targeted by App Proxy redirection: typing Internal URL generates 503 error instead of redirecting to Azure App Proxy External URL.
rootCause: Azure App Proxy not configured correctly or issues with user/group assignments. Nested groups are not supported.
solution: Check policy is applying to device via Rave/Kusto. Engage CXP to pull blob from app (needs test account). Verify URL pairings (Internal:External) in blob. Ensure user is targeted by application and App Proxy configured per docs. Target user directly to bypass grouping issues.

symptom: This article describes how to use a built-in app like the native messaging application on devices enrolled in Intune using Multi-App Kiosk Mode (COSU).

You created a device configuration profile for Android that contains a list of prohibited apps. The profile deploys successfully to devices that have prohibited apps installed. However, there is no reporting of prohibited apps in the Monitor node of your profile.
rootCause: The reporting of prohibited apps is posted to a dedicated node, separate from the policy itself.
solution: This is how to monitor restrictive apps: Login to https://portal.azure.com. On the Intune blade, go to Device Configuration. Under Monitor, select Devices with restricted apps.

symptom: Customer reports that approved Managed Play Apps are not syncing to Intune
rootCause: Two main causes are 1. Incorrect Google Account 2. IT Pro has not clicked sync in the Intune Portal
solution: Collect and review screenshots from the customer environment to verify Google Account, Organization details, and sync status in both Google Play and Intune portals.

symptom: Devices managed by Intune can be administered remotely using TeamViewer. The TeamViewer v13 out of the box experience launches the wrong components - on the ITPro side it tries to launch the Quick Support app (client component) and on the IW side it tries to use the existing older Quick Support app if present.
solution: 1) For IT Pro, install the v13 Windows app directly from https://www.teamviewer.us/teamviewer-automatic-download 2) For IW (end user) client, uninstall QuickSupport if already there, and install when promoted by Teamviewer.

symptom: When you try to enroll a Windows 10 device automatically by using Group Policy, you experience Event 76 Auto MDM Enroll: Failed (Unknown Win32 Error code: 0x8018002b) in Task Scheduler and Event Viewer.
rootCause: This issue can occur if the UPN contains an unverified or non-routable domain, such as joe@contoso.local.
solution: On the AD DS server, open Active Directory Users and Computers, select a valid UPN suffix (e.g. contoso.com) for affected users, then force a Delta Sync: Import-Module ADSync; Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Delta

symptom: Attempting to enroll an iOS device into Microsoft Intune fails with the following error: This Service is not supported. No Enrollment Policy.
rootCause: This problem can occur if an Apple MDM push certificate is not configured in Intune, or the certificate is invalid.
solution: If your MDM push certificate is not configured, follow the steps to configure it. If your MDM push certificate is invalid, follow the steps to renew it.

symptom: Attempting to enroll an iOS device using the Company Portal app fails with one of the the following errors: Company Portal Temporarily Unavailable or Workplace Join failed
rootCause: This can occur if the Company Portal app is out of date or corrupted.
solution: To resolve this issue complete the following:1. Remove (uninstall) the Company Portal app from the device.2. Download and install the latest version of the Microsoft Intune Company Portal app from the App Store.3. Enroll the device.

symptom: Attempting to enroll an iOS device fails with the following error: User License Type Invalid
rootCause: This can occur if the user trying to enroll the device does not have a valid Intune license.
solution: To resolve this issue, assign an Intune license to the user:1. Go to the Office 365 Admin Center, and then choose Users -> Active Users.2. Select the affected user account, and then choose Product licenses -> Edit.3. Verify that a valid Intune license is assigned to the user.4. Re-enroll the device.

symptom: Attempting to enroll an iOS device fails with the following error message: User Name Not Recognized. This user account is not authorized to use Microsoft Intune. Contact your system administrator if you think you have received this message in error.
rootCause: This can occur if the user trying to enroll the device does not have a valid Intune license.
solution: To resolve this issue, assign an Intune license to the user:1. Go to the Office 365 Admin Center, and then choose Users -> Active Users.2. Select the affected user account, and then choose Product licenses -> Edit.3. Verify that a valid Intune license is assigned to the user.4. Re-enroll the device.

symptom: Attempting to enroll an iOS device fails with the following error: Profile Installation Failed. The new MDM payload does not match the old payload.
rootCause: This can occur if a management profile is already installed on the device.
solution: To resolve this issue, complete the follow:1. Open Settings on the iOS device, go to General -> Device Management.2. Tap the existing management profile, and tap Remove Management.3. Re-enroll the device.

symptom: Attempting to enroll an iOS device fails with the following error: Couldn�t Enroll Device Contact your IT admin for assistance with this issue Error: [NoEnrollmentPolicy]
rootCause: This problem can occur if an Apple MDM push certificate isn't configured in Intune, or the certificate is invalid. You can check the status of your Apple MDM Push certificate (aka the APNs certificate) in the Intune portal under Device Enrollment -> Apple Enrollment -> Apple MDM Push certificate:
solution: To resolve this issue, perform one of the following:If your MDM push certificate is not configured, follow the steps to configure it in Get an Apple MDM push certificate.If your MDM push certificate is invalid, follow the steps to renew it in Renew Apple MDM push certificate.

symptom: Attempting to enroll an iOs device fails with the following error: This service is not supported Error: AccountNotOnboarded
rootCause: This problem can occur if an Apple MDM push certificate isn't configured in Intune, or the certificate is invalid. You can check the status of your Apple MDM Push certificate (aka the APNs certificate) in the Intune portal under Device Enrollment -> Apple Enrollment -> Apple MDM Push certificate:
solution: To resolve this issue, perform one of the following:If your MDM push certificate is not configured, follow the steps to configure it in Get an Apple MDM push certificate.If your MDM push certificate is invalid, follow the steps to renew it in Renew Apple MDM push certificate.

symptom: When you turn on a DEP managed device that is assigned an enrollment profile, the Intune enrollment process isn't initiated.
rootCause: This issue can occur if the Intune enrollment profile was created before the DEP token is uploaded to Intune.
solution: To resolve this problem, complete the following steps:1. Edit the enrollment profile. You can make any change you like; the purpose is to update the modification time of the profile.2. Synchronize DEP-managed devices. To do this, open the Intune portal and go to Device Enrollment -> Apple Enrollment -> Enrollment Program Tokens and choose Sync now. A sync request will then be sent to Apple.

symptom: When turning on a device managed under Apple's Device Enrollment Program (DEP), Apple Business Manager or Apple School Manager (ASM), the setup process gets stuck (appears to hang) after the user's credentials are entered. The login process stops and users are prompted to enter the password again.
rootCause: This can occur if Authenticate with Company Portal instead of Apple Setup Assistant is set to No in the Apple Enrollment Profile, and any of the following are also enabled: use multifactor authentication prompt users who need to change their password when they first sign in prompt users to reset their expired passwords during enrollment
solution: To resolve this issue, set Authenticate with Company Portal instead of Apple Setup Assistant to Yes, or disable all of the following in the Apple enrollment profile: use multifactor authentication prompt users who need to change their password when they first sign in prompt users to reset their expired passwords during enrollment

symptom: Email access issue on iOS 12 targeted by an Intune iOS Email Profile. User impact: iOS 12 users are unable to access email using the native Mail application More info: Users will receive credential prompts when attempting to sign-in. On-Premise Exchange mail box users will see �Re-enter Password� button in the email account settings, but pressing the button will appear to have no effect. User unable to access email, Office 365 / Exchange Online users, users will see �Re-enter password� button in the email account settings, when selected a new modern authentication flow will be presented and user will be requested to accept a grant permission to �iOS Accounts� Scope of impact: Impact is specific to a subset of users who are served through the affected infrastructure that included Scale units: AMSUA0602 , AMSUA0601 , AMSUC0201 , AMSUC0301 , AMSUB0101 , AMSUA0502 , AMSUB0102 , AMSUA0501 , AMSUA0402 , AMSUC0101 , AMSUA0701 Issue Posted to the Office 365 Health Message center as: Intune IT151443 Follow-up: iOS 12 Exchange on-prem users are unable to access email on the native mail app We recently posted IT151443 on your Service Health Dashboard. As described in the advisory, Intune introduced a service regression related to iOS 12 or 12.0.1 and modern authorization. While the regression was fixed, any existing iOS 12 or 12.0.1 user prior to the mitigation and meeting the criteria listed below may be unable to send/receive mail using their native email client or experience a new mail authorization flow. If your end users report problems accessing email, you can take action yourself, or you can contact Intune support and we�ll work to guide you through best option for your environment. How does this affect me? You may be affected if your end users upgrade to or enroll a new device on iOS 12 or iOS 12.0.1 before this incident was mitigated and use Exchange on-prem or Exchange Online with native mail (the iOS mail app). The Outlook app for iOS is not affected by this regression. Specifically: � If you use Exchange on-prem and are prompted to re-enter your password on email account settings, end users have reported the �re-enter password� option appears to not do anything. You�ll need to take action to unblock your users. � If you use Exchange Online/Office 365 Exchange, your end users may be prompted to enter their credentials. This is a one-time prompt. The password flow may simply be through the standard password prompt, or it may send the end user to a browser asking for credentials. Upon entering user name and password, a prompt will follow to approve permissions to "iOS Accounts". Once the user approves the permissions of the iOS account, their native mail will start syncing. Depending on your configuration, the end user may also be asked for MFA, or other authentication options. While you don�t need to take any action to unblock users, you may experience helpdesk calls related to the new iOS password flow. What do I need to do? You have three options if you use Exchange on-prem and end users upgraded to or enrolled iOS 12 or 12.0.1 prior to the mitigation of this incident: 1) Contact Intune Support and we'll apply a fix. Note that the fix will automatically remove and then reapply the email profile but will involve minimal end user involvement. 2) You can recreate the affected users email profile which will require end user involvement similar to the first time email setup on an iPhone. The end user will remove the native email account, and add it back in which will require a password, downloading mail, and contacts. Any unsaved drafts on the phone will not be returned to the device. This option will impact all iOS devices. 3) If you have a small number of impacted users and don�t want to touch their email profiles, you can ask your user to unenroll and re-enroll. Again, please contact support through the additional information link if you have any questions or would like post-incident assistance.
rootCause: This issue was caused by Intune deploying email profiles with a new iOS 12 option to support Oauth. Intune set the Oauth value to �false�, however iOS 12 and 12.0.1 devices produced the User Impact regardless. Users targeted with an Email profile on any of the impacted scale units would have had their email profile updated with this setting. Exchange On-Premise does not support Oauth natively, unless the customer has taken the current CU�s for Exchange 2013 or 2016 and configured Modern Authentication (very few customers have enabled this) iOS devices attempted to resolve the Oauth ability of Exchange when the �Re-Enter Password� button is pressed, and it unable to resolve appeared to take no action. Exchange on-line (EXO) is integrated with Azure Active Directory and in most customer environments should support Oauth also know as Modern Auth. With this support, the user sees a new login flow as they complete the modern auth vs legacy auth where they enter the password within the Apple Email profile settings.
solution: For new Email profile deployments, the issue was resolved for all impacted customers. This would include new user devices, un-enroll/re-enroll devices, or user that are added to the email profile assignment groups after the service update overnight on October 17, 2018. For impacted users there are some options available to the customer, first review the decision matrix: Decision Matrix Customer Question Exchange? Answer Offer Caveats Do you have a lot of users affected? On Prem Yes If so, you may want to go with the support team�s fix. All users will get the fix Affected users will automatically have their email profile removed and added back in. Users will have to enter their credentials, but then email and contacts will automatically be added back in without their assistance. Do you have a lot of users affected? EXO Yes If so, you may want to go with the support team�s fix. Affected users will automatically have their email profile removed and added back in, however, they will not need to download a new iOS Auth App and go through that new workflow for modern auth. Do you use the all users group and exclude groups, know who is impacted, and just have a couple of impacted end users you want to monitor? Either Yes If so, you can exclude a group from the email profile, and then the email profile will be removed. Then, after a few hours, remove the exclude group. The fixed email profile will then come down. End users will need to unlock their device while this is happening. Apple syncs every 8 hours, so this could be time consuming. Do you have one user affected who is pretty tech savvy? Either Yes If so, they can either ask the user to enroll/unenroll. Or they can delete the email account from their device and then add it back in. Any drafts not sync�d with Exchange will be deleted. Email and contacts will need to be redownloaded to the device, which may take some time. For customer that want Intune support to assist with the resolution, Intune CSS support will need to get a flighting tag added to the customer tenant after getting the customer agreement to the Caveats. The needed Flighting tag is: OptOutOfOAuth Please follow the process to request this flighting request: 1 � Make sure you share the Caveats with the customer according to their situation and that they approve them in writing via a GA account. 2 � Prepare a task with the following information:Task title: OptOutOfOAuth Flighting Request Tenant ID: <the domain used to login to service (IE intune.onmicrosoft.com)> Context ID: <Company ID FROM VIEWPOINT> AccountID: <GUID FROM VIEWPOINT/GATEWAY TOOL> MSUID: <MSUID FROM VIEWPOINT/GATEWAY TOOL> Authorization from GA: Y/NExample: TenantID: mytest.onmicrosoft.com ContextID: 74537d1e-19e1-4555-bfcb-c6d8bace6725 AccountID: 22abe0ab-8dcf-4058-8cfd-2a03fc07f519 MSU: MSUA05 Authorization from GA: Y/N 3 � Send the task to queue Intune Flighting Request in MSSolve and email the template to intunecssflighting@microsoft.com.

symptom: Log Collection Instructions - Autopilot and Enrollment Status Page(ESP)
rootCause: Customer issues
solution: Below are instructions to collect logs for various scenarios. Note: On S-mode devices, to run command lines for generating logs, press Windows+R to get the Run window, enter the command line and output the logs to your USB key (either D: or E: drive; type "explorer" in the Run window to find out which). Note: On non-S-mode devices, if you're in OOBE, press Shift+F10 to bring up an elevated cmd window.Autopilot:Before RS5, run in an elevated cmd window: licensingdiag.exe RS5+ For canonical Autopilot, run in an elevated cmd window: mdmdiagnosticstool.exe -area Autopilot -cab <pathToOutputCabFile> For plug and forget Autopilot, run in an elevated cmd window: mdmdiagnosticstool.exe -area Autopilot;TPM -cab <pathToOutputCabFile>Runtime provisioningBefore RS5, get the following logs: C:\Windows\System32\winevt\Logs\Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider%4Admin.evtx C:\Windows\System32\winevt\Logs\Microsoft-Windows-Provisioning-Diagnostics-Provider%4Admin.evtx C:\Windows\System32\winevt\Logs\Microsoft-Windows-AAD%4Operational.evtx More generally, depending on the nature of the error, the entire contents of %windir%\system32\winevt\logs can be helpful. RS5+, run in an elevated cmd window: mdmdiagnosticstool.exe -area DeviceProvisioning -cab <pathToOutputCabFile>

symptom: Attempting to enroll a new Hybrid Azure AD AutoPilot device fails despite previous enrollments succeeding. You will also see an event similar to the following logged on the ODJ connector computer:Log Name: ODJ Connector ServiceSource: ODJ Connector Service SourceEvent ID: 30132Level: ErrorDescription:{"Metric":{"Dimensions":{"RequestId":"111111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111","DeviceId":"111111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111","DomainName":"contoso.com","ErrorCode":"5","RetryCount":"1","ErrorDescription":"Failed to get the ODJ Blob. The ODJ connector does not have sufficient privileges to complete the operation","InstanceId":"111111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111","DiagnosticCode":"0x00000800","DiagnosticText":"Failed to get the ODJ Blob. The ODJ connector does not have sufficient privileges to complete the operation [Exception Message: \"DiagnosticException: 0x00000800. Failed to get the ODJ Blob. The ODJ connector does not have sufficient privileges to complete the operation\"] [Exception Message: \"Failed to call NetProvisionComputerAccount machineName=SomeComputerNameHere\"]"},"Name":"RequestOfflineDomainJoinBlob_Failure","Value":0}
rootCause: This issue is commonly caused by incorrect delegate permissions to the OU where Windows AutoPilot devices are to be created. This process is documented in the following article: Enrollment for Microsoft Entra hybrid joined devices - Windows Autopilot | Microsoft Learn
solution: To resolve this issue, follow the directions Enrollment for Microsoft Entra hybrid joined devices - Windows Autopilot | Microsoft Learn to delegate the OU to the computer where the Intune ODJ Connector is installed.
