rootCause: The issue can occur when the application being wrapped is at or near the Android dexfile 64k method limit and the Microsoft Intune Wrapping Tool cannot add the necessary Intune code to the application without exceeding this limit.
solution: To resolve this problem complete the following:1. Install the latest version of the Microsoft Intune App Wrapping Tool for Android. It can be download from this location: https://github.com/msintuneappsdk/intune-app-wrapping-tool-android2. Enable Multidex for your Android application. See the following for details: https://developer.android.com/studio/build/multidexNow test to see if the problem is resolved. If it is not, the following two workarounds are available:1. Examine the Multidex configuration to see if you have marked any classes to keep in the primary DEX file https://developer.android.com/studio/build/multidex#keep If there are too many classes specified, you may hit this issue. Reducing what classes are kept in the primary DEX file may resolve this issue.2. If you are not able to resolve the issue by changing the Multidex configuration, you can also enable Proguard: https://developer.android.com/studio/build/shrink-code#shrink-code This is likely the less desirable option however as some third party libraries may require additional Proguard configuration.

symptom: Realtime Protection is disabled (set to Off and grayed out)Error: Windows could not start the Windows Defender Antivirus Service on Local ComputerError 577:Windows cannot verify the digital signature for this file
rootCause: Defender not re-enabled properly
solution: Run Regedit.exe and navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Defender. Ensure that DisableAntiSpyware and DisableAntiVirus are set to Zero If there is security permission errors trying to change registry entries ,then try the following:On the left panel right-click Windows Defender, select Permissions� => Advanced.Next to Owner click Change. Type your Windows username, click OK.Click Add => Select a principal. Type your username, click OK.Put ticks in Full Control and Read checkboxes. Click OK in every remaining windowIf the registry entries are already set to Zero then run sfc /scannowPress Windows key + X key.Click Command Prompt (Admin).Type sfc /scannow and hit Enter.Wait until the process completes.If some defective files have been detected but could not be restored, type dism /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth and press Enter (this step requires an active Internet connection).Wait for the procedure to finish and repeat step 3.Exit Command prompt, reboot the PC.Restart the Security Center ServicePress Windows + R.Type services.msc, then press Enter.Search for Security Center service.Right click on it and select Restart.

symptom: Customer has set up a new Intune tenant for use with either Intune MDM or 3rd party MDM like Mobile Iron, customer attempts to open the Intune blade and further the App Protection blade to utilize the App Protection policies and finds that they get a "Access denied" for the Intune Overview blade and on the Intune App Protection blade they get "Bad Request, Request not applicable to Target Tenant."Example 1:Example 2:
rootCause: It seems that someone, perhaps their admin, has disabled the Intune AAD App. Verify this with screen shots from the Customer and also using the Kusto Query: AccountService_Account | where ContextId has "1dd5fe29-3c63-46da-8db1-b10452fdebbc" | project ScaleUnitName, AccountId, IsPaid, TotalLicenses, CreatedDate, Blocked, AccountState, NameExample, ebonhold.onmicrosoft.com what it should look like:If the AccountState is showing 0 - which means the Enterprise Application under AAD for Intune is disabled:This will prevent the complete on-boarding of the Intune tenant.You can also see this in Rave, when you open the tenant and then go to Applications->Intune->Data you will see all blank entries instead of the expected completed.Example of bad:Example of good:
solution: To resolve this is easy, the customer just needs to do the following steps with a GA account.Open portal.azure.comNavigate to Azure Active Directory->Enterprise Applications->Intune->PropertiesSwitch setting "Enabled for users to sign-in?" from "No" to "Yes"Click SaveThe customer will need to wait from 1 hour to up to 24 hours for the tenant to re-onboard and complete activation.Then they can validate if they can now access the blades, note that if they are using a 3rd party MDM they should only have the expectation of accessing the App Protection Blade in Intune and that not all functions will be available for the rest of Intune.

symptom: When creating an App Configuration Policy for iOS and in Configuration Settings > for Designer, you are inputting your SSO URL with an ampersand (&) as the configuration value with with the String Value Type. You are getting an error that states �which has invalid characters XML format doesn�t support. Please correct it accordingly� when saving. The issue has since been resolved by modifying the URL but would like to investigate if this can be allowed in future releases of Intune.Steps that would be taken by customer to generate this scenario:Opened IntuneCreated an App Config PolSelected Microsoft Auth since do not have their app.Entered the Configuration settings via designer as Cx did.Entered Configuration Key = ssoAuthEndpoint | Entered Value Type = String | Entered Configuration Value = https://sso.companyid.com/idpov/accesslogin/FedSSODispatch.faces?PartnerIdpId=https://sts.windows.net/74ea519d-9792-4aa9-86d9-b7cab8abcddd/&TargetResource=https://sso.companyid.com/idprov/pages/home/dispatch.jsp?SpName=MBF Clicked OK and then Add Received Error as result of these steps and scenario: Mismatch between the value type and the configuration value in the configuration settings designerThe configuration settings designer has a value type as String but a configuration value as "https://sso.companyid.com/idpov/accesslogin/FedSSODispatch.faces?PartnerIdpId=https://sts.windows.net/74ea519d-9792-4aa9-86d9-b7cab8abcddd/&TargetResource=https://sso.companyid.com/idprov/pages/home/dispatch.jsp?SpName=MBF ", which has invalid characters XML format doesn't support. Please correct it accordingly.Example 1:
rootCause: The limitation in the UI is due to the limitations of Apple aka their apps\xml do not support '&' characters on the xml. If you use '&' when the devices check in, it will error out because of not being able to identify the value as such we also do not support the value in the UI either.
solution: There are two options for solution for this to align with Apple's requirements:Solution 1: Use the short URL, example https://bit.ly/SOneCGA Solution 2: Use �&� instead of �&� in the URL, example: https://sso.companyid.com/idpov/accesslogin/FedSSODispatch.faces?PartnerIdpId=https://sts.windows.net/74ea519d-9792-4aa9-86d9-b7cab8abcddd/&TargetResource=https://sso.companyid.com/idprov/pages/home/dispatch.jsp?SpName=MBF Example of result when using either of the solutions:

symptom: During Hybrid Azure AD Join AutoPilot the device times out during the initial OOBE screen. It will present a "Something went wrong." with error code 80070774.You should also see that the device continuously fails to Ping the DC or Resolve the Domain name, as seen in the client's Event Viewer logs by checking: 1. Shift + F102. Type .\eventvwr.exe3. Navigate to Applications and Services -> Microsoft -> Windows -> Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/Admin4. Review Event ID 105 & 106
rootCause: The "Assign User" feature should only be used with Azure AD Join AutoPilot scenarios, and not with Hybrid Azure AD Join AutoPilot. The "Assign User" feature performs an AAD Join on the device during the initial sign-in screen, which puts the device in a state where it cannot domain join as part of the AutoPilot Hybrid Join experience.
solution: 1. Navigate to the Intune -> Device Enrollment -> Windows Enrollment -> Devices section. 2. Select the Ellipsis (�) to the right of the device(s) experiencing the issue.3. Choose "Unassign User" and verify the process completes.4. Ensure the Hybrid Azure AD AutoPilot profile is assigned before re-attempting OOBE.

symptom: When wrapping a LOB application using Microsoft Intune App Wrapping Tool for Android, the tool crashes with org.jf.util.ExceptionWithContext: Unsigned short value out of range: 65536
rootCause: The application being wrapped is at or near the Android dexfile 64k method limit and the Intune Wrapping Tool cannot add necessary code without exceeding this limit.
solution: 1. Install latest Intune App Wrapping Tool for Android. 2. Enable Multidex. If unresolved: reduce classes in primary DEX file or enable Proguard.

symptom: New Intune tenant: Intune blade shows Access denied, App Protection blade shows Bad Request, Request not applicable to Target Tenant.
rootCause: Intune AAD Enterprise Application was disabled. AccountState=0 prevents complete on-boarding.
solution: Azure Portal > AAD > Enterprise Applications > Intune > Properties > Enable for users to sign-in = Yes. Wait 1-24 hours for re-onboard.

symptom: Creating iOS App Configuration Policy with SSO URL containing ampersand (&) gives error: invalid characters XML format does not support.
rootCause: Apple limitation: iOS apps/xml do not support & characters in configuration values.
solution: Use short URL (e.g. bit.ly) or use & instead of & in the URL.

symptom: Hybrid Azure AD Join AutoPilot: device times out during OOBE with error 80070774. Device fails to Ping DC or Resolve Domain name.
rootCause: Assign User feature was used, which only works with Azure AD Join AutoPilot, not Hybrid. It performs AAD Join during initial sign-in preventing domain join.
solution: Intune > Device Enrollment > Windows Enrollment > Devices > Unassign User. Re-assign Hybrid Azure AD AutoPilot profile and retry OOBE.

symptom: Error 123 occurs when running application ABC. You may also notice that the customer does not possess the Continuum Transfunctioner, a device whose mystery is only exceeded by it's power.
rootCause: This can occur if you do not possess the Continuum Transfunctioner.
solution: Acquire the Continuum Transfunctioner, then try running the application again.

symptom: Customers are unable to enroll specific Huawei models that are running Android 8. 1. Download Company Portal from Google Play Store;2. Start the enrollment process;3. Observe behavior: after the "Configuring your Work Profile" window is presented, the screen goes black and no other information is provided(Android status bar and navigation buttons are available). Work Profile is created but not activated(not visible within the all apps menu);
rootCause: We have an internal thread with Huawei and they informed us that this is a KNOW ISSUE and they are working on deploying a fix around mid-November. I don�t think we are going to post anything public about it as the issue is caused directly by the Android flavor that Huawei is running on their devices, thus if you need any official statement about it, you would need to get in contact with Huawei.
solution: The below workaround was tested and confirmed for the following device models:Huawei P20Huawei P SmartHuawei Honor 9 1. Install Company Portal from the Google Play store;2. Navigate to device Settings -> Battery -> Launch -> disable launch option for Company Portal app -> if it prompts for a pop-up, do not change anything there, just tap �OK� 3. Once the launch option for Company Portal is disabled, proceed and start the normal enrollment process.

symptom: If an administrator removes or deletes a user from Azure Active Directory (AAD), and that user also has a device enrolled in Intune, the information in Intune for that user will no longer be available. If you view the enrolled device in the Intune portal under Devices -> All Devices, it will show the User Principal Name (UPN) as None.
solution: A script has been made available that will remove a device managed by Intune as long as you have the UPN of the active or deleted user. A script to remove the orphaned device in this scenario (RemoveIntuneDevice.ps1) can be downloaded from the following location: https://github.com/patlewis-MSFT/RemoveIntuneDevice.

symptom: Pre-Requisites Note: These are current requirements for internal testing and may not align with release requirements. When public documentation is released this will be updated. Client Win 10 1809+ clients Must have line of sight to Domain Controller Servers ODJ Server using Windows Server 2016+ Domain Controller Azure AD Automatic MDM Enrollment Endpoints enabled Dynamic Groups for targeting AutoPilot devices. Azure AD Premium licensingIntune AutoPilot profile for Hybrid AD Join targeting the device Configuration Profile for Domain Join targeting the device AutoPilot Enrollment Public Info: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/enrollment-autopilotAutoPilot General Troubleshooting (Internal KB): https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4134173 Overview of Hybrid Azure AD AutoPilot join 1. Initial start of device in OOBE reaches out to device discovery service to see if it is targeted by AutoPilot. Ensure connectivity to the internet and that the AutoPilot profiles are targeted properly. 2. After receiving AutoPilot profile the device discovery service (DDS) will create an AAD record. 3. User signs into OOBE as part of AutoPilot login flow. AAD should now have a record of the device corresponding to the user. 4. MDM-Only enrollment into Intune occurs. Device should now be visible in Intune under All Devices. 5. OMA-DM protocols begin being configured on the device. This includes the Domain Join profile created in Intune. Device should now change its name to format specified in the Domain Join profile. a. Device will begin attempting to Ping DC and reporting results in Event Viewer. EventID 106 corresponds to success and failure events for this process. Location: Applications and Services Logs->Microsoft->Windows->Shell-Core->Operational b. Successful Event (and additional errors) may also appear in: Applications and Services -> Microsoft -> Windows -> Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/Admin c. ODJ Connector should now have activity to download blob under Event ID: 301206. Device should now reboot and become a Domain Joined device. Extremely limited troubleshooting available if an issue occurs here. 7. Enrollment Status Page should appear if configured. 8. AD Login page appears. 9. Device re-enters Enrollment Status Page before user can access desktop. This step may take a long time (up to 30 minutes). Device is waiting to be synced from AD to Azure AD Connect and performing Hybrid Azure AD Join.a. Device must be synced to Azure. If placed in a non-syncing OU it will never get passed this stage. b. Manually syncing AAD Connect can help speed this step up when testing or troubleshooting. c. General Azure AD Connect sync troubleshooting applies. You should be able to find the device in the Metaverse. 10. Desktop / Successful join. Common TroubleshootingCLIENT LOG COLLECTION: To collect Event Viewer Logs from client PC during OOBE 1) Press SHift+F102) Type .\eventvwr.exe3) Depending on issue Navigate to... a) Applications and Services -> Microsoft -> Windows -> Microsoft-Windows-DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider/Admin b) Applications and Services Logs->Microsoft->Windows->Shell-Core->Operational4) Review logs for issues/errors //Finding Offline Domain Join messages DeviceManagementProvider| where deviceId == "<DEVICE ID>"| where env_time >= ago(1d)| project env_time, userId, name, policyId, typeAndCategory, applicablilityState, reportComplianceState, message, EventMessage, ['id']| where message contains"Domain"| take 1000 //Intune AutoPilot Profile Assignment statusCorporateDeviceDataService_ZTDDevice | where AccountId == "d08849af-7555-40d5-a1d7-3b528671000a"//| where ZTDSerialNumber == ""//| where IntuneDeviceId == ""//| where ZTDRegistrationId == "" | take 100ODJ Connector Log Collection: 1) Open Event Viewer. Right-click start menu -> Event Viewer 2) Navigate to Applications and Services -> ODJ Connector 3) Filter Event IDs as needed (although here is a filter to start with -30121,-30150). ID 30120 indicates the connector successfully received a BLOB download request.For Engineering assistance via ICM, please gather: What SG groups are targeted by the Domain Join profile and the Autopilot profile? When resetting a device, what steps are the customers taking? (Is the customer deleting the AAD record, Intune record but not the AP recorded, etc. They should either delete everything or nothing for consistency) Device ids/timestamps for attemtps that resulted in failures? Device logs from devices after failures? Known Issues Intune: Hybrid Join AutoPilot - "Failed to call NetProvisionComputerAccount" error: https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4469339Intune: Hybrid Azure AD Join AutoPilot - AutoPilot's "Assigned User" feature causes Hybrid Azure AD Join AutoPilot to fail: https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4469771

After applying a Configuration Policy that restricts what apps are hidden from the user, the user device fails to apply many of the configuration policy settings.When viewing the device in the Intune Portal, you will see that the device configuration policy settings are failing with error"-2016334108 (2004:Unsupported field value)"Additionally, the following Kusto queries show the following error in the message field...iOSPlugin[HandleCommandError]: The device has sent a Error error for Install profile: www.windowsintune.com.restrictions.payload command (). Reported errors: ; ErrorDomain:<MCProfileErrorDomain>, ErrorCode:<1000>, USEnglishDescription:<The profile �Restrictions Profile� is invalid.>, LocalizedDescription:<The profile �Restrictions Profile� is invalid.> ; ErrorDomain:<MCPayloadErrorDomain>, ErrorCode:<2000>, USEnglishDescription:<The payload �Restrictions Profile� is invalid.>, LocalizedDescription:<The payload �Restrictions Profile� is invalid.> ; ErrorDomain:<MCPayloadErrorDomain>, ErrorCode:<2004>, USEnglishDescription:<The field �blacklistedAppBundleIDs� contains the invalid value �com.apple.mobilephone�.>, LocalizedDescription:<The field �blacklistedAppBundleIDs� contains the invalid value �com.apple.mobilephone�.>Kusto Instructions:First find the an ActivityId that results in an error. You will use this in the next query. .csl-table { color:DarkOrchid; } .csl-column { color:mediumvioletred; } .csl-calculated-column { color:Purple; } .csl-operator { color:red; } .csl-string-literal { color:firebrick; } .csl-function { color:blue; } .csl-command { color:blue; } .csl-suboperator { color:blue; } .csl-comment { color:green; } .csl-client-directive { color:#9400d3; } .csl-let-variable { color:purple; } .csl-query-parameter { color:#2b91af; } .csl-plugin { color:blue; } DeviceManagementProvider | where SourceNamespace == "IntunePE" | where env_time > ago(7d) | where tenantId == "insert-validTenant-ID" | where name contains "Name of the Config Policy Here" | where reportComplianceState == "Error" | distinct ActivityId Then query for one of the erroring ActivityId's. .csl-table { color:DarkOrchid; } .csl-column { color:mediumvioletred; } .csl-calculated-column { color:Purple; } .csl-operator { color:red; } .csl-string-literal { color:firebrick; } .csl-function { color:blue; } .csl-command { color:blue; } .csl-suboperator { color:blue; } .csl-comment { color:green; } .csl-client-directive { color:#9400d3; } .csl-let-variable { color:purple; } .csl-query-parameter { color:#2b91af; } .csl-plugin { color:blue; } DeviceManagementProvider | where env_time > ago(10d) | where ActivityId == "Insert-Valid-Intune-DeviceID" | where message contains "blacklistedAppBundleIDs" | project env_time, deviceId, accountId, ActivityId, message
rootCause: There is a policy that is configured to hide the phone app, com.apple.mobilephone.This occurs because the phone app cannot be hidden on an iPhone. We confirmed this by using Apple Configurator to try to hide the phone app. Hiding the phone app or Setting app throws an error in Apple Configurator as well and the setting is not applied.Since hiding apps is a "Supervised Only" feature, only corporate owned devices (DEP) will hit this issue.
solution: Do not apply a configuration policy that is configured to hide the phone app and Setting app

Hybrid AADJ Windows 10 device will not auto-enroll with the Intune service.The GPO for auto-mdm enrollment is being deployed and applied to the computer (confirm by running gpresult in cmd prompt), however, the scheduled task which is typically generated for the auto-mdm enrollment is not created and not displayed in the task scheduler app under-> Task Scheduler Library, open Microsoft > Windows , then click EnterpriseMgmt.Additionally, when running gpupdate /force via command prompt so that the group policies can be reapplied, the following error is encountered:Windows failed to apply the MDM Policy settings. MDM Policy settings might have its own log file. Please click on the "More information" link.
rootCause: In this specific case, the computer in question had the ConfigMgrEnrollment registry key under \HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments. This was causing the computer to appear as already enrolled (even though the CM client wasn't installed) and also causing the task to not execute.
solution: Backed up the registry and deleted the enrollment key (under "\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments") which appears to have been "flagging" the computer as already MDM enrolled; then allowed time for the GPO for auto-mdm enrollment to reapply. Thanks to Tim (TIMCCU) for sharing the below command.To quickly identify the GUID for the key that should be deleted, run the following in PowerShell:Get-ChildItem HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Enrollments | ForEach-Object {Get-ItemProperty $_.pspath} | where-object {$_.DiscoveryServiceFullURL} | Foreach-Object {$_.PSChildName}

symptom: Customer used to be able to see Compliance Policies they created in Silverlight, while they knew they could not change them; they still used for reference. However now they can no longer see them in Silverlight or Ibiza portal and need them still for reference as they create new policies in the Ibiza portal.
rootCause: [NOTICE - Information detail from Rave should not be shared with customers directly, only the dumped Compliance Policies as a result of this process.]In July of 2018 we started communicating with customers that they will need to start re-creating their Compliance Policies in the Ibiza portal and to delete their old Silverlight Compliance Policies. However as of August 2018 there is still showing over 3k customers whom still had Compliance Policies in Silverlight. As a result to move progression forward, the Servicing Side Engineering team set forth the plan:First making the compliance policies read-only (Completed)Dumping all of the compliance policies into a repository for back up for each tenant (Completed)Removing the compliance policies from view in the Silverlight portal (Completed)Customers who now go back to the Silverlight portal to see the Compliance Policies for reference to attempt to document them and re-create them in the Ibiza portal will find they are no longer visible. This has the effect of the following for the customer that can result in service request being opened:Customer still needs for referenceCustomer is concerned as they were still deployed
solution: First and foremost, this is expected behavior and design, it was confirmed with the IcM on https://icm.ad.msft.net/imp/v3/incidents/details/89430833/home on 10.31.18;You will first notice when looking in Rave that you can see the Silverlight Compliance Policies showing with the Authority for the policies as Silverlight. To see these with ease do the following:Open RaveSearch the tenant for the customerNavigate to Applications->Intune->TroubleshootingSelect Policy from the drop down, then filter for Silverlight or the AuthorityFilter Example:Result Example:The Unknown policies are where you will see the Compliance policies at.Quick note on the other policy types "Device Configuration, Profiles have been migrated to Ibiza and while the Authority still shows as Silverlight (why they would show up here) they can be seen, accessed, edited, deleted, ect from the Ibiza portal."Per the wiki and CxE, the Compliance policies were the ones that needed re-created and manual bring over to the Ibiza portal. As a result they are the ones we are most likely to get cases on. So, what do we do for our customer?First we assure them that any Silverlight created Compliance Policy will be trumped\overruled by Ibiza portal policies; which should be in existence for any platform they support.Second if the customer is concerned that the Compliance Policies will cause impact if they are showing as still assigned, they can simply remove the old security group from AD\AAD which will un-assign the policy and create a new one for the users.Third if they are still wanting the Compliance Policies for reference or documentation, we can help them with this by providing them a dump of the policies.Example of a policy in Rave showing as still assigned:So, how do we do a dump of the policies? Answer is easy, CxE has already done this and per the article; you can go here to this repository: https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/teams/IntuneCRI/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?viewid=cbc0ddfc%2Da2c7%2D4e84%2D8c2a%2D5fb192c8b25f&id=%2Fteams%2FIntuneCRI%2FShared%20Documents%2FSL%20Comp%20Policy%20DumpOnce here, you can search for the contextID of the tenant, recommended you only use the first portion of the contextID in the search.Search example:Once found you can select each of the policies, then click download. This will produce a zip file.Example of download: Quick note, its best to only download the files that look like txt documents as the rest are really noise.What you will find inside the zip file produced and the txt style files is a layout as such the customer can use as reference to re-create the policy in Ibiza.Example:Once you hand these off to the customer, they should be all set and you should be good to answer any questions and close out the case.

symptom: ConfigMgr Hybrid iOS DEP enrolled devices blocked by CA for Exchange on-prem. If a customer has the following scenario: Hybrid ConfigMgr + Intune Exchange on premise Conditional access for Exchange on premise DEP enrolled iOS devicesThey will see issues with the DEP devices getting access to Exchange on premise mailboxes because the EAS ID does not match between what is in Exchange and ConfigMgr.
rootCause: The EAS ID in ConfigMgr does not match the EAS ID in Exchange.
solution: Please escalate to IET and ask for the tenant to be tagged with the DeviceMergeForBulkEnrollment flighting tag

symptom: Users are unable to save contacts from the managed Outlook app for iOS to the local contacts app even though Apple states that this is possible here: https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT208749:Changes in iOS 12In iOS 12, you can use MDM to make the following exceptions to this policy:� Allow unmanaged apps to access managed contacts� Allow managed apps to save contacts to the local Contacts app
rootCause: This can occur if there is an iOS device restriction policy that blocks the setting "Viewing corporate documents in unmanaged apps". Although Apple states in the article above that this is now possible in iOS 12, they have confirmed this to be a known issue in iOS 12.x.
solution: Microsoft is working with Apple on this issue. In the meantime, the current recommendation would be to disable the restriction to allow Managed apps to access unmanaged apps, or have the users install Outlook Mobile from the store so it�s not a managed app.

symptom: Use this query to find all MTD messages for a given account. You can also optionally filter by a Device's ReferenceID (AADDeviceID) to narrow the query further. IntuneEvent | where env_time > ago(7d) // your timeframe | where ComponentName == "PartnerAPI" | where * contains "f3f068cf-080c-4824-a912-f8c4633bd454" // your customer's ContextId | where Col3 contains "Received data upload message" | where Col3 contains "1e16eccf-07be-4c19-9693-905c34c2198b" // optional, this is the device AAD ID / ReferenceID | project env_time, Col3 The resulting data will look something like this: Received data upload message [{"EntityType":1,"TenantId":"f3f068cf-080c-4824-a912-f8c4633bd454","DeviceId":"3ff8d174-7af1-4ec5-9055-894d35f9d485","State":1,"LastUpdateTime":"2018-10-24T21:46:37.491Z"}] at time 10/24/2018 9:47:08 PM Only messages beginning with "EntityType:1" are relevant for compliance calculation / CA troubleshooting. The "State" of the device must be of a certain value (matching the admin policy intent) to be compliant. State values are as follows: Device.State 0 - Unknown - Indicates device is not yet registered with MTD Partner. Any configured MTD policy will treat this as noncompliant, since this state does not indicate a security posture 1 - Activated - Indicates device registration with MTD Partner. Any configured MTD policy will treat this as noncompliant, since this state does not indicate a security posture. 2 - Deactivated- Indicates device deactivation in MTD Partner, either due to deletion or absence of device in AAD. Any configured MTD policy will treat this as noncompliant, since this state does not indicate a security posture. 3 - Secured- Indicates 0 active threats (though threats might be resolved or ignored by admin). Any configured MTD policy will treat this as compliant, since this state is the most secure option possible. 4 - Low_Severity- Indicates max threat on the device is of level "low". Configured Policies set to "Low", "Medium" or "High" will allow devices of this state to be compliant. 5 - Medium_Severity- Indicates max threat on the device is of level "medium". Configured Policies set to "Medium" or "High" will allow devices of this state to be compliant. 6 - High_Severity- Indicates max threat on the device is of level "high". Configured Policies set to "High" will allow devices of this state to be compliant. 7 - Unresponsive- Indicates device is no longer actively communicating with MTD Partner. Any configured MTD policy will treat this as noncompliant, since this state does not indicate a security posture. 8 - Compromised- Indicates device is considered compromised by MTD Partner. Any configured MTD policy will treat this as noncompliant, since this the threat state of the device cannot be determined. 9 - Misconfigured- Indicates device is considered Misconfigured by MTD Partner. Any configured MTD policy will treat this as noncompliant, since this the threat state of the device cannot be determined. When triaging MTD issues, if the latest (by LastUpdateTime in Col3, not env_time) message contains a state, the behavior above is considered "by design" and is likely not an Intune issue. If the behavior observed does not match the states above for a given device or account, it should be escalated to the MTD team (mightytm@microsoft.com / Conditional Access And Exchange Connector, BenYim) via ICM for investigation.

You created a compliance policy for Windows 10 and checked the option to Require Firewall in the Device Security section. Some Windows 10 devices that are targeted by this policy are incorrectly reporting as incompliant even though their firewall is enabled.
rootCause: This behavior was investigated under ICM 79613269 and Emerging Issue 66. It was found that the devices are being marked as noncompliant due to a result being returned from the Windows device when a MDM check-in occurs.
solution: The issue has been fixed with an update to the Windows OS. Please advise customer to install below update to resolve the issue: RS5 or 1809: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4469342/november292018kb4469342osbuild17763167 RS4 or 1803: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4467682/windows-10-update-kb4467682RS3 or 1709: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4467681/windows-10-update-kb4467681: If the customer is unable to install the fix, educate them about the known workarounds to bridge over the issue: User Action when blocked by Conditional Access: Organizations should educate users that are blocked by Conditional Access due to a device being noncompliant for Windows Firewall, to trigger an MDM Sync of their device, once completed, they should re-check compliance on the Intune IW Portal (https://portal.manage.microsoft.com). After becoming compliant, re-attempt access to the blocked resource. Methods to trigger the sync are covered in the Intune User Help Documentation. Intune Configuration changes to avoid user impact: Option 1: (Preferred) � Open your Windows device compliance Policy, Properties > Actions for noncompliance > Select the default action �Mark device noncompliant� > change to Schedule (days after noncompliance) value from 0 to � More information on this feature is available here � Effect: Windows devices will no longer be marked as noncompliant immediately after reporting a less than healthy state for Firewall or any other setting of you compliance policy, instead the noncompliant state would be triggered after the number of days set in the Action. Option 2: � Change your Windows Compliance policy setting under, System Security > Device Security � Firewall to �Not Configured� � Effect: Windows devices firewall status will not be considered for device compliance status

symptom: After enrolling an Android Enterprise device (aka Android for Work or AfW), users see 2 versions of the Company Portal application:1. The regular Company Portal app without the work badge. When run it displays You are halfway there..2. The Company Portal app with the work badge. When run it shows device details, compliance, etc.This happens only on Android devices enrolled with a work profile and running Android 8.0 or later. The expected behavior is that once the Work Profile is activated, the Company Portal app in the personal profile (the first instance above) is deactivated or hidden.
rootCause: This is a known problem with the current version of the Company Portal app prior to December 2018
solution: Resolved with December 2018 release of Intune Company Portal

symptom: Customers are unable to save configuration policies after they added a scope tag when using the Silverlight portal (aka the classic portal). They will encounter the following error: Failed to save profile, try again later
rootCause: This behavior is by design when using the Silverlight portal as ScopeTags are not supported for policies created in Silverlight portal. You can confirm if the policy is created in Silverlight or Ibiza by checking the policy authority in Rave. To find more details about the error, you can search in Kusto in MSGRAPHKUS database. If you don't have this database, add it by using following connection source: https://msgraphkus.kusto.windows.net:443 AggregatorServiceLogEvent | where env_time between (datetime(2018-10-31)..datetime(2018-11-02)) //enter you timestamp | where tenantId == "05d75c05-fa1a-42e7-9cf1-eb416c396f2d" //context id from Rave | where internalCorrelationId == "27123621-581a-40f6-9c33-482a84c4d2e2" //request/error ID from Azure portal | project env_time,message, targetUri , targetWorkloadId, requestMethod, responseStatusCodeAnother way, would be to start F12(browser developer mode) right before clicking save button. This will open a new window that will log what the console is doing.After you click save, in the network tab(developer mode window) you should see a PATCH call to deviceManagement/deviceConfigurations('idOfthePolicy') with a 400 response. The details of the error should contain same error.
solution: As this behavior is considered by design we are not able to provide a direct fix for the customer. If this behavior happens, customer should be advised to recreate same policies in the Ibiza portal in order to assign scope tags to them.

symptom: Starting with the update planned for Summer 2019, Office 365 Mobile Device Management (MDM) uses the same backend infrastructure as Azure Intune policies. See KB 4471201This is more likely to impact customers who have O365 MDM Coexistence, See KB 3094492, however there is no technical limitation preventing O365 MDM customers who do not have EMS or Intune licenses from using the Azure portal if it provides needed functionality.While the Azure portal UI does not yet make this distinction clear, the differences documented at https://support.office.com/en-us/article/choose-between-mdm-for-office-365-and-microsoft-intune-c93d9ab9-efb2-4349-9b93-30c30562ee22 are still applicable.The purpose of this article is to list out what Device Configuration Policies are known to work with Office 365 MDM enrolled devices.

If you are an existing Intune customer, you may already be enforcing acceptance of your company terms using Intune Terms and Conditions feature. Azure AD has built their Terms of use feature to enable enforcement of company terms across Intune and other Microsoft services.

On iPhone/iPad running iOS 12.1 with Teams, OneDrive, Word, Excel, PowerPoint installed and Intune app protection policy applied, attempting to open an Office file from the file repository of any Teams channel fails. Pressing Cancel and retrying opens it successfully.

When attempting to co-manage devices that are Hybrid AD Joined and Managed with the Configuration Manager Client, MDM Enrollment fails with error 0xcaa9001f. Manual MDM enrollments of Hybrid AD Joined devices succeed. CoManagementHandler.log shows MDM enrollment failed with error code 0xcaa9001f. AAD Event Viewer shows OAuth response error invalid_grant AADSTS50155 Device is not authenticated.
rootCause: The error occurs when Integrated Windows Authentication is attempted by the Configuration Manager client against Azure AD while the verified domain is not Federated. The CM environment is either missing Azure Services (Cloud Management) or the users are not appropriately scoped for both AD and Azure AD User Discovery.
solution: Set up Azure Services (Cloud Management) in Configuration Manager and ensure users are scoped for both AD User Discovery and Azure AD User Discovery.

symptom: Guide: Using Kusto to troubleshoot Enrollment Status Page (ESP) issues. Covers Kusto queries for DeviceLifecycle, IntuneEvent, DeviceManagementProvider to diagnose ESP settings delivery, app/policy tracking, and installation failures during enrollment.

Training: Prepare your organization for Android enterprise management with Microsoft Intune. Covers Android Enterprise (formerly Android for Work) for BYOD and corp-owned scenarios, management, provisioning, and app deployment capabilities.

When deploying custom VPN profile to iOS devices, it fails with Error -2016341110 (iOS device has rejected the command due to incorrect format).
rootCause: There is a formatting error in the XML.
solution: Load the file in Apple Configurator to verify if it is formatted correctly or check XML for any formatting error manually.

symptom: While migrating from Hybrid to Intune Standalone, when user authority is changed from SCCM to Intune, any policy untargeted does not get removed from the device for 7 days. Tattoo removal is suspended for a grace period of 7 days after migration.

A Windows 10 WiFi MAC address may be incorrectly inventoried in Intune in the device hardware tab. When a device has multiple WiFi MAC address (including virtual NICs) then the inventory will only ever insert one of these MAC address into the device inventory. Connected NICs are preferred over non connected; if one or more connected NICs are returned the first one is used; otherwise the first non-connected card is returned.

One Data Collector is a Support Diagnostics Platform (SDP) package that provides agents and engineers with high-quality, fully-tested and compliant data collector sets. It is recommended that Intune Support Engineers use this tool to collect data for any Windows support incident where data is needed from an individual client PC.

In hybrid environments, Intune users may lose their device mappings in the System Center Configuration Manager console. This loss of association prevents policies, app deployments and other Intune content from being delivered to the client.
rootCause: There are numerous causes of clients losing device mappings. The most common is simply that the device is not checking in for extended periods of time. This issue also can occur if the ConfigMgr database is directly edited and user attributes are changed out-of-band.
solution: If only a limited number of devices are affected, manually remove Intune management on the devices and re-enroll them. If large numbers of clients are affected, use the SQL script to alter the MDMClientIdentity table in the SCCM DB to cause the Intune gateway to resend new RDR down to ConfigMgr. UPDATE MDMClientIdentity SET ClientState = 5 WHERE DeviceID in (deviceID).

symptom: Deployed configuration with Autonomous Single App Mode (ASAM) is not locking down the targeted app when launched.
rootCause: Application does not support the Autonomous Single App Mode feature.
solution: App must use UIAccessibilityRequestGuidedAccessSession() call. Known supported apps: TestNav, SafeExamBrowser. Customer should reach out to app developer to confirm ASAM support. ASAM is different from SAM (Single App Mode/Kiosk mode). Requirements: https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT204775

symptom: When navigating to Users or Groups in the Microsoft 365 Device Management dashboard via devicemanagement.microsoft.com, error: Unable to complete due to service connection error. The same Users and Groups work fine in portal.azure.com.
rootCause: Entry point issue with devicemanagement.microsoft.com. Has been resolved.
solution: Use the URL https://devicemanagement.portal.azure.com as an alternative entry point. Both URLs are valid entry points to the same portal.

symptom: When trying to change the Device Enrollment Limit in the Azure portal for a CM Hybrid tenant, error: An error occurred.
rootCause: Tenant authority is set to SCCM. CM Hybrid customers are expected to change device settings in the CM console.
solution: Navigate to Administration > Cloud Services > Microsoft Intune Subscription in the CM console. Double-click the subscription to change the Device Enrollment Limit.

symptom: Guide: Kusto queries for troubleshooting Android Enterprise COBO devices - retrieving latest AM API messages, device ID lookup by serial number, and latest device notifications.

When migrating from SCCM Hybrid to Intune Standalone, customers using Apple DEP may have questions about DEP token and device migration. DEP is a tenant level setting and requires authority switch. Three background tasks (token/device/profile migration) run every 6 hours. Wait 24 hours before switching authority. Pending reset state is expected if new enrollment profile is assigned.
Selective wipes initiated with Basic Mobility and Security is not removing cached emails from Outlook or cached files from OneDrive for Business on iOS/Android managed devices.
rootCause: Outlook and OneDrive apps only remove cached data when the app has been deployed by MDM or managed by MAM. Basic Mobility and Security does not have application deployment features so it cannot remove data during wipe.
solution: By-design on both Android and iOS. For applying Intune App protection policies, an Intune license is required. M365 app data will get wiped if the device is protected by Intune App Protection policies.

symptom: Guide: How to display Kusto query history - retrieve previously run queries that were not saved.
Guidance for throttling app deployment bandwidth when downloading apps in Intune.
Guide: Troubleshooting Intune enrollment using various Kusto queries, originally from intunewiki.
When querying for devices based on Corporate ownership, the value Corporate no longer shows the corporate owned devices.
rootCause: The Corporate value is no longer used for the deviceOwnership attribute.
solution: To work around this issue, replace Corporate with Company to query for corporate owned devices. For example:device.deviceOwnership -contains "Company"Note that you may need an additional query to limit your results to specific OS types (e.g. Windows, Android, IPhone, etc.)device.deviceOSType -contains "WindowsA request to update the public docs has been submitted: https://github.com/MicrosoftDocs/azure-docs/issues/18081

symptom: Windows 10 Device Configuration Profile->Settings->Networkboundary allows you for Boundary Type of IPV4 to accept format of IP Range ###.###.###.###-###.###.###.### and to save successfully but then after reverts to forced format of ###.###.###.###/### resulting in 0.0.0.0./0 in the profile.
rootCause: When IPv4 format of "###.###.###.###-###.###.###.###" is used the UI should not allow and prevent with error message; however, due to a code flaw it is allowing it. Thus is resulting in the revert back to the IPv4 format of "###.###.###.###/###" that is all 0's. Per our current article of support, as well, https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/endpoint-protection-windows-10#network-settings shows only the format of "###.###.###.###/###"; should be used.
solution: Issue resolved with Intune 1218 release completed by end of December 2018.

symptom: When attempting to wrap a .pkg file using the Intune App Wrapping Tool for Mac, the following error is received: "Unsupported package type. Only pkg file can be supported." ":SetPackageInfo failed with 80004005."
rootCause: The packageinfo file was missing from the .pkg package.
solution: The IntuneAppUtil currently only supports flat packages and the .pkg package must include the packageinfo file.

symptom: Users manually uninstall the Company Portal application from an enrolled Android device, but the Intune deployed certificates are not revoked.

Samsung devices enrolled in Intune via Knox Mobile Enrollment tool may display an ownership type of 'Personal' rather than the expected 'Corporate' setting in the Intune Console. Ownership is visible under Intune\Devices\All devices\<DeviceName>\Properties. The expected behavior is to identify all KME-enrolled devices as corporate. See https://docs.microsoft.com/en-gb/intune/whats-new#week-of-july-23-2018 for more information.
rootCause: This is a known issue.
solution: This issue will be resolved in the 1812 release. However, existing devices will require other remediation steps:Devices newly enrolled after the 1812 release are expected to reflect Corporate ownershipDevices enrolled before 1812 can be updated using one of two methods:After the 1812 update releases, unenrolling and then re-enrolling devices will properly reflect ownership. Clients will need to update the Company Portal app to the 1812 version before re-enrolling. Customers can edit existing devices using the Admin Portal and modifying the device ownership from Personal to Corporate on the device properties blade.

Go to http://contentidea . 2. Click on the orange box labeled Create/Update internal or external KB 3. Close all browser windows. When trying to register with Intune, the following error is seen on Mac devices enrolled into Jamf:Mac X devices with JAMF, Error:Could not extract user and device id from AAD tokenNote that the customer has successfully registered devices prior to this.
rootCause: This can occur if the you upgrade from Jamf Pro 10.7 to 10.7.1 and do not reaffirm consent for the Jamf Native macOS Connector app in Jamf Pro.
solution: To reaffirm consent, in Jamf Pro an Azure global administrator must navigate to Settings > Global Management > Conditional Access > macOS Intune Integration and click Open administrator consent URL.Note: If you upgraded to 10.7.0 or 10.7.1 and reaffirmed your consent, you do not need to do it again after upgrading to 10.8.0.

symptom: After deploying an IOS update and setting a window when devices can updates, the targeted devices don't update after checking in during the allowed window.Kusto shows "NotNow" response from the devices
rootCause: This appears to be a known issue where devices will not update when the devices are locked and configured with a PIN.
solution: As a workaround, end users can manually install updates on their devices from Settings > General > Software Update.

symptom: If a customer created a security baseline between 12/15 and 12/17 and were not flighted into the private preview, they will soon not be able to see the security baseline, although it will apply. Those customers will need to be flighted to be able to use security baselines until is ships on, around, or before end of January 2019.
rootCause: Security baselines was shipped too early, and customers could see and create baselines before everyone was ready.
solution: Request flighting of Security Baselines through the normal flighting request process. The flighting tag is �EnableSecurityBaseline�

symptom: The fields on the MAM set PIN will look like you can fill them in even if you select "No" for PIN. This is a UI bug only. Even if a customer selects "No" and then sets a password policy, it won't be saved. This affects iOS and Android. Screen shot is in attachments.
rootCause: This is due to a UI bug. If you say "No" PIN, none of your PIN options are saved.
solution: The bug is being fixed in the UI update in January.

symptom: You see the following error after deploying the iOS Home Screen Layout device feature configuration policy in Intune when reviewing the policy status, even after confirming the policy has successfully applied to the targeted device(s): -2016281105 (Failed to evaluate the Rule).
rootCause: There are 2 main settings in the Home Screen Layout policy: DockPages While the Dock settings are optional, the Pages settings are NOT and are required. So, if an admin, when creating the policy, only configures, the Dock settings but not the Pages settings, they will end up receiving the aforementioned error, when reviewing the policy deployment status.
solution: **Important: Other than the error in the console, this issue has no impact on targeted devices. The Dock, settings will still apply even if the admin did not configure the Pages settings. There are 2 ways to go about this issue: One is for the customer to simply ignore the error in the console as it has no impact on the targeted devices.The other is to make sure the �Pages� settings are configured, when creating the Home Screen Layout policy.

symptom: After updating to or installing the latest version of the Company Portal app for iOS and macOS (released December 12, 2018), users receive a �Company Portal Temporarily Unavailable� error when attempting to sign in.
rootCause: This occurs because the latest update to the Intune Company Portal app now enforces Apple's updated Application Transport Security (ATS) requirements which also requires TLS 1.2+.
solution: To resolve this issue, make sure that all your network connections configured for use within the iOS Company Portal app and the macOS Company Portal app meet Apple�s new requirements. Some places to check include ADFS configuration, proxy server configuration, and any custom links set in the Admin Console like the support website URL. You can learn more from the resources below: Apple has documented the planned change here: Supporting App Transport SecurityApple provides information about how Safari and WebKit do not support SHA-1 certificates for iOS 10.3+Apple has documentation on NSAppTransportSecurityApple�s nscurl tool can be used for evaluating compliance with the new ATS requirementsMicrosoft Identity has guidance on how to ensure AD FS and WAP support ATSMicrosoft Intune support has developed a PowerShell script to help validate ATS support for endpoint: https://github.com/jchri/ProbeTLSforATS

symptom: Customer has a 10-character device password requirement (alphanumeric with special characters) but Jamf only sees 4 characters and reports that to Intune. As such, Intune is marking the device as non-compliant.
rootCause: Under investigation by Jamf. Internal Product issue: PI-006635
solution: This has been fixed in Jamf Pro 10.13[PI-006635] Fixed an issue that caused computers with macOS 10.14 to fail to correctly report the inventory information for the Minimum Passcode Length value configured in the Passcode payload. This prevented users from accessing company resources in environments integrated with Microsoft Intune.

symptom: You can use the template below to quickly create an NDES lab to test SCEP certificate deployment with Intune. If there is anything missing or incorrect with the template please contact Saurabh Koshta. This template creates a configuration per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/certificates-scep-configure. See below for details.

On a few occasions, we receive cases where the Office apps such as Outlook / Company portal app are crashing or not working as expected on Android devices. To debug/investigate and involve the correct team depending on your scenario, you can collect more information from the affected device by using the Logcat tool.Logcat is a command-line tool that dumps a log of system messages, including stack traces when the device throws an error and messages that you have written from your app with the Log class. This is very similar to the Xcode tool we use to collect events from Apple devices. This article describes the steps to capture all the events that are occurring on an Android device, using the Logcat tool.

The SCEP certificate request fails during the verification phase in the Certificate registration Point so Android and iOS devices fail to receive SCEP certificates even though NDES is configured. Looking at the CRP logs you will see the following.NOTE The default log file location is C:\Program Files\Microsoft Intune\NDESConnectorSvc\Logs\Logs\CertificateRegistrationPoint_xx_xx.svclog> There are three instances of the error with respective to Cryptography Exception:First <Source Name="CertificateRegistrationPoint" />Cryptography Exception: System.Security.Cryptography.CryptographicException: m_safeCertContext is an invalid handle. at System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate.ThrowIfContextInvalid() at System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate.SetThumbprint() at System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate.GetCertHashString() at Microsoft.ConfigurationManager.CertRegPoint.Helper.ValidateChallenge(String base64Encodedtoken, X509Certificate2Collection encryptedCerts, X509Certificate2 SigningCert, String& decodedChallengePassword)SecondCryptography Exception: System.Security.Cryptography.CryptographicException: m_safeCertContext is an invalid handle. at System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate.ThrowIfContextInvalid() at System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate.SetThumbprint() at System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate.GetCertHashString() at Microsoft.ConfigurationManager.CertRegPoint.Helper.ValidateChallenge(String base64Encodedtoken, X509Certificate2Collection encryptedCerts, X509Certificate2 SigningCert, String& decodedChallengePassword) at Microsoft.ConfigurationManager.CertRegPoint.ChallengeValidation.ValidationPhase1(VerifyChallengeParams value, String& decodedChallenge, PKCSDecodedObject& pkcsObj)</ApplicationData></E2ETraceEvent>ThirdCryptography Exception: System.Security.Cryptography.CryptographicException: m_safeCertContext is an invalid handle. at System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate.ThrowIfContextInvalid() at System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate.SetThumbprint() at System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate.GetCertHashString() at Microsoft.ConfigurationManager.CertRegPoint.Helper.ValidateChallenge(String base64Encodedtoken, X509Certificate2Collection encryptedCerts, X509Certificate2 SigningCert, String& decodedChallengePassword) at Microsoft.ConfigurationManager.CertRegPoint.ChallengeValidation.ValidationPhase1(VerifyChallengeParams value, String& decodedChallenge, PKCSDecodedObject& pkcsObj) at Microsoft.ConfigurationManager.CertRegPoint.Controllers.CertificateController.VerifyRequest(VerifyChallengeParams value)
rootCause: This can occur if the registry keys responsible for verification of the certificate request are missing in NDES connector registry settings:
solution: We have identified two solutions to fix this certificate request verification issue.Solution 1:- Restart the Intune Connector Service from services.msc on the connector installed server.- Check the registry path HKLM\Software\Microsoft\MicrosoftIntune\NDESConnector to validate that the registry keys got created per the screenshot below.NOTE If rebooting the server does not resolve the issue then move to Solution 2.Solution 2:If the restart of service\computer does not fix or restore the registry keys and values, follow these steps:- On the NDES computer, open the registry and go to HKEY_LOCAL_Machine:\Software\Microsoft\Cryptography\MSCEP. Change the template values to the default (i.e IPSECIntermediateOffline) and restart the server.- Once the server is rebooted, check the registry path HKEY_LOCAL_Machine:\Software\Microsoft\MicrosoftIntune\NDESConnector and you should now see the signing certificates.- Once the keys are created, change the template name under HKEY_LOCAL_Machine:\Software\Microsoft\Cryptography\MSCEP to the custom template name which was created for SCEP/NDES.

symptom: There are times when you need to gather a Packet Capture on macOS from a customer, for example device is failing to enroll or app is not installing and the Company Portal logs are showing a timeout. In order to do this on macOS Mojave or newer, you can use Fiddler but it requires multiple support file installs and mono tends to crash; this can frustrate your customer. Using the native tools to macOS is quick and simple and gets you the trace you need.
When clicking on a Web App that has been deployed to an iOS device, you receive the following error message: Safari cannot open the page because the address is invalid
rootCause: This can occur if both of the following are true:- The Web App configuration in the Intune console has the setting Require a managed browser to open this link set to Yes. - The Intune Managed Browser for iOS has not been installed on the device.
solution: To resolve this issue, either install the Intune Managed Browser on the iOS device or or configure Require a managed browser to open this link in the policy to No.

symptom: A Windows AutoPilot deployment fails and generates the following error during the OOBE phase of setup: Something went wrong You can try again, or skip for now. OOBEIDPS
rootCause: This problem can occur if there is a proxy, firewall or other network device that is blocking access to the Identity Provider (IdP).
solution: To resolve this problem, make sure there is nothing on your network that is blocking the required communication for Windows AutoPilot. See the following for more details: Windows Autopilot networking requirements

symptom: If the customer is trying to use machine certificates for a VPN profile instead of a user EAP type VPN then currently in Intune this has to be done using custom profile and xml. Below are two examples of xml that use machine certificates.Create a custom Windows 10 profile using the following OMA-URI - ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/VPNv2/nameofvpnprofile/ProfileXML and then enter the XML in either string or string (XML file)Simple example <VPNProfile> <AlwaysOn>true</AlwaysOn> <DnsSuffix>servername.com</DnsSuffix> <NativeProfile> <Servers>vpn.servername.com</Servers> <RoutingPolicyType>SplitTunnel</RoutingPolicyType> <NativeProtocolType>IKEv2</NativeProtocolType> <Authentication> <MachineMethod>Certificate</MachineMethod> </Authentication> </NativeProfile> <DeviceTunnel>true</DeviceTunnel> <RegisterDNS>true</RegisterDNS> <TrustedNetworkDetection>sca.local</TrustedNetworkDetection> <DomainNameInformation> <DomainName>test.local</DomainName> <DnsServers>192.168.100.4</DnsServers> </DomainNameInformation> </VPNProfile> Example with more options <VPNProfile> <NativeProfile> <Servers>vpn.servername.com</Servers> <NativeProtocolType>IKEv2</NativeProtocolType> <Authentication> <MachineMethod>Certificate</MachineMethod> </Authentication> <RoutingPolicyType>SplitTunnel</RoutingPolicyType> <!-- disable the addition of a class based route for the assigned IP address on the VPN interface --> <DisableClassBasedDefaultRoute>true</DisableClassBasedDefaultRoute> </NativeProfile> <!-- use host routes(/32) to prevent routing conflicts <Route> <Address>10.10.0.2</Address> <PrefixSize>32</PrefixSize> </Route> <Route> <Address>10.10.0.3</Address> <PrefixSize>32</PrefixSize> </Route> --> <!-- traffic filters for the routes specified above so that only this traffic can go over the device tunnel <TrafficFilter> <RemoteAddressRanges>10.10.0.2, 10.10.0.3</RemoteAddressRanges> </TrafficFilter> --> <!-- need to specify always on = true --> <AlwaysOn>true</AlwaysOn> <!-- new node to specify that this is a device tunnel --> <DeviceTunnel>true</DeviceTunnel> <!--new node to register client IP address in DNS to enable manage out --> <RegisterDNS>true</RegisterDNS> <!--Added by PJM to specify trusted network --> <TrustedNetworkDetection>test.local</TrustedNetworkDetection> <!-- Added by PJM to specify domain name and DNS Server --> <DomainNameInformation> <DomainName>test.local</DomainName> <DnsServers>192.168.100.4</DnsServers> </DomainNameInformation> </VPNProfile>Settings for dns suffix, server name, address, address ranges, trusted network detection, domain name and dns servers will need to be changed to reflect the values for the customers environment.
If you have a customer reporting that they import a device into Autopilot via the Intune console but it fails to create an AAD device object, following the troubleshooting process below.

Scenario 1 - Base app does not install MacOS line of business (LOB) apps created with the Intune Wrapper for MacOS do not seem to install on the device. Either deployed as Available or as Required, the app installation does not end with the expected result installed app not showing, script not executed, or apps blocked in download on the Mac. Kusto confirms this with a "NotInstalled" status. Customers would say that running the .pkg file locally works for them; that is, the app appears, the script is executed. When preparing the package for MDM distribution, customers more often than not do not finish the process. Or, customer has followed the right steps, but the package name contains spaces, leading to a malformed URL - https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4490742. Scenario 2 - App does not update Reference this excellent article to begin your investigation https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4344141
rootCause: Scenario 1 - Base app does not installAs per Apple documentation https://developer.apple.com/business/documentation/MDM-Protocol-Reference.pdf, macOS apps are installed through MDM as packages. Using productbuild, each package must be signed with an appropriate certificate (such as a TLS/SSL certificate with signing usage) that is verifiable on the client. Only the package needs to be signed, not the app within the package. This means that you would use your Apple Developer certificate, for best experience. Example of running productbuild on a package (with a definitive example in More Information): Open Terminal Type:pkgbuild --root <payload> --scripts <scripts> --version <version> --identifier <packageName> ./<component>_component.pkgproductbuild --package <component>_component.pkg <distributionPackageName>.pkg Use this PKG output to wrap the app with the Intune MacOS wrapper as in - https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/lob-apps-macos Make sure that the package name does not contains spaces, as it can lead to a malformed URL - https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4490742. To test that the .PKG file is signed, use the command pkgutil --check-signature <filename>.pkg where <filename.pkg> is the path to the package. The leaf cert (#1 in the list below) must be marked "Devloper ID Installer". For example:user$ pkgutil --check-signature /Volumes/USBDRIVE/Test.pkg Package "Test": Status: signed by a certificate trusted by Mac OS X Certificate Chain: 1. Developer ID Installer: TestCompany (XX111111U3) SHA1 fingerprint: 00 00 00 82 B7 3B 63 7C 11 B9 4B 58 AF 88 DF BF 1D 73 94 E9 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- 2. Developer ID Certification Authority SHA1 fingerprint: 3B 16 6C 3B 7D C4 B7 51 C9 FE 2A FA B9 13 56 41 E3 88 E1 86 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- 3. Apple Root CA SHA1 fingerprint: 61 1E 5B 66 2C 59 3A 08 FF 58 D1 4A E2 24 52 D1 98 DF 6C 60Scenario 2 - App does not updateApp update should be packaged in the same format, the need is the same. This time, also pay attention that both the base app and the wrapped app have an incremental BuildNumber and VersionNumber for update detection.For example, in the pkgbuild command from Scenario 1, you would increment <version> when creating the components. Based on article https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4344141, review the output of Detection.xml to extract the detection parameters and version for the created .Intunemac file. On a MacOS system with the .intunemac file run the command ./IntuneAppUtil -r <filename.intunemac> [-v] Example output to read:<MacOSLobChildApp BundleId="com.microsoft.autoupdate2" BuildNumber="3.8.1" VersionNumber="3.8.1"/> Refer to https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4344141 for more details.
solution: Scenario 1 - Base app does not install First of all, remove any Required deployments of the affected application. Then, restart the download daemon on the Mac, in case it is hanging. You can do so by opening <Utility Monitor> and searching for downloadd. Alternatively, restart the Mac and sign into Company Portal to sync the device again. Re-package the apps based on the use of productbuild command. Sign the exported package using an appropriate certificate. Use this signed package to create the .intunemac file. Ensure that the customer has followed the productbuild packaging approach and then continue your investigation. At this point, focus your attention to the downloadd process or the installation process on the Mac. If you see this type of error message, follow through with checking for a malformed URL - https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4490742 - default 15:42:28.578234 +0300 storeassetd MDM Invalid download asset URL https://fef.msua04.manage.microsoft.com/DownloadService/GetAppPassive/MacOS/Test LOB.pkg?contentGuid=<>&fileNameHash=<>.pkg.bin&client-request-id=<>&api-version=1.0&IntuneJwtId=<> Note: currently, I am not aware of this working when wrapping multiple distribution archives into one distribution archive. In this case, I recommend you deploy each distribution archive separately. Scenario 2 - App does not update Repackage the apps to contain the missing details, both for Intune's services and for the MacOS to allow in-place update.

symptom: Apps installed in Android Enterprise either crash or do not let you set certain permissions. For example: Outlook's Enable Contact Sync does not turn on (slider remains in Off) Gmail's Contact sync does populate with contacts in the Contacts app or crashes Skype for Business throws a "Permission required" prompt
rootCause: From what we have seen so far, a policy for Android Enterprise work profile to set Default App Permissions to Auto grant. Apps fail to read the permissions as set by the policy and cannot make any changes as instructed. Policy path: Intune > Device Configuration > Profiles > Android Enterprise policy > Work profile > Device restrictions > Work profile settings > Default app permissions.
solution: Option 1: Set this to Device default and resync the devices and try to run the app again. This would break the seamless scenario where users are not prompted for any permission requests, asking users if they allow or block certain permissions when using the apps. Option 2: Based on the app's permissions (which you could read from Google Play Store), you can create an Application Configuration profile for Managed Devices > Android, where you select the needed permissions and set them to Auto grant. For example, in order to enable Outlook's contact sync: Go to Intune > Client Apps > App configuration policies > Add Set a Name Device Enrollment type > Managed devices Platform > Android Associated app - Microsoft Outlook Permissions - Add all from Permission Group > Contacts Set Permission State > Auto grant for each permission Assign policy to affected users Reinstall the app for quick applicability

symptom: There are 2 possible scenarios for having compliance issues in Intune due to SecureBoot:Scenario 1: Windows devices that are able to do SecureBoot. They will show either as Compliant or Not compliant depending on the attestation result.Scenario 2: Windows devices that are NOT able to do Secure boot. They will show as Not applicable.Example: Under this situation, the device may show as Compliant. But please be aware that Compliant does not mean The device has Secure Boot enabled.Unless you dig specifically into the Device compliance tab its not very clear the policy may not actually be applied. One way to verify for sure whether device health is being attested is to go to the Device Compliance/Windows Health attestation report in the UI which will show a bunch of nonsense for the device if its health isnt being attested.Example:
rootCause: Scenario 1: The compiance result will depend on the TPM recording into PCR7 (SecureBoot) or not. Please go to the Resolution part in this article to find out the troubleshooting steps and data needed.Scenario 2: Windows devices that are not able to do SecureBoot means that the TPM itself is not able to do SecureBoot. Customer needs to check with the device manufacturer to confirm if the device/TPM is able to do SecureBoot.
solution: First, please confirm that TPM shows only as TPM is ready for use (please ask the customer to send a snapshot): TPM is ready for user with limited functionality is not always enough from an Intune point of view and may cause issues. Please involve the Windows team to help the customer get the TPM into a TPM is ready for use" state.You can ask for the following registry keys in order to check this:o Exported HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\TPM\WMI\Admino Exported HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\TPMhttps://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/dubaisec/2017/02/28/tpm-owner-password/When GPO is configured to store Full authentication data, TPM Owner password will be stored as OwnerAuthFull in the registry: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\TPM\WMI\Adminhttps://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/information-protection/tpm/change-the-tpm-owner-passwordImportant Although the TPM owner password is not retained starting with Windows 10, version 1607, you can change a default registry key to retain it. However, we strongly recommend that you do not make this change. To retain the TPM owner password, set the registry key 'HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\TPM' [REG_DWORD] 'OSManagedAuthLevel' to 4. The default value for this key is 2, and unless it is changed to 4 before the TPM is provisioned, the owner password will not be saved.If the problem persists, please get the following data:o Output of powershell command manage-bde -protectors -get c:o Output of powershell command get-tpmo Snapshot of TPM.msco And finally (and most important) please provide the following logs: C:\Windows\Logs\MeasuredBootThe MeasuredBoot logs will show if the TPM is actually recording into PCR7 (SecureBoot) or not, that is why they are important, because they show information coming directly from the TPM.You can use the strings.exe tool to convert the logs (as these are binary logs) and look for the following strings:SecureBootEV_EFI_VARIABLE_DRIVER_CONFIGSample use of Strings.exe:You can either convert the whole file and check the information in it, or alternatively, you can run the following command to see if the SecureBoot string shows up (replace the name of the log with your own log):strings.exe 0000000371-0000000000.log | findstr SecureBootIn case these logs are not returning anything, it means that there is no SecureBoot string in the log. So DHA cant say it is attested.In this situation, the device will show as Not Compliant.As a proof of concept, you can run the same command on the logs from your own laptop, you should get the SecureBoot string as a result:C: \Strings>strings.exe "C:\MeasuredBoot\0000000107-0000000005.log" | findstr SecureBootSecureBootIf you see the SecureBoot string in the output from the above command, then the machine has a secure boot variable. Else it isnt measuring it.

symptom: Intune: Ingesting Office 16 ADMX-Backed policies using Microsoft Intune Starting in Windows 10, version 1703, we can import ADMX files (also called ADMX ingestion) and set those ADMX-backed policies for Win32 and Desktop Bridge apps by using Windows 10 Mobile Device Management (MDM) on desktop. The ADMX files that define policy information can be ingested to your device by using the Policy CSP URI, ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall. The ingested ADMX file is then processed into MDM policies. When the ADMX policies are imported, the registry keys to which each policy is written are checked so that known system registry keys, or registry keys that are used by existing inbox policies or system components, are not overwritten. This precaution helps to avoid security concerns over opening the entire registry. Currently, the ingested policies are not allowed to write to locations within the System, Software\Microsoft, and Software\Policies\Microsoft keys, except for the following locations: � Software\Policies\Microsoft\Office\ � Software\Microsoft\Office\ � Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ � Software\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\ � software\policies\microsoft\shared tools\proofing tools\ � software\policies\microsoft\imejp\ � software\policies\microsoft\ime\shared\ � software\policies\microsoft\shared tools\graphics filters\ � software\policies\microsoft\windows\currentversion\explorer\ � software\policies\microsoft\softwareprotectionplatform\ � software\policies\microsoft\officesoftwareprotectionplatform\ � software\policies\microsoft\windows\windows search\preferences\ � software\policies\microsoft\exchange\ � software\microsoft\shared tools\proofing tools\ � software\microsoft\shared tools\graphics filters\ � software\microsoft\windows\windows search\preferences\ � software\microsoft\exchange\ � software\policies\microsoft\vba\security\ � software\microsoft\onedrive Summary of steps to Ingest an ADMX files: Download the ADMX�s backed polies for office16 from the list https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=49030 Create OMA-URI and its value for the policy. Create a Device Configuration Policy in Intune and Assign to the user. review the Settings after the Deployment from Intune. Configure the settings and reflect it on Office Apps installed on the targeted Machines. . Summery Step 1: After downloading the ADMX's for Office 16 to your machine you will notice that each product under Microsoft office has its own ADMX-Backed Policies Step 2: Now we need to deliver these ADMX's-Packed Policies to our machines that we want to manage Office 16 Apps Using Intune Custom Profile using the following steps : In the Azure portal, select All services, filter on Intune, and select Microsoft Intune. Select Device configuration > Profiles > Create profile. Enter the following settings: Name: Enter a name for the profile, such as Office 16 custom profile. Description: Enter a description for the profile. Platform: Choose Windows 10 and later. Profile type: Choose Custom. In Custom OMA-URI Settings, select Add. Enter the following settings: Name: Enter a unique name for the OMA-URI setting to help you identify it in the list of settings. Description: Enter a description that gives an overview of the setting, and any other important details. OMA-URI (case sensitive): Enter the OMA-URI you want to use as a setting ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/Office16Policy Data type: Choose the data type you'll use for this OMA-URI setting. Your options: String Value: Enter the data value you want to associate with the OMA-URI you entered. in our case we will put the content of Office16 ADMX file into the Value Box. Note: For office16 ADMX File its a large file and I managed to Split it and reduce the size of the file to 3 parts and keeping the formatting intact for the policies and its related Categories. Below table showing the OMA-URI for the rest of Office Apps, Data Type and the Values for each one. Office Application OMA-URI Data Type Value Microsoft Access 2016 ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/Access16 String The content inside access16.admx Microsoft Excel 2016 ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/Excel16 String The content inside excel16.admx Microsoft Lync 2016 ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/lync16 String The content inside lync16.admx Microsoft OneNote 2016 ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/Onenote String The content inside onent16.admx Microsoft Outlook 2016 ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/Outlook16 String The content inside outlk16.admx Microsoft PowerPoint 2016 ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/Powerpoint16 String The content inside ppt16.admx Microsoft Project 2016 ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/Project16 String The content inside proj16.admx Microsoft Publisher 2016 ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/Publisher16 String The content inside pub16.admx Microsoft Visio 2016 ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/Visio16 String The content inside visio16.admx Microsoft Word 2016 ./Device/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/ConfigOperations/ADMXInstall/Office16/Policy/Word16 String The content inside word16.admx you Can put all the Application settings under one custom profile by repeating step number 4 "In Custom OMA-URI Settings, select Add. Enter the above settings for each office app Step 3: 5 Select OK to save your changes. Continue to add more settings as needed. 6 When finished, choose OK > Create to create the Intune profile. When complete, your profile is shown in the Device configuration - Profiles list. 7 check Device Status on the Created Profile: Step 4: Review the settings from Machine Side: - Open Registry edit - Navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\AdmxInstalled\ - Navigate to: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\AdmxDefault Step 5: After ingesting the Office 16 apps we are going now to configure a sample setting for Microsoft PowerPoint L_Save : In the Azure portal, select All services, filter on Intune, and select Microsoft Intune.
