Select Device configuration > Profiles > Create profile. Enter the following settings: Name: Enter a name for the profile, such as Office 16 custom profile. Description: Enter a description for the profile. Platform: Choose Windows 10 and later. Profile type: Choose Custom. In Custom OMA-URI Settings, select Add. Enter the following settings: Name: Enter a unique name for the OMA-URI setting to help you identify it in the list of settings. Description: Enter a description that gives an overview of the setting, and any other important details. OMA-URI (case sensitive): Enter the OMA-URI you want to use as a setting ./User/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/Office16~Policy~L_MicrosoftOfficePowerPoint~L_PowerPointOptions~L_Save Note: Office16~Policy~L_MicrosoftOfficePowerPoint~L_PowerPointOptions~L_Save: indicate the location of the current policy that we ingest previously User : because the policy is targeting user ( as mentioned in the PPT16 ADMX File ) <policy name="L_Defaultfilelocation" class="User" displayName="$(string.L_Defaultfilelocation)" explainText="$(string.L_Specifiesthedefaultlocationforpresentationfiles)" presentation="$(presentation.L_Defaultfilelocation)" key="software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\powerpoint\options"> <parentCategory ref="L_Save" /> <supportedOn ref="windows:SUPPORTED_Windows7" /> <elements> <text id="L_defaultfilelocation0" key="software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\powerpoint\recentfolderlist" valueName="default" required="true" expandable="true" /> </elements> </policy> Data type: Choose the data type you'll use for this OMA-URI setting. Your options: String Value: <enabled/><data id="L_defaultfilelocation0" value="default"/> Select OK to save your changes. Continue to add more settings as needed, in this Article I will add another few settings like L_AutoRecoversavefrequencyminutes as it will be enabled by just inputting the required settings not by <enabled/> <policy name="L_SaveAutoRecoverinfo" class="User" displayName="$(string.L_SaveAutoRecoverinfo)" explainText="$(string.L_CheckedCheckstheoptionSaveAutoRecoverinfoUncheckedUnchecksth)" presentation="$(presentation.L_SaveAutoRecoverinfo)" key="software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\powerpoint\options"> <parentCategory ref="L_Save" /> <supportedOn ref="windows:SUPPORTED_Windows7" /> <elements> <boolean id="L_EnablesaveAutoRecoverinfo" valueName="saveautorecoveryinfo"> <trueValue> <decimal value="1" /> </trueValue> <falseValue> <decimal value="0" /> </falseValue> </boolean> <decimal id="L_AutoRecoversavefrequencyminutes" valueName="frequencytosaveautorecoveryinfo" minValue="1" maxValue="9999" /> <text id="L_AutoRecoversavelocation" valueName="pathtoautorecoveryinfo" expandable="true" /> </elements> </policy> This Policy include multiple Values inside it like the highlighted ones ( Boolean, Decimal and text ), in this kind of policies I advise to add the ADMX inside windows policy definitions and the ADML files inside Policy Definition/en-us in order to review the policy settings and the parameters inside it. Using the gpedit.msc ( User and Device Administrative template then Microsoft PowerPoint 2016 ) as we only need to input the data inside these parameter to make it enabled. So the settings from Intune side to make this Policy enabled to the targeted machines: OMA-URI: ./User/Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/ Office16~Policy~L_MicrosoftOfficePowerPoint~L_PowerPointOptions~L_Save/L_SaveAutoRecoverinfo Data-Type: String Value: <data id="L_AutoRecoversavefrequencyminutes" value="1"/> <data id="L_AutoRecoversavelocation" value="%USERPROFILE%\Application Data\Microsoft\PowerPoint"/> When finished, choose OK > Create to create the Intune profile. When complete, your profile is shown in the Device configuration - Profiles list. Summery: This support guide has been tested and reviewed as its show us the full cycle of ingesting an ADMX file for office and how to work with the policies inside it noting that each policy has its own Configurations and you need to be aware in how to deal with them to deliver them successfully to your desired machines, noting that some policies inside each ADMX File is different from the other ones, as I advised before in this Article I recommend to review the required policy from the administrative template as its showing you a Clear User Interface view For the settings included in this policy. Also, we have a new Preview feature has been recently added in Intune (Administrative Templates) and its include some of the ingested settings but at this point not all of them, hopefully we will be added in the near future.

After configuring Intune/Jamf integration, Mac clients fail to to enroll and you notice one or more of the following the symptoms:- the Jamf logs contain the error abnormalcode:64 - The Company Portal app on the device spins indefinitely and never completes.- The device appears in the Intune admin portal with a sign-in status of failure.- The error no AAD ID found for macOS user account X is displayed in the logs.- The Mac device never becomes compliant.
rootCause: This can occur if the user fails to authenticate via the Company Portal app 3 times. This is a requirement of Jamf.
solution: To resolve this issue, have the user launch the Company Portal app and sign in again until they get the "Compliant" screen. According to Jamf this must be done three times. An example of a device showing to be in compliance after the 3rd authentication is below.

symptom: When the SCEP profile is applied to a Windows 10 computer it can be seen at this location in the registry.HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\SCEP\MS DM Server\ModelName_ScopeID_ID_ConfigurationPolicy_ID\InstallThis can be used to troubleshoot SCEP enrollment issues by insuring the SCEP policy is being applied correctly to the device
IIS logs shows 400 error (bad request) when mobile device contacts the NDES server.
rootCause: Handler Mappings in IIS are in the incorrect order
solution: To fix this do the following 1) Open IIS 2) Select �Default Web Site� 3) Click �View Applications� in the action panel on the right 4) Double click the mscep application 5) Double click �Handler Mappings� 6) Click �View Ordered List�� in the action panel 7) Select ExtensionlessUrlHandler-ISAPI-4.0_64bit and move it down so it is below StaticFile 8) Repeat steps 6-8 for the mscep_admin application 9) Restart IIS

symptom: When reading Mac console logs the 3rd SCEP request shows a 414 response error.
rootCause: The 414 error is request size too big. This can happen if there are extra inactive Intune Certificate connector object in the Intune Admin console from multiple install attempts of the Certificate connector.
solution: Check the Intune Admin console under Intune -> Device configuration -> Certification Authority and remove any inactive Intune Certificate connector objects.

symptom: The main objective of the Help desk Operator role is to allow standard users to troubleshoot using the Troubleshooting blade. However, when logged on as a Helpdesk operator role member, the Troubleshooting blade displays a banner stating, �Account status You do not have enough permissions� and no user/ device data is displayed.
rootCause: The user is not licensed for Intune.
solution: Login to https://portal.azure.com as administrator. On the Intune blade, go to Users, then All Users. Click the user for whom the Troubleshooting blade is failing to load. On the Licenses tab, click +Assign and follow the wizard to assign an Intune license. Advise Helpdesk role member to login to Ibiza in a new browser session.

symptom: The Intune Connector Service (NDESConnectorSvc) fails to start and you see one or more of the messages below.The Network Device Enrollment Service policy module was started successfully.The Network Device Enrollment Service cannot retrieve one of its required certificates (0x80070057). The parameter is incorrect.The Network Device Enrollment Service cannot be started (0x80070057). The parameter is incorrect.
rootCause: This can occur if the NDES computer cannot reach the Certificate Revocation List (CRL).
solution: Engage the Certificate Authority team to assist in investigating and resolving the underlying CRL issue.

symptom: In an Intune/Configuration Manager hybrid environment, you may notice the following symptoms:Newly enrolled devices do not show up in the ConfigMgr console.Changes made to existing policy and settings do not take effect on devices (existing policy will continue to work). New policies are not being applied to devices.You may also notice alerts in Configuration Manager indicating that the certificate for the Intune Connector has expired.
rootCause: There is a known issue that can occur if the customer has certain versions of ConfigMgr current branch installed on Windows Server 2012 R2 or Windows Server 2012. This issue can cause the connector certificate to fail to be renewed. If the certificate cannot be renewed or has expired, these symptoms may be observed. Currently there have been reports of this with Configuration Manager CB 1802, 1806 and 1810.
solution: There are two resolutions depending on whether the certificate has already expired or not. This can be determined by looking at the entries in the DMPUploader log file.If the certificate is still valid but cannot be renewedIf the certificate cannot be renewed but is still valid you will find an entry similar to the following in the DMPUPloader log file:GetServiceVersion failed. Possibly this api doesn't support by current cloud service. You can ignore it. Exception: [Unable to cast COM object of type 'System.__ComObject' to interface type 'CERTENROLLLib.CX509PrivateKey'. This operation failed because the QueryInterface call on the COM component for the interface with IID '{728AB362-217D-11DA-B2A4-000E7BBB2B09}' failed due to the following error: No such interface supported (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80004002 (E_NOINTERFACE)).]If your customer is receiving this error message then they will require a hotfix to Microsoft.ConfigurationManager.DmpConnector.Connector.dll which will resolve the problem on SCCM 1806 or 1810. The hotfix can be found here or in via the public KB article here:4487960 - Microsoft Intune connector certificate does not renew in Configuration Manager (https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4487960)Note that this zip file contains both the 1806 and the 1810 versions of the fix. If you use the zip please be sure that you send the customer the right one for their CM version.To install the hotfix, complete the following:1. Stop the SMS_Executive service2. Remove or copy out the existing file from the customers bin/x64 folder in the CM install folder3. Copy the hotfix file into the bin/x64 folder4. Restart the SMS_Executive serviceOnce the certificate is successfully renewed you will find a line like this in DMPUploader.log:Connector certificate renewed. SMS_DMP_UPLOADER 17/01/2019 20:57:23 36852 (0x8FF4)If the connector certificate is already expiredIf the certificate has already expired you will find an entry similar to the following in the DMPUPloader log file:Making Web Request to Location Service Url exception System.Net.WebException: The remote server returned an error: (403) Forbidden.~~ at System.Net.HttpWebRequest.GetResponse()~~ at Microsoft.ConfigurationManager.DmpConnector.Connector.SccmProxyGenerator.GetRestUserAuthLocationServiceResponse()If your customer is receiving this error message then there are two options available:1. Migrate to Intune Standalone: They can do this by deleting their ConfigMgr subscription from SCCM and selecting the option to migrate to standalone. They will need to recreate their policy within 7 days within Intune Standalone or the devices will lose their policies/settings etc.2. File an IcM to have the Intune CxE team manually renew the certificate. Note that your case must be escalated to the Intune Escalation Team (IET) so they can create the IcM. Once the IcM has been created, CxE will perform a remote session with the customer and obtain a new certificate for them, and also help them import this into their CM server. This typically takes about 40 minutes to complete and the customer must provide us with credentials for an account with an Intune license, that has the Intune Administrator role, and does not have either ADFS or MFA enabled on the account. A 'cloud only' account created for this purpose is ideal. As soon as CxE provides the customer with the new certificate the account can be disabled, deleted or the credentials changed. IET members: Please contact John Arnold when you raise this IcM request. For any questions, concerns, or problems with this process please engage the Microsoft Teams channel here.

symptom: In order to use the Kusto database to troubleshoot Intune issues, support engineers will need to submit a request to gain access to Kusto. If you are a member of the CSS Intune Support Team you can request access by following the steps below. 1. Navigate to https://coreidentity.microsoft.com/manage/Entitlement/entitlement/intunekustoc-52ol. If the link does not work, go to https://coreidentity.microsoft.com/, select Entitlements, then search by keyword for IntuneKusto-Css. Click on IntuneKusto-CSS, then select Request membership at the top. In the Request membership wizard, select For Myself. Under Select which personal account, ensure your corporate account is selected. Under Role, select ReadOnly. Under Business Justification, enter "As an Intune Support Engineer, I need access to support Intune CSS Customers" Click Submit. When your request is approved, you will receive a welcome email. To configure Kusto, use these steps: Download Kusto Explorer from https://aka.ms/ke Download and launch Kusto Explorer Right-click Connections and choose Add connection For Cluster connection, enter https://intune.kusto.windows.net and click OK to save your settings. This setting will persist the next time you launch Kusto Explorer. Expand Connections\Intune\Intune under the Connections pane on the left-hand side of Kusto Explorer. Click on the second Intune entry to select it. This will set the cluster context for your queries.

Consider the following scenario: Start with a device enrolled into an Intune integrated Jamf Pro server and register the device with Azure ADClean out the login keychain, as recommended by Jamf, to re-register when needed.ReregisterClean out keychain again to re-register a second time.ReregisterRun /usr/local/jamf/bin/jamfAAD gatherAADInfo multiple times to produce multiple entries of the same AAD IDs on the machine.Expected Results: Only one entry per valid AAD ID is held in the database Actual Results: Every time the jamfAAD binary sends over AAD ID information, a new entry gets created. All of the entries update at the same time when submitting information to Intune, and all of them get deactivated at the same time when a qualifying event occurs.
rootCause: Product Issue on the Jamf side (PI-006679)
solution: To resolve this, delete the device record from Jamf Pro and re-enroll the device.

symptom: The Device compliance reporting for a Windows 10 device displays a compliance state for a System account, as well as for the user account which enrolled the device.This can be seen within the Device compliance section, under Monitor, when navigating to the following within the Intune portal and selecting a device record: Intune -> Devices-> All devices.
rootCause: The System account is used during the device check-in if there is no user logged in to the device, or if the user that enrolled the device is not the user that is currently logged in.
solution: The reported behavior is expected.

symptom: Customers may find that they are unable to access the Apple ID account to renew their APNs certificate in Intune. To avoid creating a new account/APNs certificate and re-enrolling their iOS devices, they may first opt to reach out to Apple DEP Support to request the ownership of the APNs certificates be transferred to another Apple ID. In cases where customers are verified and do not have the original .PEM certificate available, Intune Support is able to retrieve the APNs certificate details that have already been used to enroll iOS devices.IMPORTANT: You must get written authorization from an Intune Admin or Global Admin (GA) before providing the information below. Simply have the admin (Intune or GA) send you an email stating that you have permission to obtain and share information about the existing Apple certificate with the customer requesting it. Make sure this is documented in the case.In Rave, navigate to the Customer�s Tenant -> Applications -> Intune -> Data tab. Search for a user who has an enrolled iOS device with the APNs certificate. Select the user�s iOS device -> Policy Targeting Information -> Policy Type 7 -> Download Policy Body. This contains the APNs certificate value downloaded to devices from Apple. Open the policy in your XML editor of choice.Copy the APNs certificate value inside of the quotation marks. This should be a string of characters and ends in two equal signs. Paste the value into a new file. Save a copy as an �All File� type and append the file name with .CER. Double click or open the .CER locally and verify the certificate matches the one the customer is requesting. You can match these using the Subject ID/Expiration details visible in the customer�s Intune portal (Intune -> Device Enrollment -> Apple Enrollment -> Apple MDM Push Certificate) and the Thumbprint/Expiration details in Rave (Intune -> Troubleshooting -> Enrollment -> Platform Onboarding -> Platform iOS). The customer should now have the information needed to complete their assistance request to Apple DEP Support.*Microsoft makes no guarantees that Apple support will be able to fulfill these requests for customers, and we cannot open Apple support requests for customers on their behalf.

Devices are unable to obtain certificates from the NDES server and the following errors are logged:On the Certificate Authority --> Failed Requests Request Status Code:"The requested certificate template is not supported by this CA. 0x80094800 (-2146875392 CERTSRV_E_UNSUPPORTED_CERT_TYPE)"Request Disposition Message:"The request was for a certificate template that is not supported by the Active Directory Certificate Services Policy: <Template name in the client request>"On the NDES Server --> Application Event LogLog Name: ApplicationSource: Microsoft-Windows-NetworkDeviceEnrollmentServiceEvent ID: 31Level: ErrorUser: CONTOSO\SVC_NDESComputer: NDES1.contoso.comDescription:The Network Device Enrollment Service cannot submit the certificate request (The requested certificate template is not supported by this CA.). 0x80004005Event Xml:<Event xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/win/2004/08/events/event"> <EventData Name="EVENT_MSCEP_FAIL_SUBMIT"> <Data Name="ErrorCode">The requested certificate template is not supported by this CA.</Data> <Data Name="ErrorMessage">0x80004005</Data> </EventData></Event>
rootCause: This can occur if the name of the template on the NDES computer does not match the template name on the Certification Authority (CA). In this case the customer had put in the Friendly Name of the template rather than the template name.
solution: To resolve this issue, verify that the values in the following registry map to the certificate template name correctly.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MSCEP\SignatureTemplateEncryptionTemplateGeneralPurposeTemplateAfter correcting the values, restart the "Intune Connector Service" on the NDES computer.

symptom: Customer has shared that they have had a hard time finding a field person at Lenovo to help get devices auto-registered. Here's how to contact Lenovo, Dell, and HP:Lenovo: autopilot@lenovo.comDell: DellAutoPilot@dell.comHP: autopilot.requests@hp.com

When MAM policies (aka App Protection Policies or APP) are applied to Outlook Mobile app version 2.2.260 or version 3.0.0, users of Samsung J7 devices running Android 6.x (Marshmallow) may experience one or more of the following issues:The Outlook mobile app crashesUsing the Report function fails with "Unfortunately, Outlook has stopped."Outlook shows a white screen after configuring the corporate accountEmails load but Outlook crashes if you try to scroll up or down or click on an email to read it.Please Note:Other Android devices may be affected but this is mostly seen with Samsung J7 devicesOther versions of Android may also be affectedUsers on iOS devices are not affected.If Intune MAM policies are remove the problems no longer occurTypically users report that Outlook worked fine before upgrading to version 2.2.260 or version 3.0.0,
rootCause: There is a bug in Android's Java Virtual Machine implementation on specific Android devices (predominantly the SM-J700F sku of the Samsung J7) running Android 6.0.1 with a specific firmware build (J700FXXU4BRL2). This Android bug manifests in a crash in the VM when the Intune MAM code calls a function in an object created by the java.lang.reflect.Proxy function newProxyInstance. The MAM code calls this function in accordance to the Android documentation
solution: As of 2/8/2019 a fix has been created and released as an update to the Intune SDK via Android Company PortalCustomers will also need to install the latest version of the Outlook Mobile app

symptom: Apple iOS allows MDM's to configure the home screen layout on iOS devices running 9.3 or later.These devices must be supervised. This feature allows you to deploy "pages" to the iOS device. For example, you can deploy a new page that contains 12 applications, and a folder.Inside of this folder, you can specify further applications (that are not listed elsewhere) that are contained in here. This allows administrators to categorize applications, such as the Apple-included apps into a folder, Microsoft apps into a folder, LOB apps, etc. ==================================However, what was happening for this scenario was different - devices would receive all applications as expected, the "stock" apps (pre-installed on iOS) would be categorized as expected, but the VPP applications etc. that MDM was deploying were not all being placed into the correct folders.When checking the Intune Portal, the policy showed as compliant, but the applications never were placed as expected. Note, this scenario had upwards of 50 applications being deployed, so it might be caused by the large number of applications and pages.

When trying to log in to the Intune connector for Active Directory, your sign-in fails with the following error:An unexpected error has occurred.An error occurred while processing your request. View Service Status
rootCause: This can occur if the account used to sign-in is not assigned an Office or Intune license.
solution: To resolve this problem, assign an Intune license to the user account being used to sign-in to the connector.

symptom: The purpose of this document is to explain how transfer a Rave case from the Broad Commercial team to the Intune pod. There are three scenarios that require transfer to the Intune pod: 1. Customer is inquiring about a dedicated pod support topic 2. Case was filed by a premier customer3. Case was filed by a partner who has the Advanced Support for Partners (ASfP) or Premier Support for Partners (PSfP) service level Engineer Workflow: Step 1) Confirm whether the case falls under pod scope: Scenario 1: Customer is inquiring about a dedicated pod support topic: *** The following support topics require pod expertise: Company Resource Access (NDES, SCEP, Certificates, Wifi profiles, VPN profiles, email profiles, NDES Connector, CISCO ISE)Intune Graph API, Intune App SDK (Prepare/ Wrap LOB apps for app protection), Windows Information Protection Intune Insiders (Report a problem with pre-release features, sign up your tenant for Intune Insiders) If the information in the case does not allow you to conclude whether the customer is inquiring about a pod support topic, contact the customer to scope the issue and confirm whether the case indeed requires pod expertise. If the case is found to fall under a dedicated pod support topic, move on to step 2. Scenario 2: Case was filed by a premier customer: ***Validate Premier tenant status in Rave: While the case is open, click on the company name in the upper left (next to the case #). On the Profile tab, check the Premier tenant field. If Premier tenant value is yes, proceed with prerequisite. Scenario 3: Case was filed by a partner who has the Advanced Support for Partners or the Premier Support for Partners service level: *** To check whether the tenant has the Advanced Support for Partners or the Premier Support for Partners flag, load the case and check Quick facts on the left: Step 2) If you have established that the case requires pod expertise, post your case to the BC to POD Teams channel. Step 3) Tag your TA in the BC to POD Teams channel to notify them about the pending transfer. TA workflow: TAs will monitor the BC to Pod channel and review pending transfers from their respective teams. When a case is uploaded to the BC to Pod Channel, TA will proceed as follows: Step 1) Confirm that case indeed falls under pod scope based ***If case is found to not fall under pod scope, the TA will ask the submitting engineer to resume work on the case. Step 2) Ensure case resides in EnterpriseIntune support area ***Look up the current support area of the case, and if it is not EnterpriseIntune, take steps to transfer it to EnterpriseIntune: Change case view to Full History. In the case logs, look for an entry similar as the one below: If the support area is not EnterpriseIntune, transfer the case to EnterpriseIntune and take ownership of the case once it arrives in EnterpriseIntune:Under Case action, select Transfer, then OK. Set Transfer Reason to Customer needs specialized support. Set Product to EnterpriseIntune.Hit Transfer. Take ownership of the EnterpriseIntune case. Step 3) Fill out the following template and save it to the case: Transfer from BC to Intune Pod Customer region: NOAM/APAC/EMEA Reason for transfer: Indicate reason of transfer, for example, "premier customer ", �Partner case� or "pod support topic" Step 4) Submit an Assistance Request to engage the Mobility Pod: Under Case Action, select Request assistance, then OK. Under Team, select EnterpriseIntune. Under Description, provide a summary in the following format: BC to POD - Customer region: NOAM/ APAC/ EMEA - transfer reason: Premier customer, partner case or pod support topic Examples:BC to POD - NOAM - Premier customerBC to POD - APAC - Partner case BC to POD - EMEA - Pod support topic (NDES) Click Request. ***IMPORTANT: Do not include < or > symbols in the description field. Text that is entered between less than (<) and greater than (>) symbols will be lost. Step 5) Update case thread in Teams Channel once steps above have been taken. Pod engineer workflow: Pod engineer closes the assistance request and takes over the case. *** If you are technically blocked from taking over the case, ask a TA to assist with the reassignment.

If you ever find an internal KB that is out of date, has wrong information or otherwise needs updating, you can always file a Content Idea request to get it changed, however did you know that you can also edit that KB yourself? Just like how we encourage everyone to write KB articles, we also encourage everyone to keep these KBs up to date and accurate, so always feel welcome to update any KB you find whether you wrote it or not. Below are instructions for updating a KB article without having to open a Content Idea request to get it changed.

If you find this article prior to February 15th, 2019, please send a quick email to jchornbe letting him know that you found it and how you found it (e.g. searched in Service Desk Knowledge, saw it referenced in Teams, etc.).

MSFB apps are not being deleted from "Intune > Apps" blade, after successfully refunding them from MSFB portal. (Steps followed below or https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/windows-store-for-business#remove-apps) i. Navigate to MSfB ii. Go to the desired application details and select � Remove product iii. Confirm Your refund was successful. iv. Navigate to Endpoint Manager > Tenant admins > Microsoft Store for Business > Sync Observed Behavior: MSFB continue to show in Intune > Apps blade, despite being refunded in MSFB portal and multiple Syncs being forced. Expected Behavior: MSFB should disappear from Intune > Apps blade, after being refunded in MSFB portal and a sync between Intune and MSFB occurs. An app needs to be made available to no one in the Private Store and then refunded to be removed from Intune. A refund for a paid app can only occur within 90 days of purchase and can only be done by an Admin or the Purchaser. After 90 days no refund can occur, so an app cannot be removed from the Private Store inventory. Once refunded, this will then trigger a removal from the Intune side once the app is refunded. A manual sync of the Store may need to occur to see the change, otherwise the Store will sync every 24 hours.
rootCause: Currently there is a known issue where some applications won't be able to be removed/refunded from the Microsoft Store for business. Now since MSFB will be out of support soon (2023) there are no plans to address that behavior.
solution: Admins can try the steps under symptoms to refund paid or Free Apps https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/windows-store-for-business#remove-apps. However, if they run into the known issue described in this article, there is nothing that can be done and that is a MSFB team decision and not Intune's. Intune Rave case for reference: 30772720

symptom: As there seems to be a lot of confusion around what the difference is between Modern Auth and OAuth2, I�ve included some info you may find helpful going forward.From Hybrid Modern Authentication overview and prerequisites for using it with on-premises Skype for Business and Exchange servers:What is Modern Authentication?When talking about communication between a client (for example, your laptop or your phone) and a server, Microsoft uses the phrase 'Modern authentication'.Modern authentication is an umbrella term for a combination of authentication and authorization methods, as well as some security measures that rely on access policies that you may already be familiar with. It includes:Authentication methods: Multi-factor Authentication; Client Certificate-based authentication; and the Active Directory Authentication Library ( ADAL).Authorization methods: Microsoft's implementation of Open Authorization (OAuth).Conditional access policies: Mobile Application Management (MAM) and Azure Active Directory Conditional Access.Managing user identities with Modern Authentication gives administrators many different tools to use when it comes to securing resources and offers more secure methods of identity management to both on-premises (Exchange and Skype for Business), Exchange hybrid, and Skype for Business hybrid/split-domain scenarios.What is OAuth 2.0:From RFC 6749:The OAuth 2.0 authorization framework enables a third-party application to obtain limited access to an HTTP service, either on behalf of a resource owner by orchestrating an approval interaction between the resource owner and the HTTP service, or by allowing the third-party application to obtain access on its own behalf. This specification replaces and obsoletes the OAuth 1.0 protocol described in RFC 5849.In the traditional client-server authentication model, the client requests an access-restricted resource (protected resource) on the server by authenticating with the server using the resource owner's credentials. In order to provide third-party applications access to restricted resources, the resource owner shares its credentials with the third party. This creates several problems and limitations:Third-party applications are required to store the resource owner's credentials for future use, typically a password in clear-text.Servers are required to support password authentication, despite the security weaknesses inherent in passwords.Third-party applications gain overly broad access to the resource owner's protected resources, leaving resource owners without any ability to restrict duration or access to a limited subset of resources.Resource owners cannot revoke access to an individual third party without revoking access to all third parties, and must do so by changing the third party's password.Compromise of any third-party application results in compromise of the end-user's password and all of the data protected by that password.OAuth addresses these issues by introducing an authorization layer and separating the role of the client from that of the resource owner. In OAuth, the client requests access to resources controlled by the resource owner and hosted by the resource server, and is issued a different set of credentials than those of the resource owner.Instead of using the resource owner's credentials to access protected resources, the client obtains an access token -- a string denoting a specific scope, lifetime, and other access attributes. Access tokens are issued to third-party clients by an authorization server with the approval of the resource owner. The client uses the access token to access the protected resources hosted by the resource server.For example, an end-user (resource owner) can grant a printing service (client) access to her protected photos stored at a photo-sharing service (resource server), without sharing her username and password with the printing service. Instead, she authenticates directly with a server trusted by the photo-sharing service (authorization server), which issues the printing service delegation-specific credentials (access token).If you�d like to read more on Modern Auth and OAuth 2.0, here are some docs you may find helpful:Modern AuthenticationHybrid Modern Authentication overview and prerequisites for using it with on-premises Skype for Business and Exchange serversHow to configure Exchange Server on-premises to use Hybrid Modern AuthenticationHow to use Modern Authentication (ADAL) with Skype for Business Removing or disabling Hybrid Modern Authentication from Skype for Business and ExchangeOAuth 2.0OAuth 2.0RFC 6749: The OAuth 2.0 Authorization FrameworkRFC 6750: OAuth 2.0 Bearer Token UsageOAuth 2 Simplified

This article describes the official Microsoft CSS policy for asking customers to send their Apple LOB apps (IPA files) to test and repro issues. Please read and understand the information below before requesting any IPA files from a customer.
If your customer is reporting Managed Google Play applications are not auto-updating when enrolled as Android Dedicated (COSU) devices, please follow these instructions.
rootCause: There are many factors controlled by Google that can impact the app update behavior
solution: Managed Google Play app update overview The update experience for apps installed from managed Google Play is dependent upon the Play Store's internal app update behavior. Once an app is installed on a device, when the app developer publishes an update to the Play Store the Play store infrastructure handles automatic updates of the app. Google's public statement on this behavior is here https://developer.android.com/distribute/googleplay/work: Deploying updates Google Play makes it easy to deploy app updates. Auto-updates are enabled by default on every Android device with Google Play installed. Just publish your app update through the Google Play Console, and Google Play will automatically do the rest. It might take a few days for your app to be updated on every device. This is because Google Play waits for the optimal time to update an app, such as when the device is charging and on Wi-Fi. Best practices for managed Google Play app updates The Intune team has met with the Play Store team at Google to get clarity on the expected behavior for app updates in managed Google Play. Per Google, the timing for when an app update gets installed onto a device is dependent upon several factors that Play takes into consideration to determine the optimal time to apply the app update on the device. These factors include: Battery level CPU load Network connection type and strength Whether the device is idle or in use Whether or not the app to be updated is actively being used in the foreground To ensure the best results for app updates, Google recommends the following: Set the following configuration settings within Intune for your devices. Both of these settings are Android Device Owner Device configuration settings under the General category: Set App auto-updates to Wi-Fi only or Always depending on your needs - this device setting instructs Play to ignore network connection heuristics when determining when to install an update. Note that setting this to Always will cause Play to update apps over mobile data if your device is configured for that, which may incur additional cost. Set System update to a specific Maintenance window - This setting controls when pending system updates get applied, but it also allows apps to update if they are in the foreground of the device. For example, if an app is being actively used and an update is available, Play will close the app and update it during the maintenance window. Otherwise, Play will wait until the app is no longer in use before it updates it in order to minimize disruption for the user on the device. Set the following configuration settings within Intune for your devices. Both of these settings are Android Device Owner Device configuration settings under the General category: Ensure that devices are plugged in periodically and are charged - Play has internal logic to prioritize app updates when a device is plugged in, and when the battery is sufficiently charged. Ensure that there are no pending permission updates for apps - if the developer publishes a new version of an app which requires a new Android runtime permission, Play will prevent the update until the admin allows the new permission. You can set up automatic permission reapproval to automatically approve new permissions, and also sign up for email updates to get notified when new permissions are requested. See here for information on that: https://support.google.com/googleplay/work/answer/6137917?hl=en Next steps: How to proceed if you are seeing app update delays Google has agreed to hear feedback and work together with the Intune team and Intune customers to make improvements to the Play app update behavior. The following information would be helpful as a next step: If you have followed the best practices above but are still having issues with app updates, Google requests that a device bug report be obtained. This is the key logging information that they have asked for in order to troubleshoot. How to obtain a device bug report: https://developer.android.com/studio/debug/bug-report Google and Intune would also like to understand your typical device lifecycle. This information can be used to plan features and further improvements. For example: How often are devices plugged in to be recharged? Eg, nightly, on-demand (such as whenever the battery runs low), between shifts? Do devices have batteries swapped without ever being plugged in? What percentage of time are devices reliably connected to a Wi-Fi and/or reliable mobile data network? Any other details about the typical device lifecycle that you feel is relevant

symptom: Thanks to automation added to ContentIdea and the power of Microsoft Teams, it is now easier than ever before to stay up-to-date on all the latest Intune support issues, KB articles and content changes. Starting now you can get a timely, once-a-day notification of all new or updated support content delivered automatically right to your desktop. This is not enabled for you by default, however if you choose to opt-in you will get a notification in Teams (or an email if you are logged out of Teams) once per day telling you about any articles published in the last 24 hours. See below for details and instructions on how you can opt-in.

This is the ideal method for updating the Intune certificate connector to ensure a clean as possible update to minimize problems.1) Uninstall the Microsoft Intune connector from the Control panel.2) Once uninstall is finished go to portal.azure.com and log in.3) Go to Microsoft Intune -> Device configuration -> Certificate Connectors4) Delete any inactive connector objects present.5) Click add and download the latest version of the Intune connector.6) Install the new Intune connector software on the NDES server.7) Once install is complete open the UI and log in with your Intune Admin account to complete setup.

On computers running Windows 10 build 1709 or later, if they are configured to receive updates from Windows Update (usually Windows Update for Business or WUfB), servicing and definition updates are installed successfully however feature updates are never offered. You will also find error 0x80070426 in the WindowsUpdate.log file. This error code translates to:ERROR_SERVICE_NOT_ACTIVE - # The service has not been started.
rootCause: This can occur if the Microsoft Account Sign In Assistant (MSA or wlidsvc) is not running. The DCAT Flighting service (ServiceId: 855E8A7C-ECB4-4CA3-B045-1DFA50104289) relies on the Microsoft Account Sign In Assistant (MSA) to get the Global Device ID for the device. Without the MSA service running, the global device ID will not be generated and sent by the client, thus the search for feature updates never completes successfully.
solution: To resolve this issue, reset the MSA service to the default StartType of Manual.In Intune we have the following configuration in Configuration Profile -> Device Restriction -> Cloud and Storage -> Microsoft Account sign-in assistant:The OMA-URI information is below.https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/client-management/mdm/policy-csp-accounts#accounts-allowmicrosoftaccountsigninassistantAccounts/AllowMicrosoftAccountSignInAssistantHomeProBusinessEnterpriseEducationMobileMobile Enterprise222222Scope:DeviceAdded in Windows 10, version 1703. Allows IT Admins the ability to disable the "Microsoft Account Sign-In Assistant" (wlidsvc) NT service.

symptom: After making edits to the column widths and/or filters for a specific section of the Intune on Azure portal, the changes are not saved when they navigating away from the blade and back, or after signing out and signing back in again.
rootCause: This is a known issue.
solution: Currently there is no known fix or work around for this behavior.

symptom: After deploying a PKCS profile to issue certificates to mobile devices, the certificates are not acquired and the following errors are seen in NDESConnector_YYYY-MM-DD_XXXXX.svclog: Log Entries: <E2ETraceEvent xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2004/06/E2ETraceEvent"><System xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2004/06/windows/eventlog/system"><EventID>1</EventID><Type>3</Type><SubType Name="Error">0</SubType><Level>2</Level><TimeCreated SystemTime="2019-01-10T18:18:05.4771468Z" /><Source Name="NDESConnectorService" /><Correlation ActivityID="{00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000}" /><Execution ProcessName="NDESConnector" ProcessID="3252" ThreadID="18" /><Channel /><Computer>PKCS-Server</Computer></System><ApplicationData>IssuePfx - The submission is pending: Taken Under Submission</ApplicationData></E2ETraceEvent> <E2ETraceEvent xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2004/06/E2ETraceEvent"><System xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2004/06/windows/eventlog/system"><EventID>1</EventID><Type>3</Type><SubType Name="Error">0</SubType><Level>2</Level><TimeCreated SystemTime="2019-01-10T18:18:05.4771468Z" /><Source Name="NDESConnectorService" /><Correlation ActivityID="{00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000}" /><Execution ProcessName="NDESConnector" ProcessID="3252" ThreadID="18" /><Channel /><Computer>PKCS-SERVER</Computer></System><ApplicationData>IssuePfx -Generic Exception: System.InvalidOperationException: IssuePfx - The submission is pending at Microsoft.Management.Services.NdesConnector.MicrosoftCA.GetCertificate(PfxRequestDataStorage pfxRequestData, String& certificate, String& password)</ApplicationData></E2ETraceEvent> <E2ETraceEvent xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2004/06/E2ETraceEvent"><System xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2004/06/windows/eventlog/system"><EventID>1</EventID><Type>3</Type><SubType Name="Information">0</SubType><Level>8</Level><TimeCreated SystemTime="2019-01-10T18:18:05.4927737Z" /><Source Name="NDESConnectorService" /><Correlation ActivityID="{00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000}" /><Execution ProcessName="NDESConnector" ProcessID="3252" ThreadID="18" /><Channel /><Computer>PKCS-SERVER</Computer></System><ApplicationData>Deleting one certificate from store 'LocalMachine/REQUEST' with DN 'CN=user1'</ApplicationData></E2ETraceEvent> <E2ETraceEvent xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2004/06/E2ETraceEvent"><System xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/2004/06/windows/eventlog/system"><EventID>1</EventID><Type>3</Type><SubType Name="Error">0</SubType><Level>2</Level><TimeCreated SystemTime="2019-01-10T18:18:05.4927737Z" /><Source Name="NDESConnectorService" /><Correlation ActivityID="{00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000}" /><Execution ProcessName="NDESConnector" ProcessID="3252" ThreadID="18" /><Channel /><Computer>PKCS-SERVER</Computer></System><ApplicationData>Failed to issue Pfx certificate for Device ID XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX : </ApplicationData></E2ETraceEvent> On the Certificate Authority you will also see the PFX request being received but waiting for approval to issue the certificate:
rootCause: This can occur if the option �Set the request status to pending. The administrator must explicitly issue the certificate� is selected as like the screenshot below. This is in Certificate Authority properties under Policy Module -> Properties, By default, all requests reaching CA for certificate will be moved to a pending state waiting for administrator approval even if the template is set for no admin approval required.
solution: To resolve this issue, in the CA Properties under policy module -> Properties, change the option to �Follow the settings in certificate template. If applicable. Otherwise, automatically issue the certificate�.Once the option is changed, you should see the PKCS certificate profile deployment status show as Success in the portal and certificate issuing information in NDESConnector_YYYY-MM-DD_XXXXX.svclog:

symptom: You cannot enroll Windows 10 devices. After entering the user credentials under Windows Settings -> Accounts -> Access work or school -> Connect you receive the following error message:You're all set! We encountered an error enrolling your device into your organization's management service. So, we weren't able to register your device and add your account to Windows.Your access to org resources may be limited. Contact your organization's helpdesk for assistance Additional problem informationError code:Correlation ID: <Guid>Timestamp: More Information: https://www.microsoft.com/wamerrorsServer Message: Unknown error code: 0x80180014You will also see an Event ID 71 error in Applications and Services Logs-> Microsoft-> Windows-> DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostics-Provider->Admin log with the following description:MDM Enroll: Failed (Unknown Win32 Error code: 0x80180014)
rootCause: This issue can occur if Windows MDM enrollment is disabled in your Intune tenant, or if there is a custom restriction assigned to a group containing the affected user that is blocking Windows MDM enrollment.
solution: Sign in to the Azure portal as administrator. Select Intune, and then go to Device enrollment > Enrollment restrictions. In Device Type Restrictions, check all "Restrictions" created (including Default) click Platforms, and then select Allow for Windows (MDM). Click Save.

symptom: This article provides instruction on how to create a request for access to the Azure Support Center. Note1: To get access to ASC see Intune: New process to request access to Azure Support Center (2/6/2023). Note2: For more information about ASC see ASC Documentation | Azure CXP Support Docs.
Android APK app update fails with 'App installation failed' notification on user devices
rootCause: Users have removed the app from their devices. When Intune tries to update the app, the update fails because the app is no longer installed.
solution: Have the users reinstall the app from the Company Portal to their devices.

symptom: Applications don't appear as Available in the Intune Company Portal app on Windows 10 devices
rootCause: Users haven't identified their device in the Information Worker portal (IW portal) at https://portal.manage.microsoft.com.
solution: Users must go to portal.manage.microsoft.com → My Devices → Tap here → select their device to identify it. After identification, apps will appear on the Apps screen.

symptom: Cannot save corporate data in WIP-allowed apps (Word, Excel) on Hybrid Azure AD joined or co-managed devices. Error: 'Word cannot complete the save due to a file permission error' or Error 0x8007177C about invalid recovery certificate.
rootCause: The Encrypting File System (EFS) Data Recovery Agent (DRA) certificate in the organization's Group Policy has expired, preventing file encryption.
solution: 1) Export old DRA certificate and private key for decrypting existing files. 2) Create and verify a new EFS DRA certificate. 3) Update the Group Policy to use the new DRA certificate.

symptom: macOS devices are unexpectedly unenrolled from Microsoft Intune service or enrollment fails
rootCause: MDM agent mishandles failed MDM certificate installations. When the MDM agent doesn't receive expected headers, the client automatically removes its MDM enrollment profile.
solution: Re-enroll the affected macOS device.

symptom: Samsung Android Enterprise fully managed devices running Android 11+ show as noncompliant after a managed update is applied, potentially affecting access to corporate resources via Conditional Access
rootCause: Known Samsung issue with compliance state after managed update on fully managed devices.
solution: For DA and work profile: upgrade Company Portal to version 5.0.5358.0 (fix released Dec 2021). For fully managed: issue still active, monitor Intune Customer Success blog for updates.

symptom: Approximately 1% of iOS 13+ devices enrolled in Intune cannot perform password reset - the passcode reset token is not returned
rootCause: Apple bug in iOS 13 where the token needed to allow password reset is not returned for some enrolled devices. Fixed in iOS 13.3.1, but already-enrolled devices need re-enrollment.
solution: 1) Update device to iOS 13.3.1 or later. 2) Remove and re-enroll the device in Intune. Use PowerShell script to identify affected devices (see Intune Customer Success blog).

symptom: Profile error 'Invalid Profile: The configuration for your iPad/iPhone could not be downloaded' when enrolling iOS devices with Apple Configurator for Setup Assistant enrollment
rootCause: Invalid enrollment URL used in Apple Configurator setup.
solution: See workaround in Intune Customer Success blog post for correcting the enrollment URL.

symptom: Users are signed out of ALL Office mobile apps on iOS once they sign out of a single Office app (or auto-signed out), forced to reauthenticate, sometimes leading to authentication loop
rootCause: Known issue with app protection policies (MAM) on iOS where signing out of one Office app triggers sign-out from all managed Office apps.
solution: See workaround in Intune Customer Success blog post.

symptom: Samsung work profile devices missing certificates after updating to Android 12, affecting Gmail and AnyConnect VPN access
rootCause: Samsung Android 12 update issue causing certificate loss on work profile enrolled devices.
solution: See temporary workarounds in Intune Customer Success blog. Issue has been resolved.

symptom: 'Rename device' setting disabled for Windows devices that are Hybrid Azure AD joined in Intune admin center
rootCause: Renaming Hybrid Azure AD joined devices can cause device single sign-on errors after user password change. Microsoft intentionally disabled this feature.
solution: This is by design. Device renaming is only available for co-managed devices that are Azure AD joined (not hybrid joined).

symptom: Cannot uninstall iOS apps installed from Apple App Store using Intune - only MDM-deployed apps can be removed
rootCause: By design: Intune can only uninstall apps deployed through the MDM channel. Apps installed directly from App Store are not MDM-managed.
solution: 1) Add the apps to Intune and assign as Available or Required (prompts user to allow Intune management). 2) Change assignment type to Uninstall. For DEP supervised devices, management takeover prompt is skipped.

symptom: OneDrive configured as managed app with APP policy allows users to attach corporate protected files via iMessage using Open-In feature
rootCause: The Open-In feature is controlled by the OS, not by Intune APP. App protection policies cannot prevent this behavior.
solution: Use MDM General Intune iOS Configuration policy: set 'Allow managed documents in other unmanaged apps' and 'Allow unmanaged documents in other managed apps' to No.

symptom: 'Mismatch between the value type and the configuration value' error when iOS app configuration policy contains URL with ampersand (&) character
rootCause: iOS configuration profiles don't support the ampersand character in XML configuration files.
solution: Replace '&' with '&' in the URL, or create a short URL redirect that avoids the ampersand.

symptom: Intune APP policy 'Select which storage services corporate data can be saved to' does not prevent saving images from email messages locally in Outlook for iOS
rootCause: By design: the data relocation storage restriction setting does not apply to images in email messages in Outlook for iOS.
solution: No workaround available. This is by design limitation of current APP policy for Outlook iOS images.

symptom: macOS LOB app (.pkg) not installed on targeted devices with no error messages shown in Intune
rootCause: The .pkg package is missing required information: package version/CFBundleVersion strings and correct install-location (/Applications or subfolder).
solution: Contact app developer to rebuild .pkg package with correct pkg-info containing CFBundleVersion and install-location set to /Applications. Verify with: xar -x -f <pkg> -C <output> and check output for required fields.

symptom: Error 0x87D13BA2 'One or more apps contain invalid bundleIDs' when deploying macOS LOB app, even if app is successfully installed
rootCause: Multiple applications included in the macOS app package; not all individual applications report installation status back to Intune.
solution: 1) Run 'sudo /usr/libexec/mdmclient QueryInstalledApps > InstalledApps.txt' on device. 2) Compare installed apps list with Intune's included apps. 3) Remove apps from Intune that don't appear in InstalledApps.txt. 4) Save and sync device.

symptom: macOS 11.2.x devices: apps can't be downloaded/installed (Install Pending indefinitely), shell scripts don't run, Intune management agent installation fails. Error 506 'Install request is a duplicate' in device console log.
rootCause: Known macOS Big Sur 11.2.x bug causing ASDErrorDomain Code=506 duplicate install request errors.
solution: Upgrade the device to macOS 11.3 or later version.

symptom: Microsoft 365 apps for macOS close without notification and restart unexpectedly (e.g., Teams closes during a call)
rootCause: Multiple deployments of Microsoft 365 apps assigned as Required to the device, or known issue with the M365 Apps for macOS suite assigned as Required.
solution: 1) If multiple Required deployments: assign only one deployment. 2) If M365 suite assigned as Required: change assignment type to 'Available for enrolled devices'.

symptom: Microsoft 365 apps on macOS close without notification, update, and restart unexpectedly (no MAU deadline notification shown)
rootCause: Multiple configuration profiles managing Microsoft AutoUpdate (MAU) settings are deployed, or a single profile contains too many per-app MAU settings causing conflict.
solution: Deploy only one configuration profile for MAU settings. Include only MAU settings that apply to all apps in the Microsoft 365 Apps for macOS suite.

symptom: Intune App Wrapping Tool for Android crashes with 'The application could not be wrapped' error and ExceptionWithContext: Unsigned short value out of range
rootCause: The LOB app reaches or nearly reaches the 64K method reference limit of DEX files, preventing the wrapping tool from adding Intune code.
solution: 1) Install latest App Wrapping Tool version. 2) Enable multidex for the Android app. 3) If persists, reduce classes in primary DEX file and enable ProGuard code shrinking.

symptom: Outlook for Android shows 'Your organization has removed its data associated with this app' error, requiring re-sign-in
rootCause: Outlook went offline for too long (signed out by APP policy), or Outlook cannot detect Company Portal after system update and loses expected access token.
solution: 1) Turn off battery optimization for Outlook and Company Portal. 2) Change app Launch from Automatic to Manage Manually (enable Auto-launch, Secondary launch, Run in background). 3) Enable unrestricted data usage if Data Saver is on. 4) Enable notifications for both apps.

symptom: Discovered apps list in Intune shows what appear to be personal apps on Android Enterprise work profile devices
rootCause: By design: these are system apps/services duplicated in the work profile by the device OEM, not actual personal apps. Intune cannot inventory personal profile apps.
solution: No action needed. Verify by checking Settings > Work Profile > Apps on the device. Work apps show a briefcase icon.

symptom: Cannot attach photos in work profile apps (Word, Excel, Outlook, OneDrive) on Android Enterprise enrolled devices - 'Unable to add attachment due to IO error'
rootCause: Android requires an additional file explorer application in the workspace to open photos for attachment.
solution: Approve and assign a File Explorer app (e.g., Google Files) from managed Google Play store to the device. Then use the app's Attach icon to attach photos.

symptom: Outlook for Android shows 'This action is not allowed by your organization' when creating new email after APP policy is applied
rootCause: User signed in with Exchange Server on-premises account directly. Only Microsoft Entra accounts are supported by Intune APP; Exchange direct on-premises accounts are not supported.
solution: Sign in with a Microsoft Entra account after configuring Exchange hybrid Modern Authentication.

symptom: 'Safari cannot open the page because the address is invalid' error when opening a web app on iOS device
rootCause: Web app policy has 'Require a managed browser to open this link' set to Yes, but Intune Managed Browser for iOS is not installed on the device.
solution: Either install the Intune Managed Browser on the iOS device, or set 'Require a managed browser to open this link' to No in the policy.

symptom: Error 0x87D1FDE8 displayed in Intune admin console after deploying Managed Browser policy with allowlist URLs or during app installation
rootCause: Known issue in Microsoft Intune with Managed Browser policy deployment. The error is cosmetic and does not affect actual functionality.
solution: The error resolves automatically after the device checks in again. No action needed - safe to ignore.

symptom: Only one Intune app configuration policy is applied to Microsoft Edge or Managed Browser when multiple configuration profiles targeting the same settings are assigned
rootCause: Intune does not support merging Edge or Managed Browser app configuration policies. When multiple policies with overlapping settings target the same user, only one is applied.
solution: Configure each setting in only a single app configuration policy per user/group. Use exclusion groups to ensure no user is targeted by multiple policies with the same settings.

symptom: Save application failed. You don't have enough permissions to update this application error when updating an app in Intune with a custom role
rootCause: The application's targeted groups are not included in Scope (Groups) of the custom Intune role assignment.
solution: Add the application's targeted groups to Scope (Groups) in the custom role assignment.

symptom: Microsoft Store for Business volume-purchased apps do not synchronize and do not appear in the Intune portal
rootCause: The apps use the encrypted app package format (EAppxBundle), which is not supported for Intune synchronization.
solution: Distribute the affected apps through the private store instead of relying on Intune sync.

symptom: Samsung Galaxy Note 4 users not prompted for fingerprint authentication in Intune APP-managed apps despite 'Allow fingerprint instead of PIN' set to Yes
rootCause: Samsung Galaxy Note 4 uses a proprietary fingerprint management API not supported by Intune
solution: Use an alternate device that supports a fingerprint management API supported by Intune

symptom: Cannot assign SCEP certificates to devices after certificate renewal - NDESPlugin.log stops at 'Sending request to certificate registration point', CAPI2 log shows 'certificate not within validity period'
rootCause: NDES policy module still uses the thumbprint from an expired client authentication certificate that was selected during initial NDES/Intune Certificate Connector installation
solution: On NDES server: find expired client auth cert in certlm.msc → renew with new key → reinstall Intune Certificate Connector to link to new cert → restart Intune Connector Service and World Wide Web Publishing Service

symptom: iOS SCEP enrollment fails - IIS logs show good GetCACerts (200) but no GetCACaps request generated, device console shows 'The Registration Authority response is invalid' and SecTrustEvaluate errors
rootCause: Trusted Root certificate profile in Intune uses a different cert than NDES server's actual root (commonly an intermediate cert uploaded instead of root), or Root/Intermediate CA uses unsupported signature algorithm (RSASSA-PSS or SHA-1)
solution: Reissue new Root and Intermediate certificates with supported signature algorithm (e.g. SHA256RSA), redeploy on NDES server, ensure NDES-related certs reference the new trusted certs

symptom: SCEP certificate profile shows Failed; IIS logs show HTTP 500 on /CertificateRegistrationSvc/Certificate/VerifyRequest; Event ID 5009 (SCEP app pool crash) and Event ID 1310 (error code 3007 compilation error)
rootCause: CertificateRegistrationSvc app pool account (e.g. Network Service) missing Write permission on C:\Windows\Temp folder
solution: Grant Write permission to Network Service on C:\Windows\Temp; ensure no policies remove it afterward. Note: only affects legacy certificate connector.

symptom: Devices cannot obtain SCEP certificates; CA shows error 0x80094800 CERTSRV_E_UNSUPPORTED_CERT_TYPE; NDES Application log Event ID 31 with 0x80004005
rootCause: NDES registry template name mismatch - using friendly name instead of actual template name in HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MSCEP (SignatureTemplate/EncryptionTemplate/GeneralPurposeTemplate)
solution: Correct the registry values under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MSCEP to match the actual certificate template name on the CA, then restart Intune Connector Service

symptom: SCEP certificate deployment fails to Windows 10 devices after CA certificate renewal; Event 32, 307 in DeviceManagement-Enterprise-Diagnostic-Provider; Event 30 in CAPI2 with 800B0109 (root not trusted)
rootCause: RA certificates on NDES server still reference old CA certificate after CA renewal, causing trust chain failure
solution: Reinstall both the NDES server role and Microsoft Intune Certificate Connector to reissue RA certificates. Does not affect Android/iOS.

symptom: NDES connector installation fails with error 0x80090014 CryptAcquireContext failed in SetupMSI.log; NDES certificate not installed
rootCause: NDES certificate template is not a v2 (or later) template; CryptAcquireContext fails with legacy v1 templates
solution: Create the NDES certificate as a v2 template (Windows Server 2008 R2 or 2012 R2 template). Add Client Authentication as Enhanced Key Usage.

symptom: Intune client connectivity issues: devices cannot access Intune infrastructure, receive updates/policies, or complete new enrollments
rootCause: Automatic root certificate update mechanism disabled and Baltimore CyberTrust root certificate not installed on client
solution: Install latest root certificates from Microsoft Update Catalog (search 'root update'); install only the latest cumulative package. Or re-enable automatic root certificate update mechanism.

symptom: SCEP certificate request fails during CRP verification phase; Android/iOS devices do not receive certificates; CRP logs show CryptographicException: m_safeCertContext is an invalid handle
rootCause: NDES connector registry keys under HKLM\Software\Microsoft\MicrosoftIntune\NDESConnector are missing (signing certificate references not created)
solution: Solution 1: Restart Intune Connector Service and verify registry keys are created. Solution 2: Reset MSCEP template to default IPSECIntermediateOffline, restart server, verify signing certs appear, then set custom template name back.

symptom: SCEP certificate reporting phase fails: no new files appear in Succeed folder under CertificateRequestStatus; files stuck in Processing folder
rootCause: Intune Connector Service not started on NDES server, or errors in Ndesconnector.svclog preventing certificate status upload
solution: Verify Intune Connector Service is started. Check Ndesconnector.svclog for errors. Review IIS logs for POST to /CertificateRegistrationSvc/Certificate/Notify returning 204.

symptom: PKCS certificate deployment fails with error 0x800706BA RPC server unavailable; trusted root cert on device but PFX cert missing
rootCause: Cause 1: PKCS profile has wrong CA FQDN/name. Cause 2: CA cert renewed but UseDefinedCACertInRequest not enabled.
solution: Cause 1: Fix Certification authority and name in PKCS profile. Cause 2: Run certutil -setreg ca\UseDefinedCACertInRequest 1 then restart Certificate Services.

symptom: PKCS certificate fails with 0x80094015 enrollment policy server cannot be located
rootCause: Connector host cannot locate certificate enrollment policy server
solution: Run Add-CertificateEnrollmentPolicyServer PowerShell cmdlet on connector host.

symptom: PKCS submission pending: Taken Under Submission; PFX request in CA Pending Requests folder; devices cannot acquire certificates
rootCause: CA Policy Module set to require admin to explicitly issue certificate
solution: Change CA Policy Module to Follow the settings in the certificate template or automatically issue.

symptom: PKCS certificate fails with 0x80070057 parameter is incorrect; connector configured but devices get no certs
rootCause: PKCS profile misconfigured: wrong CA name or SAN for email but user has no email (null SAN)
solution: Verify PKCS profile CA name, user group assignment, and that users have valid email addresses.

symptom: PKCS certificate denied by Policy Module; device gets trusted root but not PFX
rootCause: Computer Account of connector server lacks Read and Enroll permissions on certificate template
solution: Add connector server Computer Account to cert template Security tab with Read + Enroll permissions, then re-issue template.

symptom: PKCS certificate profile stuck as Pending in Intune admin center; no obvious errors in logs
rootCause: Cause 1: PfxRequest files stuck in Failed/Processing folders. Cause 2: Wrong CA or root cert associated with PKCS profile.
solution: Check %programfiles%\Microsoft Intune\PfxRequest folders for errors. Verify trusted cert profile and PKCS profile reference correct CA.

symptom: PKCS fails with -2146875374 CERTSRV_E_SUBJECT_EMAIL_REQUIRED: Email name unavailable cannot be added to Subject
rootCause: Supply in the request option not enabled on certificate template Subject Name tab
solution: Open cert template Properties > Subject Name tab > select Supply in the request > re-issue template.

symptom: Intune Certificate Connector Setup Wizard ends prematurely; SetupMSI.log shows Error 0x80070002 Site not found and ConfigureIISLimits error 1168
rootCause: Default Web Site name in IIS Manager was renamed from its default value
solution: Open IIS Manager, find site with ID=1, rename back to Default Web Site, restart NDESConnectorSetup.exe.

symptom: NDES Connector UI sign-in error 0x80004003 unexpected error; svclog shows NDES Connector certificate could not be found
rootCause: Sign-in account lacks valid Intune license
solution: Assign valid Intune license to sign-in account (must be Intune Service Admin or Global Admin).

symptom: Intune Connector for Active Directory installed but not visible in Intune portal; ODJ event log shows Failed to get value for Key OdjServiceBaseUrl
rootCause: Proxy server blocks connector communication with Intune service; proxy not configured in ODJConnectorSvc.exe.config
solution: Add proxy config to ODJConnectorSvc.exe.config with system.net/defaultProxy element, then restart ODJConnector Service.

symptom: Intune Connector for AD sign-in unexpected error: An error occurred while processing your request
rootCause: Sign-in account not assigned Intune or Microsoft Office license
solution: Assign Intune license to the user account.

symptom: Co-management: Hybrid Azure AD join auto MDM enroll fails with error 0x8018002a. AADSTS50076 requiring MFA.
rootCause: MFA is Enforced (not just Enabled), preventing ConfigMgr client agent from enrolling.
solution: Set MFA to Enabled but not Enforced, or temporarily disable via Trusted IPs.

symptom: Co-management: Device sync fails after auto-enrollment. Error 0xcaa2000c interaction_required.
rootCause: MFA or CA policies requiring MFA applied to all cloud apps block user token.
solution: Disable per-user MFA, use Trusted IPs, or exclude Intune app from CA policies requiring MFA.

symptom: Co-management: Enroll fails with 0x800706D9 or 0x80180023. dmwappushservice missing.
rootCause: dmwappushservice service is missing from the device.
solution: Export dmwappushservice reg key from working device, merge on affected, restart twice with AD cleanup.

symptom: Co-management: Hybrid AAD join fails 0x801c03f2. Public key user certificate not found.
rootCause: UserCertificate attribute missing or not synced to Azure AD.
solution: dsregcmd /leave, delete MS-Organization-Access cert, restart, verify cert on AD object, delta sync.

symptom: Device registration fails 0x80280036. TPM FIPS mode.
rootCause: TPM has FIPS mode enabled, not supported for Azure device registration.
solution: Disable FIPS mode on TPM.

symptom: Hybrid AAD join fails 0x80090016 Keyset does not exist.
rootCause: Windows is not TPM owner. TPM init failed.
solution: Clear TPM via Windows Security. Disable BitLocker first. Restart.

symptom: Co-management bootstrap: Azure AD users not in ConfigMgr DB. Error 0x87d00231.
rootCause: API permissions or Azure AD user Discovery not configured.
solution: Configure API permissions and Azure AD user Discovery in ConfigMgr.

symptom: CMG connection point shows disconnected.
rootCause: Permissions issue: remote site system cannot access primary site inboxes.
solution: Add remote site system machine account to Local Admins on primary site.

symptom: Clients cannot locate MP via CMG, error 403 CMGConnector_Clientcertificaterequired.
rootCause: CRL validation failure for client certificate. Revocation server offline.
solution: Ensure CRL distribution points accessible or temporarily disable CRL checking for CMG.

symptom: ConfigMgr agent unhealthy in Intune, last check-in 2/1/1900.
rootCause: Compliance policies workload managed by ConfigMgr not Intune.
solution: Switch compliance workload to Intune or Pilot Intune.

symptom: Client apps workload not visible in co-management Properties.
rootCause: Pre-release feature not enabled.
solution: Enable pre-release feature in ConfigMgr: Administration > Updates and Servicing > Features.

symptom: Settings not reverted after unassigning Intune policies (no tattoo removal).
rootCause: Tattoo removal not supported when Device Configuration workload is ConfigMgr.
solution: Set Device Configuration workload to Intune, refresh policy on device.

symptom: Email profile: Users repeatedly prompted for password.
rootCause: Certificate profiles not assigned to same group type as email profile.
solution: Assign all cert profiles to same group type (user or device) consistently.

symptom: iOS user-created email profile blocks Intune profile deployment. Not compliant.
rootCause: Duplicate email profile detected based on hostname and email address.
solution: Remove manually created email profile before/after enrollment.

symptom: Samsung KNOX: EAS email profile error 0x87D1FDE8 remediation failed.
rootCause: Samsung Notes sync option selected but no longer supported.
solution: Deselect Samsung Notes sync option. Wait up to 24h.

symptom: Users cannot send images from auto-configured email accounts.
rootCause: Allow e-mail from third-party applications not enabled.
solution: Enable the setting in email profile Properties > Settings.

symptom: Password expiration setting not working. User not prompted after configured days.
rootCause: Editing the policy resets the timer even if the expiration value was not changed.
solution: Avoid editing policy unnecessarily. Expiry = N days after last modified date.

symptom: Deploying device configuration profile to iOS: error 2016341112 iOS device is currently busy. Profile not applied when device is locked.
rootCause: iOS device busy (locked/installing) sends NotNow status via MDM protocol.
solution: Unlock the iOS device. Profile applied automatically once unlocked.

symptom: Android Enterprise fully managed: password settings deployment fails when profile assigned after enrollment.
rootCause: Profile assigned after enrollment does not prompt user during setup.
solution: Assign device restrictions profile with password settings before enrollment.

symptom: Custom VPN profile (OMA-URI) reports 2016281112 Remediation Failed in Intune but VPN works on Win10.
rootCause: Win10 response XML differs from policy XML; Intune interprets mismatch as failure. Known issue up to Win10 1809.
solution: Ignore error or use standard VPN profile instead of custom OMA-URI.

symptom: Enterprise Mode site list not deployed to co-managed Win10 devices via Intune profile.
rootCause: ConfigMgr creates MicrosoftEdge EnterpriseMode SiteList registry that takes precedence over Intune.
solution: Set AllowConfigureMicrosoftEdge=0 under HKLM Policies Microsoft CCM; delete conflicting SiteList.

symptom: FRP emails not enforced as expected on Android Enterprise after factory reset.
rootCause: FRP varies by enrollment type/reset method. COPE: Settings reset enforced; COBO/COSU: only Recovery. Intune wipe does not preserve FRP.
solution: Block Factory reset in restrictions; use Recovery mode for FRP enforcement.

symptom: iOS email profile: no password prompt after password change. No email sent/received.
rootCause: EAS basic authentication limitation does not trigger re-auth prompt.
solution: Enable OAuth in email profile. Ensure email service supports OAuth first.

symptom: iOS Personal Hotspot restriction policy does not block on iOS 12.1 or earlier.
rootCause: Personal Hotspot restriction only enforced starting iOS 12.2.
solution: Upgrade to iOS 12.2 or later.

symptom: Users cannot log on to Entra-joined Windows 10/11 with multi-app kiosk profile assigned. Sign-in fails immediately before user profile loads. Event 31000 AssignedAccess error, 0xCAA2000C interaction_required.
rootCause: Conditional access policies requiring user interaction (MFA or TOU) conflict with multi-app kiosk mode which does not support interactive auth prompts.
solution: Exclude kiosk users from conditional access policies that require user interaction (MFA, TOU). Disable MFA for kiosk accounts.

symptom: Feature updates never offered on Intune-managed Windows 10 v1709+. Servicing/definition updates work. WindowsUpdate.log shows error 80070426 ERROR_SERVICE_NOT_ACTIVE for DCAT Flighting service.
rootCause: Microsoft Account sign-in assistant (MSA/wlidsvc) service disabled via Intune device restriction. DCAT Flighting service depends on MSA for global device ID.
solution: In Intune admin center > Device configuration > Profiles > Device restrictions > Cloud and Storage, set Microsoft Account sign-in assistant to Not configured.

symptom: Skype for Business on Android Enterprise does not save SIP address and password after app close or device restart. Users must re-enter credentials every time.
rootCause: Intune device restriction Add and Remove accounts set to Block prevents credential persistence on Android Enterprise work profile.
solution: In Device configuration > Profiles > Device restrictions > Work profile settings, change Add and Remove accounts to Not Configured.

symptom: Company Portal shows "We cannot update enrollment now. Try again later" notification when enrolling devices in Intune.
rootCause: Outdated version of the Company Portal app on the device.
solution: Update Company Portal to the latest version from the respective app store (Microsoft Store / App Store / Play Store).

symptom: Devices continue trying to enroll in Intune after subscription canceled and different MDM provider selected.
rootCause: CNAME DNS records (enterpriseenrollment/enterpriseregistration) still point to manage.microsoft.com / EnterpriseRegistration.windows.net, redirecting enrollment to Intune.
solution: Delete old CNAME records from domain registrar pointing to manage.microsoft.com and EnterpriseRegistration.windows.net. Set new CNAMEs per new MDM provider.

symptom: Android device cannot sign in to Company Portal: missing required certificate. ADFS intermediate cert not in SSL hello or user cert not trusted by Android 7.0+.
rootCause: ADFS server does not include intermediate certificates in SSL Server hello response; or ADFS uses user certificate (not publicly trusted CA) which Android 7.0+ ignores from user cert store.
solution: Sol 1: User installs missing cert. Sol 2: Import intermediate certs into ADFS Personal cert store, restart ADFS. Sol 3: Use cert chaining to publicly trusted root CA on ADFS.

symptom: Android Enterprise work profile device ownership auto-changed from Personal to Corporate after enrollment.
rootCause: Corporate device identifier (IMEI/serial) added for the device in Intune Admin Center.
solution: Delete the corporate identifier, then change device ownership back to Personal.

symptom: Microsoft Edge on Android 13 ignores PAC setting in per-app VPN profile. Edge does not consume proxy after VPN established.
rootCause: Android 13 behavior change causes Edge to ignore PAC when VPN established before Edge starts.
solution: Disconnect/reconnect VPN while Edge running. For Always-on VPN use airplane mode toggle. Alt: Postpone Android 13 upgrade.

symptom: iOS/iPadOS devices not checking in with Intune: Management State Unhealthy, cannot receive policies/apps/commands, users lose Conditional Access.
rootCause: Device cannot successfully sync with Intune; may not be properly enrolled or lost contact.
