solution: For PKCS renewal: must revoke/remove existing cert first then redeploy. To remove: unassign profile or use Remove-certificates action. For auto-renewal needs, consider SCEP instead (each request generates unique cert with fresh validity). Ref: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/protect/certificates-configure and learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/protect/remove-certificates
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Common TSG/Intune renew PKCS certificate.md

symptom: PFX Certificate connector keeps contacting decommissioned old CA server instead of newly configured CA; certificates fail to issue even though Intune profile points to new CA
rootCause: PFX connector caches unprocessed certificate requests in a 'processing' folder. Old requests downloaded before CA change still reference old CA. Connector retries these 10 times before marking failed, masking the real issue.
solution: Delete contents of the PFX connector 'processing' folder to clear backlog of old CA requests. Connector will then download fresh requests pointing to new CA. Ref ICM-418855554.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Common TSG/Known issue - PFX Certificate connector is pointin.md

symptom: LOB app update behavior varies by platform and assignment type; iOS auto-updates silently for Available assignment while Android prompts user if Check apps from external sources is enabled
rootCause: Platform-specific LOB update handling: iOS auto-updates installed Available LOB apps without notice; Android respects Check apps from external sources setting which prompts user for external app installation
solution: Upload LOB app and replace original deployment (verify version reflects update). iOS Available: silent auto-update. iOS Required: auto-update. Android Available/Required: prompt if Check apps from external sources enabled; disable setting for silent update.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/LOB available app update expected behavior.md

symptom: Mac device needs to re-enroll in Intune or IntuneMdmDaemon process is stuck/unresponsive
rootCause: MDM enrollment profile expired or corrupted, or IntuneMdmDaemon process hung
solution: Run 'sudo profiles renew -type enrollment' to re-enroll Mac in Intune. If IntuneMdmDaemon is stuck, kill it first with 'sudo killall IntuneMdmDaemon'.

symptom: Windows devices unexpectedly join or auto-enroll to wrong Intune tenant (Global vs 21v) in dual-federation environment with SCCM co-existence; VDI session hosts enroll to incorrect cloud
rootCause: Service Connection Point (SCP) registry keys cloned from master/snapshot VDI images point to the wrong Azure AD tenant. Additionally, SCCM auto-enrollment policy collection rules may not properly exclude devices by naming convention (e.g., CN prefix). In dual-fed environments with both Global and 21v SCCM, the enrollment target depends on SCP configuration and SCCM collection membership.
solution: (1) Check SCP registry keys on affected device to verify they point to correct tenant; (2) For VDI, ensure master image SCP registry is cleared or set correctly before snapshot; (3) Verify SCCM collection membership and exclusion rules (e.g., device name = CN excluded from global auto-enrollment); (4) Use customer's PowerBI dashboard to audit device-to-collection mapping; (5) For dual-fed tenants, confirm which domains are whitelisted to route to 21v vs Global.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/EY - 安永.md

symptom: Network connectivity drops during Windows new hire onboarding after Intune deploys SCEP certificate and WiFi profile; WiFi disconnects when authentication method switches from PEAP to EAP-TLS on the same SSID
rootCause: When Intune pushes SCEP+WiFi profile to newly enrolled device, the WiFi authentication method changes from PEAP (user credential) to EAP-TLS (certificate-based) on the same SSID. The transition causes temporary network disconnection because the device needs to re-authenticate with the new method on the same network.
solution: (1) Check WiFi connection timestamp vs auth method switch timing to confirm this is the cause; (2) Customer-developed PowerShell script workaround (coordinated with networking team) handles the auth transition; (3) Consider using separate SSIDs for PEAP and EAP-TLS to avoid in-place auth switching; (4) If using ClearPass NAC, coordinate with networking team to ensure smooth cert-based auth transition.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/Xiaohongshu - 小红书.md

symptom: Apple Business Manager (ABM) automated device enrollment fails with HTTP 404 error
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/Xiaohongshu - 小红书.md

symptom: macOS FileVault profile is installed via Intune but FileVault is not enabled on the device. Kusto DeviceManagementProvider shows iOSPlugin: FileVault payload is installed but FileVault is not enabled. Error code -2016341107 (FileVaultNotEnabled) or -2016341108 (PrerequisitesNotSatisfied).
rootCause: FileVault prerequisites are not met on the device, or the FileVault payload was deployed but the user has not yet enabled FileVault encryption.
solution: Ensure device meets prerequisites. Check Kusto DeviceManagementProvider with device ID and look for iOSPlugin messages. Verify FileVault profile is correctly targeted in device channel (not user channel). Properties like PersonalRecoveryKeyHelpMessage and PersonalRecoveryKeyRotationInMonths are only supported in device channel sessions.

symptom: macOS LOB app (.pkg wrapped to .intunemac) shows Install status in Company Portal even after successful installation. App is usable but status never changes to Re-install. Kusto DeviceManagementProvider shows app as Not Installed.
rootCause: The .pkg file contains multiple app installers (e.g., Wireshark has 3 sub-packages), and not all install to /Applications or its child folders. Intune only reports successful installation when ALL installed apps are detected, and detection requires install-location to be /Applications or a child folder.
solution: Extract the .pkg file using 'xar -x -f <pkg> -C <output>' and verify each sub-package install-location. Ensure all packages install to /Applications or its child folder. If the .pkg contains utilities installing to /usr/local/bin or /Library/Application Support, consider repackaging or using a custom detection script.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Mac/macOS LOB app installation status not correct.md

symptom: NDES service stops issuing SCEP certificates after installing Windows Server update KB5014702 on the CA server. Event log shows DCOM authentication errors (Event ID 10036): server-side authentication level policy requires RPC_C_AUTHN_LEVEL_PKT_INTEGRITY.
rootCause: KB5014702 raises DCOM authentication level on the CA server (CVE-2021-26414 hardening). The NDES server running an older Windows version without matching patches cannot authenticate at the required level.
solution: Three options: 1) Install KB5014702 on the NDES server as well. 2) Uninstall KB5014702 from the CA server (not recommended). 3) Set registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Ole\AppCompat\RequireIntegrityActivationAuthenticationLevel (DWORD) to 0 on the CA server.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/Known issue_ NDES service stop to working after in.md

symptom: NDES server stops issuing SCEP certificates and returns HTTP 500 error. Connector event log shows certificate validation failure. Certificates were renewed but NDES still fails.
rootCause: The root CA CRL (Certificate Revocation List) has expired. Even though NDES certificates were renewed, the CRL distribution point shows expired CRL, causing certificate chain validation to fail.
solution: 1. Check CRL status using PKIVIEW.msc on the CA jump server. 2. On the root CA, run 'certutil -crl' to renew CRL. 3. Check registry for actual CRL storage path (may differ from default). 4. Copy new CRL and publish: 'certutil -dspublish -f <CA-name>-CA.crl <CA-name>-CA'. 5. Verify CRL is renewed in PKIVIEW.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/NEW Intune Cert SCEP troubleshooting workflow.md

symptom: Hybrid Azure AD joined device fails to receive user-based SCEP certificate. SCEP profile is configured and assigned but certificates are not delivered to hybrid AADJ devices.
rootCause: SCEP certificate profile Key Storage Provider (KSP) is set to Windows Hello for Business. On Hybrid AADJ devices where WHfB is enabled via GPO, SCEP cannot use WHfB as KSP.
solution: Change the SCEP certificate profile KSP setting from 'Windows Hello for Business' to 'Enroll to TPM KSP if present, otherwise Software KSP'.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/NEW Intune Cert SCEP troubleshooting workflow.md

symptom: SCEP certificate enrollment fails. Testing external NDES URL returns BadGateway error. Internal NDES URL works fine.
rootCause: Azure AD Application Proxy is misconfigured. When multiple web application proxies are installed, the external NDES URL redirects to the wrong proxy connector.
solution: Create an Application Proxy group in Azure AD > Application Proxy. Modify the NDES enterprise application to use the correct proxy group with the right connector assignment.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/NEW Intune Cert SCEP troubleshooting workflow.md

symptom: iOS devices enrolled via ABM show Azure AD Registered as empty or false in Intune device export. Large number of devices (1400+) affected.
rootCause: iOS devices were activated via ABM but users did not sign into Company Portal app after activation. Multiple enrollment attempts during activation created legacy/residual records, preventing proper Azure AD registration.
solution: Adopt Just in Time (JIT) deployment for ABM enrollment to ensure proper Azure AD registration. Note: previously affected devices may not be recoverable and may need to be re-enrolled.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/奥迪.md

symptom: iOS/iPadOS devices display compliance status as '未评估' (Not Evaluated) in Intune admin portal after ABM enrollment.
rootCause: Same root cause as Azure AD Registered = false issue. Device did not complete expected registration flow after ABM activation — Company Portal was not signed into, so compliance evaluation cannot proceed.
solution: Use Just in Time (JIT) deployment for new ABM enrollments. Already affected devices likely require re-enrollment.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/奥迪.md

symptom: Intune PowerShell scripts using legacy Microsoft Intune application (client) ID (d1ddf0e4-d672-4dae-b554-9d5bdfd93547) stop working or fail authentication after April 2024.
rootCause: Microsoft removed the global Intune PowerShell application (client) ID based authentication method as part of migration to Microsoft Graph SDK-based PowerShell module. The legacy ID is no longer valid.
solution: 1) Create a new app registration in Microsoft Entra admin center. 2) Update all PowerShell scripts to replace the Intune application ID (d1ddf0e4-d672-4dae-b554-9d5bdfd93547) with the new registered application ID. Reference: https://github.com/microsoft/mggraph-intune-samples
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Feature Update/MC721851：Update your PowerShell scripts with a Mic.md

symptom: DDM (Declarative Device Management) policy assigned to all users but some devices do not show the policy under device configuration.
rootCause: Enrollment mechanism and prerequisites not fully established before policy deployment. When device registration/readiness (step 1) is not confirmed, policy assignment (step 2) will have inconsistent results.
solution: Verify enrollment mechanism and device readiness prerequisites before broad DDM policy deployment. Do not deploy policies en masse until enrollment and device state are fully confirmed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Customer Profile/奥迪.md

symptom: Intune NDES/SCEP connector fails to communicate with Intune service. Error: System.NotSupportedException: The ServicePointManager does not support proxies with the xxx scheme. Connector status shows unhealthy.
rootCause: The Unified Intune Certificate Connector only supports proxy in URL format (http:// or https://). If proxy is configured as bare hostname (e.g., app-uat-proxy.bwainet.net) without scheme prefix, the connector throws NotSupportedException.
solution: On NDES server, navigate to C:\Program Files\Microsoft Intune\PFXCertificateConnector\ConnectorUI, open PFXCertificateConnectorUI.exe, edit proxy configuration to URL format: http://"proxy address" or https://"proxy address". Connector status will return to OK after change.

symptom: Windows clients fail to receive SCEP certificate. SCEP profile shows error -2016409340. NDES event log shows 'Failed to verify SCEP request' with ChallengeExpired error, and RPC server unavailable (0x800706ba) Event ID 31.
rootCause: The NDES service account password expired, causing RPC communication failure between NDES and the CA server. SCEP challenge validation also fails because the expired password prevents the connector from communicating with Intune to validate challenges.
solution: Reset the NDES service account password. After password reset, SCEP certificates can be issued and profiles deploy successfully. Note: gMSA (Group Managed Service Account) is NOT supported by the unified Intune cert connector due to a known cert renewal failure issue with no workaround.

symptom: SCEP certificates cannot be issued to clients via NDES. Connector event log shows Event ID 2: 'The client and server cannot communicate, because they do not possess a common algorithm' during SCEP verify request.
rootCause: TLS 1.2 is not enabled or properly configured in .NET Framework on the NDES server. The Intune connector requires TLS 1.2 for communication with Intune service endpoints, but the server defaults to older TLS versions.
solution: Enable TLS 1.2 on the NDES server: 1) Set registry keys HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v4.0.30319 and HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v4.0.30319 with SystemDefaultTlsVersions=1 and SchUseStrongCrypto=1. 2) Enable TLS 1.2 Server and Client under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.2. 3) Restart the server.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/NDES/Unable to issue certs through SCEP _ The client an.md

symptom: SharePoint site can be accessed via web browser but user cannot download/print/sync with error: 'Your organization doesn't allow you to download, print, or sync using this device. To use these actions, use a device that's joined to a domain or marked compliant by Intune'
rootCause: SharePoint admin enabled 'Allow limited, web-only access' for unmanaged devices in SharePoint admin center. This auto-creates 2 Conditional Access policies: one blocks app access from non-hybrid-AADJ/non-Intune-enrolled devices, another enforces app-enforced restrictions on browser sessions preventing download/print/sync from unmanaged devices.
solution: 1. Verify SharePoint admin center > Access control > Unmanaged devices setting. 2. If 'Allow limited, web-only access' is set, two auto-created CA policies exist. 3. To allow download: either enroll device in Intune or Hybrid AAD Join the device to satisfy the CA policy. 4. Alternatively, SharePoint admin can change to 'Allow full access from desktop apps, mobile apps, and the web' if security posture permits.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/SharePoint site can be access via web browser, but.md

symptom: After deploying app as Available type to user group, end user unable to see the app in Company Portal or app takes very long time to appear; Company Portal app list returns 0 results (resultCount=0 in Kusto HttpSubsystem)
rootCause: Effective group membership change has not yet propagated to Intune backend. When user's group membership changes, EffectiveGroupMembershipsUserService takes time to update. Until PolicyAssignmentProvider picks up new effective group, app assignments are not reflected in Company Portal API responses. Even apps assigned to 'All Users' may be affected during membership transition.
solution: 1. Check Kusto HttpSubsystem for Company Portal API calls: filter by deviceId, verify resultCount. 2. Check EffectiveGroupMembershipsUserService_RawData for EffectiveGroupChangeTime. 3. Check PolicyAssignmentProvider for policy assignment count changes. 4. Compare timestamps between group change and Company Portal query. 5. If resultCount=0 after group change, wait for replication (typically minutes, can be hours). 6. For apps assigned to 'All Users', verify the assignment is effective regardless of group membership change.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Troubleshooting Available app deploy to user group (2).md

symptom: Hybrid AADJ Autopilot provisioning delays 3-4 hours; device stuck at ESP Account Setup phase waiting for Azure AD registration
rootCause: The Automatic-Device-Join scheduled task under \Microsoft\Windows\Workplace Join is disabled after domain join. The task should be enabled by default after domain join + GPO apply, but remains disabled for ~2 hours, preventing timely HAADJ registration.
solution: Workaround: Deploy Intune PowerShell script during WhiteGlove to run 'Enable-ScheduledTask -TaskPath "\Microsoft\Windows\Workplace Join" -TaskName "Automatic-Device-Join"' to force-enable the task immediately after domain join, speeding up HAADJ registration.

symptom: Windows Intune enrollment fails; OMA-DM sync does not execute after omadmprc.exe activation; SessionState set to 5 prematurely; error SCHED_E_UNKNOWN_OBJECT_VERSION (0x80041313)
rootCause: A corrupted scheduled task trigger registry entry (e.g., Media Center RecordingRestart task) has invalid trigger version byte (0xFF instead of 0x17/0x16/0x15) at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Schedule\TaskCache\Tasks\{TaskGUID}\Triggers. Task Scheduler returns SCHED_E_UNKNOWN_OBJECT_VERSION to omadmprc.exe, which interprets it as activation failure and sets SessionState=5 (completed). When omadmclient.exe starts, it sees SessionState=5 and skips SyncML.
solution: Identify the corrupted task trigger registry entry. Fix or delete the invalid Triggers binary value under the offending TaskCache\Tasks\{GUID}. The corrupted task is unrelated to Intune (e.g., Media Center) but blocks OMA-DM session activation globally.

symptom: Microsoft Intune Enrollment service principal (AppId d4ebce55-015a-49b5-a083-c84d1797ae8c) missing from Azure AD; MDM auto-enrollment fails
rootCause: The 'Microsoft Intune Enrollment' enterprise application was accidentally deleted from Azure AD, breaking MDM enrollment flow.
solution: 1) Go to Azure portal > Azure Active Directory > MDM > select 'Microsoft Intune Enrollment' > Delete. 2) Open admin PowerShell: Connect-AzureAD, then run New-AzureADServicePrincipal -AppId d4ebce55-015a-49b5-a083-c84d1797ae8c. Verify with Get-AzureADServicePrincipal | Where-Object DisplayName -like '*Intune*'.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Windows/How to add back Microsoft Intune enrollment.md

symptom: Intune-managed Windows device cannot receive remote commands or policy updates immediately; device only syncs at default 8-hour check-in interval
rootCause: Windows Push Notification Services (WNS) channel is not working. Common causes: (1) HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments\*\Push key missing or channel expired; (2) WNS service not running or unstable; (3) Dependency services disabled — 'Network Connection Broker' (ncbservice) or 'Microsoft Account Sign-in Assistant'; (4) Microsoft Account Sign-in Assistant disabled prevents device compact ticket generation, causing Event 1116 error 0x80070426.
solution: 1) Check Push reg key exists under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments\{EnrollmentID}\Push and verify expiry time is in future. 2) Verify WNS service is running and stable. 3) Ensure dependency services are started: ncbservice and Microsoft Account Sign-in Assistant. 4) Check netstat for ESTABLISHED connections to WNS IPs (e.g., 20.198.x.x). 5) Check Event Viewer > Microsoft-Windows-PushNotification-Platform Operational for Event ID 1116. 6) Verify device ticket at HKU\.DEFAULT\Software\Microsoft\IdentityCRL\Immersive\production\Token.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Windows/Intune Remote command Push and Policy assignment u (2).md

symptom: Users blocked from launching MAM-protected iOS/Android apps after June 2024; app fails to start with MAM policy enforcement error
rootCause: iOS apps using Intune SDK or App Wrapping Tool version older than 17.7.0. Android Company Portal not updated to v5.0.6198.0. Microsoft enforced minimum SDK/wrapper version starting June 1, 2024 (MC775341).
solution: Update iOS LOB apps to Intune SDK/wrapper v17.7.0+ (recommended: SDK 19.2.0). Update Android Company Portal to v5.0.6198.0+. Use Conditional Launch settings: Min SDK version to block apps with SDK < 17.7.0, Min Company Portal version to warn users on CP < 5.0.6198.0.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Feature Update/MC775341_ Update to the latest Company Portal for.md

symptom: Newly enrolled Apple (iOS/macOS) devices not appearing in Entra dynamic groups after June 2024; device registration incomplete despite successful MDM enrollment
rootCause: Starting June 2024 (MC777834), Apple device registration was decoupled from enrollment — registration now occurs AFTER enrollment completes. Users must return to Company Portal to finish registration. If user does not return, device is enrolled but not registered, so dynamic groups based on device properties do not populate.
solution: Ensure users return to Company Portal after enrollment to complete registration. Alternatives: (1) Migrate to web-based enrollment with Just-In-Time registration (auto-registers during normal use); (2) Enable Conditional Access policies to force users back to Company Portal; (3) Migrate from dynamic groups to assignment filters (no registration dependency). macOS Company Portal auto-completes registration if not manually closed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Feature Update/MC777834：Update for Apple user and device enrollme.md

symptom: Cannot deploy printer drivers to non-administrator users via Intune after KB5005652 security update; Point and Print driver installation blocked
rootCause: KB5005652 (CVE-2021-34481) changed default behavior to restrict printer driver installation to administrators only. Non-admin users can no longer install printer drivers via Point and Print.
solution: Option 1: Deploy PowerShell script via Intune (run as SYSTEM) to set registry HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers\PointAndPrint\RestrictDriverInstallationToAdministrators=0 and create scheduled task to run Add-Printer -ConnectionName at logon. Option 2: Run elevated PowerShell as logon user to Add-Printer. Option 3: Use Intune Device Configuration Profile (Printer settings under device restrictions, works for user groups only).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## How To/## Windows/Deploy Printer Driver via Intune.md

symptom: Company Portal (CP) unable to sign in on iOS/Android in 21Vianet (Mooncake) environment; error message: 'This username may be incorrect. Make sure you typed it correctly'
rootCause: User's domain was previously verified in Global Azure AD tenant. When CP attempts authentication, it routes to Global auth page instead of 21v auth page. LogsMiner shows request not being redirected to 21v authentication endpoint.
solution: Temporary workaround: have user first sign in with user@partner.onmschina.cn format, which redirects to correct 21v auth page. Teams and Outlook are not affected because they use office discovery to route to 21v tenant first. If LogsMiner confirms no redirect record, submit IcM (reference: IcM-412709475, IcM-378453964).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Known Issue/CP unable sign in_ This username may be incorrect..md

symptom: Intune Device Restriction policy for password complexity (DeviceLock/MinDevicePasswordComplexCharacters) reports 'Error' status on Windows 10 desktop devices; SyncML returns status 405 (command not allowed) with HResult 0x86000011
rootCause: Password complexity value 4 ('Digits, lowercase letters, uppercase letters, and special characters are required') is documented as 'Not supported in desktop' in the Policy CSP. Intune pushes this value via OMA-URI ./Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/DeviceLock/MinDevicePasswordComplexCharacters but the Windows MDM client rejects it with 405. This is by design.
solution: Change password complexity setting to a supported value (1, 2, or 3) for Windows desktop devices. Value 4 is only supported on mobile. Use Windows LAPS if customer needs to manage local admin passwords. Kusto: DeviceManagementProvider with EventId 5786 to check policy compliance status. Reference IcMs: 163176114, 160587823.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Known Issue/Policy CSP - Device Lock.md

symptom: Custom OMA-URI device configuration policies may stop working or show unexpected behavior after Intune migrates Windows policies to unified settings platform
rootCause: Microsoft is migrating Windows device configuration policies to unified settings platform. Custom policies (OMA-URI) should only be used when the same setting is NOT available in Settings Catalog. Using custom OMA-URI for settings already available in Settings Catalog can cause conflicts or migration issues.
solution: Check if the custom OMA-URI setting is already available in Settings Catalog. If yes, migrate to Settings Catalog policy and remove the custom policy. Reference: Microsoft Tech Community blog 'Support tip: Windows device configuration policies migrating to unified settings platform in Intune' (article ID 4189665).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Known Issue/Usages clarify about custom policy - custom policy.md

symptom: Device previously had statically assigned AAD group memberships for Intune policy targeting, but those memberships are suddenly missing. Policies that were previously applied via static groups no longer target the device.
rootCause: The device was deleted from AAD and then re-registered (re-added), causing the AAD device ID to change. Static group memberships were assigned to the old AAD device ID and are not automatically transferred to the new device object.
solution: Query IntuneEvent ServiceName=StatefulDeviceService, ComponentName=AADDeviceRecordManagement, EventUniqueName=64213 with the Intune device ID. Check if AAD_DeviceID (Col1) changed between entries - a change confirms delete/re-add. Re-assign the device to the required AAD groups using the new device object ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Kusto Query/Device and User Effective Group/See if a device has been removed and re-added to A.md

symptom: Admin needs to verify whether Intune sent a noncompliance notification email to the end user for a specific device. Cannot confirm if the email action was triggered by the compliance policy scheduled actions.
solution: Query IntuneEvent with DeviceId, ApplicationName=Compliance, filter ColMetadata for 'scheduledActionsToAdd' or 'ruleDetails' containing 'ScheduledActionTask:1' (noncompliance email action). ScheduledActionTask enum: 1=email notification, 2=push notification, 3=retire device, 9=mark not compliant. Note: email recipient address may not be visible in recent data due to compliance/privacy policies.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Kusto Query/Device noncompliance notification email sent.md

symptom: VDI (Virtual Desktop Infrastructure) device enrollment in Intune fails with failureReason=24 (VDIMetadataError). DeviceLifecycle EventId 46804 shows ExternalManagementHint in details field.
rootCause: The VDI device has metadata issues that prevent proper enrollment. This can occur with Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD), Windows 365 Cloud PC, or other VDI environments where the ExternalManagementHint metadata is missing or conflicting.
solution: Query DeviceLifecycle EventId=46804 filtering for failureReason=24 or details containing 'ExternalManagementHint'. Verify the VDI environment configuration and enrollment type (CloudPCAADJoin=38, WVDDeviceAADJ=39, CloudPCHybridAADJ=40, WVDDeviceHybridAADJ=41). Check VDI TSG at https://www.intunewiki.com/wiki/VDI_TSG for known issues.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Kusto Query/DeviceLifecycle - Enrollment type.md

symptom: Need to determine a device's enrollment type (BYOD, corporate, Autopilot, DEP, co-managed, etc.) and check if a user has reached their device enrollment limit in Intune.
solution: For enrollment type: Query DeviceManagementProvider with ActivityId=<IntuneDeviceId>, look for 'EnrollType=' in message field (e.g., EnrollType=OnPremiseCoManaged). For enrollment limit: Query IntuneEvent with ActivityId=<IntuneDeviceId>, ServiceName=EnrollmentMTSessionService, EventUniqueName in ('RunEnrollmentEligibilityChecks', 'EnrollmentEligibilityChecks') - Message will show current device count vs limit.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Kusto Query/Get EnrollmentType and AccountId and user's enroll.md

symptom: Admin initiated MAM selective wipe from Intune portal but cannot confirm whether the wipe request was actually received and processed by the Intune backend service.
solution: Step 1: Query HttpSubsystem with accountId and httpVerb=POST and url contains 'wipe' to confirm backend received the wipe request. Step 2: Query IntuneEvent with AccountId, filter for 'wipe' and user ID in Message - this confirms server processed the wipe. Step 3: Query HttpSubsystem with url contains 'managedAppRegistrations' to verify the wipe status was reported back to the portal. Admin can also track status through Azure portal: Mobile apps > App Selective Wipe.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Kusto Query/HTTPS - MAM selective wipe.md

symptom: MAM diagnostic Kusto functions AmsActivityForUser() and AMS() are not available in 21v Mooncake; queries using these functions fail
rootCause: AmsActivityForUser() and AMS() are convenience functions available only in Global Intune Kusto clusters. 21v Mooncake clusters do not have these functions deployed.
solution: Use alternative queries based on IntuneEvent + HttpSubsystem tables directly. For MAM checkin: query IntuneEvent where ServiceName startswith 'StatelessApplicationManagementService' and EventUniqueName == 'IsAccountInMaintenance', then join ActivityId to HttpSubsystem to get full MAM flow (Checkin/Enroll/Unenroll/Action). For MAM policy state: use GetAction events with ColMetadata containing 'AppInstanceId;AadDeviceId;UpdateFlags;PolicyState'.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Kusto Query/MAM Applied.md

symptom: Apple VPP token sync completes but purchased apps not showing in Intune; VppApplicationSyncEvent shows 0 applications returned
rootCause: Apple's Volume Purchase Program sync response does not return the purchased apps. The issue is on Apple's side — the apps are not associated with the VPP token in Apple Business Manager/School Manager.
solution: Check VppApplicationSyncEvent in Kusto: VppFeatureTelemetry | where TaskName == 'VppApplicationSyncEvent' | project applications — if count is 0, customer needs to resolve with Apple why purchased apps are not showing against the token. Also verify: (1) VPP token last sync time via VppService_VppMultiTokenAccount.LastApplicationFullSyncTimeInUtc, (2) user/device counts via VppService_VppMultiTokenUser/Device tables, (3) token state is valid.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Kusto Query/VPP Kusto.md

symptom: Windows Update Ring policy only deploys B (Patch Tuesday) releases; C and D preview releases are not distributed to managed devices
rootCause: By design, Windows Update Ring policy in Intune only receives B releases (Patch Tuesday, second Tuesday of each month). C releases (third week) and D releases (fourth week) are preview releases containing only non-security fixes intended for testing; they are not distributed by Update Ring policy. Confirmed by PG.
solution: This is expected behavior. Inform customer: (1) B releases (Patch Tuesday) are the primary security updates and are the only regular releases distributed by Update Ring, (2) C/D releases are preview non-security updates for testing only — they roll into the following month's B release, (3) out-of-band releases for critical security vulnerabilities are also distributed. If customer needs C/D releases, they must use other mechanisms (WSUS, manual deployment).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Kusto Query/Windows Update Ring.md

symptom: iOS enrollment troubleshooting requires correlating Kusto data across DeviceLifecycle and IOSEnrollmentService tables using ActivityId
solution: Query DeviceLifecycle by userId to get scenarioType/ActivityId/managementState transitions, then correlate to IOSEnrollmentService using ActivityId to see SCEP challenge details, device type, serial number, and enrollment messages. Key fields: env_time, callerMethod, deviceTypeAsString, serialNumber, message, siteCode.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Kusto Query/iOS Enrollment.md

symptom: Need to escalate ICM priority for an Intune incident; unclear what factors justify higher priority
rootCause: ICM escalation criteria are not well-documented; engineers may not know the thresholds for higher priority
solution: Factors that justify higher ICM priority: (1) Customer has Premier/Unified support package to reach high-level Intune management team, (2) Device impact count >10K, (3) Security impact, (4) Sales or contract renewal impact. Include these factors in the ICM impact description to justify escalation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MISC/21V Escalation Template - Intune/Tips for icm escalation.md

symptom: Customer raises commerce/licensing ticket for 21V Intune; unclear which team handles it and what the escalation flow is
rootCause: 21V Intune commerce questions go through a different support chain than technical issues
solution: 21V Intune commerce flow: Customer raises ticket from portal.partner.microsoftonline.cn → 21V M365 team (equivalent to 21V Commerce) → MS CPC v-Team → MS CPC FTE Team → Engineering Team. The 21V M365 team acts as the first-level commerce handler, and CPC v-/FTE teams act as the Sheldon-equivalent role.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MISC/Intune Commerce.md

symptom: Need to check Intune feature release schedule or submit a customer RCA (Root Cause Analysis) request after an incident
solution: Feature releases: check Intune release announcements at https://supportability.visualstudio.com/M365%20Release%20Announcements/_wiki/wikis/M365-Product-Updates.wiki/255592/Intune. RCA requests: use the Post Incident Review form at https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/4e0dc6e7-2f03-d9fd-5954-517cdab148cd.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## MISC/Intune Feature release & RCA request.md

symptom: USB drive access fails with 'Please Insert a Disk Into USB Drive' error after removing Intune USB block policy; USB still inaccessible
rootCause: StorageCardDisabled registry key (set to 1) persists under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\StorageSense\Parameters even after removing the Intune USB block policy. fvevol.sys (BitLocker) FveGroupPolicyIsBDEForcedOn check interferes with volume discovery.
solution: Remove the StorageCardDisabled value from HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\StorageSense\Parameters and reboot. If issue persists, collect fvevol ETL logs and Process Monitor logs for Windows team analysis.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/USB Blocked with error _Please Insert a Disk Into.md

symptom: Win32 app download skipped with 'opt-in policy is not set' error; Managed Installer remediation fails with HRESULT 0x800703EE; AppLocker policy merge fails
rootCause: The registry.pol file under C:\Windows\System32\GroupPolicy\User is 0 bytes. GP component does not handle zero-length files, causing AppLocker policy merge failure during managed installer setup.
solution: Delete the registry.pol file under C:\Windows\System32\GroupPolicy\User and retry managed installer remediation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Win32_IME_PowerShell Script TSG/Managed Installer remediation failure.md

symptom: Managed Installer remediation script fails with 'The local policy cannot be obtained' HRESULT E_FAIL; AppLocker local policy retrieval error
rootCause: Broken GUIDs in C:\Windows\System32\GroupPolicy\gpt.ini under gPCMachineExtensionNames/gPCUserExtensionNames cause Group Policy service failure.
solution: Delete all files and folders under C:\Windows\System32\GroupPolicy to reset local group policy, then retry managed installer remediation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Win32_IME_PowerShell Script TSG/Managed Installer remediation failure.md

symptom: Intune remediation script result not reported to service after network outage; stale compliance status; may take up to 7 days to update
rootCause: Known issue: when network failure occurs during result upload, IME may not detect the failure and considers result as sent. Will not resend same result unless there is a change.
solution: Wait up to 7 days for correction. Workaround: modify remediation script to produce different output to force IME to report. Manual sync: intunemanagementextension://synccompliance.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Win32_IME_PowerShell Script TSG/PowerShell Script & Remediation Script.md

symptom: Win32 app deployment via Intune Management Extension (IME) downloads extremely slowly for China Telecom network users; CDN latency high
rootCause: IME deployment uses Verizon CDN which has high network latency when connected via China Telecom
solution: PG confirmed re-deployment with Azure CDN (ETA FY24Q3). Workaround: use non-China Telecom network (e.g. China Unicom/Mobile) for large app downloads
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Win32_IME_PowerShell Script TSG/Win32 deployment Troubleshooting (IME).md

symptom: Win32 app or PowerShell script deployment fails; Intune Management Extension (IME) not installed on device; AAD registered (Workplace joined) device
rootCause: AAD registered devices do not support IME installation. Only AAD joined (EnrollmentType=4), Hybrid AAD joined (type=9), or co-management enrolled (type=10) devices support IME. GPO-enrolled devices also unsupported
solution: Verify EnrollmentType in Kusto (DeviceManagementProvider table). Change device join type to AADJ/HAADJ/co-management. Check IME presence via Kusto: IntuneEvent where ApplicationName=="SideCar"
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Win32_IME_PowerShell Script TSG/Win32 deployment Troubleshooting (IME).md

symptom: Intune Management Extension (IME/MSI) download fails and does not retry; IntuneWindowsAgent.msi stuck in failed state
rootCause: EnforcementRetryCount exhausted in registry at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\EnterpriseDesktopAppManagement\
solution: Navigate to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\EnterpriseDesktopAppManagement\, find IntuneWindowsAgent.msi key, set EnforcementRetryCount=0 and EnforcementRetryIndex=0, empty JobStatusReport, delete LastError and Status values, then sync device
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Win32_IME_PowerShell Script TSG/Win32 deployment Troubleshooting (IME).md

symptom: Intune PowerShell script fails and stops executing after 3 retry attempts; script stuck in failed state
rootCause: Script timeout (30 min) or device reboot during execution counts as an error attempt. After 3 cumulative errors, IME stops retrying the script
solution: Reset DownloadCount to 1 in registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\IntuneManagementExtension\Policies\{context}\{policyId}. Must run in 64-bit PowerShell host. Verify software installation success before resetting
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Win32_IME_PowerShell Script TSG/Windows PowerShell Scripts Retry logic.md

symptom: Windows 10 MDM enrollment fails with error 0x80180023 (via AAD join) or 0x80070002 (via AAD register). Event log: MDM Enroll OMA-DM client configuration failed RAWResult 0x800706D9
rootCause: dmwappushservice (Device Management WAP Push message Routing Service) is missing from the device. Running New-Service alone is insufficient because additional registry parameters are required
solution: From a working device with same Windows 10 version: (1) Export HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\dmwappushservice registry key; (2) Copy dmwappushsvc.dll from C:\Windows\System32 if missing; (3) Import .reg file to affected device; (4) Reboot - service auto-loads. Do NOT use New-Service alone
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows 10 enrollment error 0x80180023 & 0x8007000.md

symptom: BitLocker recovery key not visible in Azure AD/Intune portal despite Intune BitLocker policy applied; key only escrowed to on-premises AD
rootCause: If BitLocker was enabled and recovery key already uploaded to on-premises AD before Intune policy deployment, Intune policy will NOT trigger automatic re-upload to AAD. Backup destination determined by dsreg.dll: ADJ->AD, AADJ->AAD, HAADJ->AAD+AD
solution: Use PowerShell script to manually escrow recovery keys to AAD (e.g. Invoke-EscrowBitlockerToAAD from GitHub). Check FVE registry and GPSearch to confirm applied backup policy. Deploy batch migration from MBAM to MEM as needed
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows 10 enrollment error 0x80180023 & 0x8007000/Bitlocker - 关于上传recovery key的逻辑.md

symptom: After BitLocker key rotation or manual key deletion, old recovery key still visible in Azure AD portal (Windows 10)
rootCause: Windows 10 22H2 does not remove old recovery keys from AAD after local deletion. Windows 11 22H2+ asynchronously deletes old key from AAD when key is deleted locally or rotated via Intune
solution: For Windows 11 22H2+: Intune Key Rotation button removes old key within minutes. For Windows 10: old keys remain in AAD by design - inform customer this is expected behavior
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows 10 enrollment error 0x80180023 & 0x8007000/Bitlocker - 关于上传recovery key的逻辑.md

symptom: BitLocker key rotation initiated from Intune portal fails with error
rootCause: Missing 'Save BitLocker recovery information to Azure Active Directory' setting in Endpoint Protection profile. Prerequisites: Windows 10 1909+, AADJ or HAADJ, MDM enrolled. For VMs: ISO file mounted prevents encryption
solution: Configure Endpoint Protection profile: enable BitLocker encryption, set 'Client-driven recovery password rotation', ensure 'Save BitLocker recovery info to AAD' is enabled. For VMs, unmount ISO before enabling encryption
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows 10 enrollment error 0x80180023 & 0x8007000/Bitlocker Rotation.md

symptom: Intune compliance policy reports device as 'not compliant' for BitLocker encryption even though the device is encrypted; also affects Secure Boot compliance
rootCause: Device Health Attestation (DHA) collects boot data during boot. BitLocker compliance status is only accurately reported after a reboot following encryption completion. DHA-Report is device-bound and valid only for current boot cycle
solution: Reboot the device after BitLocker encryption completes, then sync with Intune. DHA flow: boot -> DHA-Boot-Data -> DHA-Service -> encrypted blob -> MDM validation -> DHA-Report. Check HealthCertificateValidationResponse XML for BitlockerStatus and SecureBootEnabled
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows 10 enrollment error 0x80180023 & 0x8007000/Windows Compliance - BitLocker related _ DHA.md

symptom: After user-driven Autopilot, device reports 'not compliant' for BitLocker in Intune compliance; affects conditional access
rootCause: In user-driven Autopilot, BitLocker encryption occurs during or after ESP. DHA boot data collected at boot does not reflect encryption state completed after boot
solution: Three options: (1) Use White-glove/pre-provisioning with BitLocker policy assigned to device group; (2) Use 'encryption of data storage on device' compliance setting instead of DHA-based BitLocker check; (3) Add mandatory reboot after Autopilot completes
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows 10 enrollment error 0x80180023 & 0x8007000/Windows Compliance - BitLocker related _ DHA.md

symptom: Windows auto MDM enrollment fails with error 0x80010106 (Cannot change thread mode after it is set). Common in China with financial/banking U-key software
rootCause: Third-party crypto provider (e.g. CFCA_UKEY_csp) at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\Defaults\Provider initializes COM with COINIT_APARTMENTTHREADED in DllMain. dmenrollengine.dll then gets RPC_E_CHANGED_MODE when trying COINIT_MULTITHREADED
solution: Uninstall CFCA UKEY-related software. Verify no third-party providers at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\Defaults\Provider. Delete enrollment records at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments. Delete Intune portal records. Restart device. Note: enrollment renews yearly, may recur
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Auto enrollment fail - 0x80010106.md

symptom: Cannot deploy Windows Company Portal app as 'Required' to devices via Microsoft Store legacy in Intune; only 'Available' assignment type supported
rootCause: Microsoft Store legacy integration only supports 'Available' assignment type for Company Portal app, not 'Required'
solution: Download Company Portal offline installer from Microsoft Download Center (https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=105219) and deploy as Required LOB app. Long-term: PG plans to support via winget
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Company Portal deployment.md

symptom: Intune policy shows Succeeded but removed sub-settings (TattooRemoval) are not getting removed from device; user must wait up to 7 hours for settings cleanup
rootCause: When admin uses only User groups (or only Device groups) for all profile assignments without mixing, a hold-off period (7 hours for production, 15 hours for CTIP) is applied during Effective Group pre-processing before tattoo removal runs.
solution: Create a dummy/fake policy targeting impacted devices using the opposite assignment type: if all policies use Device groups, add a User group assignment; if all use User groups, add a Device group assignment. This triggers mixed-mode processing and bypasses the hold-off delay.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Windows/Intune Remote command Push and Policy assignment u.md

symptom: Intune-managed Windows device cannot receive WNS push notifications; Event ID 1116: Device Compact Ticket request failed with error 0x80070426
rootCause: WNS push channel depends on hidden service dependencies: (1) Network Connection Broker (ncbservice) and (2) Microsoft Account Sign-in Assistant. When Sign-in Assistant is disabled, device ticket cannot be generated, preventing WNS TCP connection.
solution: 1) Enable Microsoft Account Sign-in Assistant service; 2) Enable Network Connection Broker (ncbservice); 3) Verify device ticket exists in registry; 4) Check WNS TCP via netstat | findstr ESTABLISHED; 5) Trigger sync to re-establish Push channel
sourceRef: MCVKB/Intune/Windows/Intune Remote command Push and Policy assignment u.md

symptom: MSI LOB app deployed via Intune fails to download; no MSI file in C:\Windows\System32\config\systemprofile\AppData\Local\mdm; registry Status=30 (DownloadFailed)
rootCause: BITS job fails to download the MSI package. Common causes: network/proxy blocking BITS, CDN endpoint unreachable, or BITS service issues.
solution: 1) Check registry EnterpriseDesktopManagement for Status (30=DownloadFailed); 2) Run Bitsadmin /list /allusers; 3) Check BITS event log; 4) Reset EnforcementRetryCount and EnforcementRetryIndex to 0, clear LastError and Status to retry

symptom: MSI LOB app downloaded but fails silent installation; no install log at expected path
rootCause: MSI package fails to install silently via Intune MDM enforcement. Silent install may require additional parameters, missing prerequisites, or MSI internal error.
solution: 1) Check logs: device-targeted at %windir%\temp\{MSIProductID}.msi.log; 2) Enable verbose MSI logging via registry (Debug=7, Logging=voicewarmupx); 3) Manual test: msiexec /I package.msi /l*vx log; 4) Check MDM event log

symptom: Intune compliance policy reports device not compliant for Secure Boot even though Secure Boot is enabled in BIOS
rootCause: DHA requires UEFI 2.3.1 BIOS + TPM 2.0 to report Secure Boot status. Devices with TPM 1.2 or pre-UEFI 2.3.1 BIOS can have Secure Boot enabled but DHA cannot report it. PCR7 Configuration must be Bound.
solution: 1) Run MSINFO as admin - verify BIOS Mode=UEFI and PCR7 Configuration=Bound; 2) Run Confirm-SecureBootUEFI in PowerShell; 3) Run manage-bde -protectors -get c: to verify PCR7 binding; 4) Update BIOS for UEFI 2.3.1/TPM 2.0 if needed

symptom: Intune device compliance state shows Unknown; ValidPolicyCompliancesCount is 0
rootCause: Two common causes: (1) Device not checked in within last 2 days; (2) Device enrolled with DEM account - DEM devices cannot evaluate compliance. Also GPO/Hybrid AADJ/multi-user devices show inconsistent results with user group targeting.
solution: 1) Verify device checked in within 2 days; 2) Confirm not DEM-enrolled; 3) Use device-based groups for compliance targeting with GPO/Hybrid AADJ; 4) Kusto: IntuneEvent where ServiceName==StatelessComplianceCalculationService

symptom: Quality update rollback via Intune WUfB Ring fails silently; SyncML error 0x80070490 (NotFound) for Rollback/QualityUpdate and 0x82AA0002 (AlreadyExists) for PauseQualityUpdates
rootCause: Rollback CSP requires 3 conditions: (1) WUfB Connected, (2) Device in Paused State, (3) Latest quality update installed. PauseQualityUpdates Add fails with 418/AlreadyExists, blocking the Sequence, and Rollback Exec fails with 404/0x80070490. Update CSP does not support Home edition.
solution: 1) Verify all 3 rollback prerequisites; 2) Check MDM event log for CSP errors on PauseQualityUpdates and Rollback/QualityUpdate; 3) Check registry PolicyManager for PauseQualityUpdates=1; 4) Verify Windows Update UI shows paused; 5) If AlreadyExists conflict persists, file ICM to PG

symptom: During OOBE, AutoPilot customized branding (org name, logo) not displayed; standard OOBE choices shown instead of AutoPilot experience
rootCause: Device not recognized by AutoPilot service. Causes: (1) Hardware hash not uploaded or changed; (2) No serial number; (3) Missing Azure AD Premium license; (4) Company branding not configured in Azure AD.
solution: 1) Re-capture hardware hash: Shift-F10 in OOBE > PowerShell > Get-WindowsAutoPilotInfo; 2) Upload CSV and verify in device list; 3) Verify Azure AD Premium license; 4) Configure company branding in Azure AD

symptom: AutoPilot Azure AD join fails with error 801C0003 Something went wrong during OOBE
rootCause: User not allowed to join devices to Azure AD (device settings restriction), or user reached maximum device join limit.
solution: 1) Azure AD > Devices > Device settings: enable user device join; 2) Set device limit to Unlimited; 3) Reference error code table at docs.microsoft.com

symptom: AutoPilot MDM enrollment fails with error 80180018 - device could not be enrolled in MDM during OOBE
rootCause: Three causes: (1) Missing Azure AD Premium license; (2) Missing Intune license; (3) Reached Intune enrollment limit. Also: auto MDM enrollment not enabled or user not in enrollment scope group.
solution: 1) Assign Azure AD Premium P1/P2 license; 2) Assign Intune license; 3) Check enrollment restrictions; 4) Verify auto MDM enrollment in Azure AD > Mobility; 5) Verify user in enrollment scope group

symptom: Intune Endpoint Protection で Firewall Policy を展開すると、Windows Defender Firewall with Advanced Security のインバウンド/アウトバウンドルール一覧に表示されず、Monitoring > Firewall セクションにのみ表示される
rootCause: Intune (MDM) 経由で配布された Firewall ルールは PolicyManager ではなく HKLM\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\SharedAccess\Parameters\FirewallPolicy\Mdm\FirewallRules に書き込まれるため、通常のルール一覧には反映されない
solution: MDMDiagnosticlog や Event Viewer (Event ID 2097: Windows Firewall With Advanced Security/Firewall) でルール適用を確認。Monitoring > Firewall セクションで実際のルール状態を検証する
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Defender Firewall.md

symptom: 21v (Mooncake) 環境で Intune Endpoint Security > Antivirus > Windows Security Experience から Tamper Protection をオン/オフしようとすると、ポータルで 'pending update' と表示され設定が適用されない
rootCause: 21v では Tamper Protection が PG によりデプロイされていない。デバイスが Intune に登録されると ManagedDefenderProductType が 6 になるが、21v Intune は Tamper Protection を制御できず、MDE onboard もない場合はデッドロック状態になる
solution: 1) MDE onboard で Tamper Protection を制御する 2) UI で手動管理する場合は ManagedDefenderProductType=0, SenseEnabled=0, ProductType=0 にレジストリを変更 3) 一時的に無効化するには Defender troubleshooting mode を使用 (4時間限定) 4) Defender ログ収集: MpCmdRun.exe -getfiles
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Defender- Tamper Protection not enabled in.md

symptom: Windows MDM enrollment が失敗し、SessionState が 5 (completed) に設定されるが実際には SyncML が実行されない。エラーコード 0x80041313 (SCHED_E_UNKNOWN_OBJECT_VERSION)
rootCause: Task Scheduler の Trigger レジストリデータが破損（先頭バイトが 0xFF、正常は 0x17/0x16/0x15）。omadmprc.exe が Task Scheduler 経由で omadmclient.exe を起動する際、破損した Trigger を読み取り SCHED_E_UNKNOWN_OBJECT_VERSION を返す。omadmprc.exe がアクティベーション失敗と判断し SessionState=5 を書き込むため、omadmclient.exe は SyncML を実行しない
solution: 破損した Task Scheduler タスクのトリガーデータを修正または削除する。問題のタスク例: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Schedule\TaskCache\Tree\Microsoft\Windows\Media Center\RecordingRestart。ProcMon で OMADM セッションレジストリの SessionState と SessionHRESULT を確認して特定する
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Enrollment/High level Windows Enrollment - 1.md

symptom: Windows device Intune sync fails with 'The sync could not be initiated' error after device has been offline for extended period or certificate was tampered with
rootCause: The Microsoft Intune MDM certificate (Local Machine > Personal > Certificates) has expired, was manually deleted, or the device was enrolled in a different Intune tenant. Without a valid enrollment certificate the sync cannot proceed.
solution: Method 1 (with data loss): Disconnect work/school account via Settings > Accounts > Access work or school. Method 2 (without data loss): 1) Delete stale scheduled tasks under Task Scheduler > Microsoft > Windows > EnterpriseMgmt and note enrollment ID; 2) Delete stale registry keys under enrollment ID in HKLM paths (Enrollments, EnterpriseResourceManager, PolicyManager, Provisioning/OMADM); 3) Delete expired Intune cert from certlm.msc > Personal; 4) Run deviceenroller.exe /c /AutoEnrollMDM in SYSTEM context via PSExec. PowerShell script can automate all cleanup steps.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Enrollment/Unenroll and reenroll.md

symptom: Windows Hello for Business (WHFB) Identity Protection profile set to Enabled for a user group does not take effect; users not prompted to set up PIN during sign-in
rootCause: Tenant-wide Windows Enrollment WHfB policy is set to Disabled. The Enrollment disable setting takes priority over Identity Protection enable. Identity Protection can configure PIN complexity/biometrics but cannot override enable/disable from Windows Enrollment. Also: changing tenant-wide WHfB policy does NOT affect existing enrolled devices, only new enrollments during OOBE.
solution: Set tenant-wide Windows Enrollment WHfB policy to 'Not Configured' (not Disabled), then use Identity Protection profile to enable WHfB for specific user groups. Alternative: use OMA-URI custom profile with ./User/Vendor/MSFT/PassportForWork/<TenantId>/Policies/UsePassportForWork = True assigned to target user groups.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Hello for Business (WHFB_WH4B).md

symptom: Intune PowerShell script deployed to multi-app kiosk device reports success in Azure portal but script does not actually execute (e.g. registry changes not applied)
rootCause: In multi-app kiosk mode only applications listed in Assigned Access XML allowed apps can execute. PowerShell scripts via Intune run through the Intune Management Extension (IME) which requires powershell.exe and IME AppUserModelId to be explicitly allowed.
solution: Add to kiosk XML allowed apps: <App AppUserModelId='Microsoft.Management.Clients.IntuneManagementExtension' />, <App DesktopAppPath='%SystemRoot%\system32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe' />, <App DesktopAppPath='%WinDir%\system32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\PowerShell_ISE.exe' />, <App DesktopAppPath='%SystemRoot%\syswow64\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe' />, and <App DesktopAppPath='%windir%\regedit.exe' /> if registry modification needed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Kiosk Mode.md

symptom: Intune Kiosk mode auto-logon stops working; device does not auto-login to the kiosk account after reboot
rootCause: Device configuration profile or compliance policy with password policy settings creates an EAS registry key at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\EAS. Once this registry key is present, kiosk auto-logon is broken.
solution: 1) Remove or exclude password policy from configuration/compliance profiles targeting kiosk devices; 2) Delete the EAS registry path HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\EAS; 3) Re-enroll the device to restore auto-logon.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Kiosk Mode/intune Kiosk mode not auto logging in.md

symptom: Kiosk profile deployment shows error 'No Mapping Between Account Names and Security IDs was Done' in Device Management logs
rootCause: The user account specified in the kiosk profile logon type (Local user or Azure AD user) does not exist on the target device. The kiosk profile requires the account to be present before it can be applied.
solution: Ensure the specified kiosk user account exists on the device before deploying the kiosk profile. For local user: create the account first. For Azure AD user: ensure user has previously signed in, or use auto-logon with dynamically created account.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Kiosk Mode/Understand Windows Kiosk setting in Intune portal.md

symptom: Specified application does not appear in kiosk mode start menu despite being configured in the kiosk XML alternative start layout
rootCause: The .lnk shortcut file referenced in the kiosk XML start layout does not exist at the specified path, or the kiosk user does not have read access to the .lnk file location. The system resolves app icons via .lnk files; if inaccessible the app is silently omitted.
solution: 1) Verify .lnk file exists at exact path in XML (e.g. %ALLUSERSPROFILE%\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\); 2) Test by navigating to lnk path as kiosk user; 3) If lnk missing, create it or switch to default layout (no alternative XML) which auto-generates icons for allowed apps.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Kiosk Mode/Understand Windows Kiosk setting in Intune portal.md

symptom: Conditional Access policy requiring device compliance shows 'failure' in sign-in logs during initial Intune enrollment via OOBE; admin concerned enrollment is being blocked
rootCause: Expected behavior. During OOBE, device registration (DRS) and Intune MDM enrollment occur sequentially. Device is not yet compliant when enrollment begins, but Intune enrollment endpoint handles this gracefully - enrollment succeeds despite CA failure report.
solution: No action needed. Do NOT exclude Microsoft Intune Enrollment from compliance CA policy. The CA failure log during initial enrollment is informational and harmless. After enrollment completes, compliance policies deploy and device becomes compliant.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Enrollment/Windows OOBE enrollment process with CAP.md

symptom: Windows LAPS client fails to update password to Azure AD; Event ID 10025 with error 0x80072EE7 (INTERNET_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED) in LAPS Operational log
rootCause: Client cannot resolve enterpriseregistration.windows.net DNS endpoint due to network filtering, proxy, or firewall blocking
solution: Verify connectivity: curl https://enterpriseregistration.windows.net/ -D - from admin cmd; check proxy/firewall rules; if Reset-LapsPassword -Verbose also fails, confirms network issue
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows LAPS with Azure AD/Troubleshooting Windows LAPS with Azure AD -Event.md

symptom: Windows Update scan fails with USO error 0x87c52200 (certificate error) during MoUpdateOrchestrator scan process
rootCause: SSL/TLS inspection device replaces certificate for settings-win.data.microsoft.com with inspection cert, causing cert validation failure
solution: Exclude settings-win.data.microsoft.com from SSL inspection on proxy/firewall; verify certificate chain using CAPI2 event log
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Update ring/TSG_ WUFB troubleshooting.md

symptom: Windows Update scan fails with HTTP 407 Proxy Authentication Required when connecting to fe3cr.delivery.mp.microsoft.com
rootCause: Network proxy requires negotiate authentication but Windows Update service (SYSTEM context) cannot provide valid proxy credentials
solution: Configure proxy to allow unauthenticated access to WU endpoints (*.delivery.mp.microsoft.com); or configure WinHTTP proxy for SYSTEM context
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Update ring/TSG_ WUFB troubleshooting.md

symptom: Intune Update Ring shows device Failed in End User Update Status but device itself shows Up to date with no visible failed updates
rootCause: Windows Update service regulation blocks download of applicable update; device Update CSP reports FailedUpdates to Intune even though device considers itself current
solution: Kusto IntuneEvent (ComponentName==ProcessWindowsUpdateStatus) to get FailedUpdates GUID; look up in WU Catalog; check WindowsUpdate.log for service regulation; manually install blocked KB; known Intune reporting limitation
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Update ring/TSG_ Windows Update ring show failed.md

symptom: WUFB Feature Update policy deploys wrong OS version; devices get different version than specified in policy
rootCause: Multiple causes: device scanning WSUS instead of Microsoft Update; device enrollment issues; multiple overlapping deployments; DSS enrollment delay
solution: Check HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate for WSUS config; verify IsDefaultAUService via PowerShell; ensure single feature update deployment; set deferral days to avoid DSS race condition
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Update ring/WUFB feature update troubleshoot.md

symptom: TargetReleaseVersion CSP and Feature Update policy behave differently for controlling Windows feature update versions
rootCause: TargetReleaseVersion CSP is client-side control; Feature Update policy is server-side via DSS. Different control planes cause different behavior
solution: Use TargetReleaseVersion CSP (OMA-URI ./Vendor/MSFT/Policy/Config/Update/TargetReleaseVersion) as workaround for co-managed devices; choose based on client vs server control needs
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## Windows TSG/Windows Update ring/WUFB feature update troubleshoot.md

symptom: iOS device cannot exit old AppLock (Kiosk single-app mode) when switching to a new AppLock policy; new AppLock profile deployment shows error: The profile 'App Lock Profile' is invalid
rootCause: When old AppLock policy is unassigned and new AppLock policy is assigned in the same check-in, Intune detects a same-type single-instance profile replacement and sends InstallProfile (with same Payload UUID) instead of RemoveProfile. AppLock is the only payload type that cannot be overwritten by InstallProfile - the device rejects the new profile while old one remains active.
solution: Split into two check-ins: (1) First check-in: unassign old AppLock policy only, do NOT assign new policy - this triggers RemoveProfile and device exits single-app mode. (2) Second check-in: assign new AppLock policy - device enters new single-app mode. No workaround for single-step replacement. Apple notified via PG feedback.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/AppLock (Kiosk mode) behavior in iOS.md

symptom: iOS MDM enrollment fails after Quick Start restore with error: Unable to create unlock token - The unlock token could not be created because a passcode has already been set (MCKeybagErrorDomain Code=37002)
rootCause: Quick Start restore runs after Setup Assistant completes and passcode is already configured. MDM enrollment requires creating an unlock token during Setup Assistant (before passcode is set). When passcode exists, unlock token creation fails and TokenUpdate message cannot establish valid MDM enrollment.
solution: Use iCloud or iTunes backup restore during Setup Assistant (before passcode step) instead of Quick Start. If Quick Start must be used, instruct user to NOT set passcode before Quick Start. After restore, device must go through Setup Assistant with DEP to properly create unlock token.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/Backup and restore.md

symptom: Apple Configurator fails to add iOS device to ABM after Intune wipe. Device shows activation lock and cannot be paired.
rootCause: Intune remote wipe does not remove Find My iPhone (activation lock). The activation lock persists and prevents Apple Configurator from adding the device to ABM.
solution: Before using Apple Configurator, erase the device locally via iOS Settings > General > Transfer or Reset > Erase All Content and Settings (this removes activation lock). Also delete the device from Intune portal. Do NOT use Intune remote wipe for devices that need to be re-enrolled via Apple Configurator.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/How to - Apple Configurator enroll - via iPhone.md

symptom: DEP device sync disabled in Intune. Sync status shows Terms and Conditions not accepted even after accepting new TC on Apple Business Manager portal.
rootCause: Apple released new Terms and Conditions causing Intune to stop DEP sync. Re-accepting TC on Apple portal does not automatically resume sync in Intune.
solution: Use Graph Explorer to manually trigger sync: POST https://graph.microsoft.com/BETA/deviceManagement/depOnboardingSettings/{depOnboardingSettingId}/syncWithAppleDeviceEnrollmentProgram. The depOnboardingSettingId (token ID) can be found in the URL when accessing the DEP token page in Intune admin portal.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/Manually sync with ABM token via Graph Explorer.md

symptom: Need to convert an unsupervised iOS managed device to supervised mode without losing user data and apps.
rootCause: Supervision state is tied to the enrollment method. Directly supervising an existing unsupervised device requires a factory reset which erases all data.
solution: Workaround: (1) Backup Device A via iTunes/iCloud, (2) Restore to temp Device B, (3) Backup Device B, (4) Add Device A to DEP/Apple Configurator and enroll it (supervised), (5) Restore Device B backup onto enrolled Device A. Important: Uncheck Remove apps on profile removal and Restrict App data backup before starting.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/How to supervise managed devices without data loss.md

symptom: VPP app install fails on iOS device. Logs show is not ready for install since user yet to accept VPP invite.
rootCause: The user has not accepted the Apple Volume Purchase Program (VPP) invitation. If the user never received the invite, the Apple App Store may be blocked by a device restriction policy.
solution: Ensure user accepts the VPP invitation. If user did not receive the invite, check that App Store is not blocked via Intune device restriction policy. Use hide app option instead of blocking to maintain VPP functionality.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/Troubleshooting iOS VPP app install.md

symptom: VPP app license assignment fails with error code 9632: Too many recent calls to manage licenses with identical requests.
rootCause: Too many devices for a single user or too many Apple IDs making requests from the same organization, causing Apple to throttle license management API calls.
solution: Switch from user-based VPP licensing to device-based licensing. Device licensing associates each VPP license to a unique device and does not require an Apple ID, avoiding the throttling issue.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/Troubleshooting iOS VPP app install.md

symptom: VPP app license assignment fails with error code 9616 (regUsersAlreadyAssigned). Some apps install while others fail for the same user.
rootCause: The user Apple ID is already associated with the VPP sToken by another Intune user. Apple recorded the first user Apple ID, so license assignment for a second user sharing the same Apple ID fails with 9616.
solution: Options: (1) Move to device licensing that does not involve user Apple ID, (2) Create a brand new Intune user for this user and associate a brand new Apple ID with it.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/Troubleshooting iOS VPP app install.md

symptom: VPP app install fails with LicenseNotFound. Apple rejects install request even though Intune shows license assigned.
rootCause: User updated their Apple ID on one or more devices during the lifecycle of Intune management. The Apple ID recorded in Apple system from initial VPP agreement acceptance no longer matches the Apple ID the user is currently signed in with.
solution: Options: (1) Log back in with the original Apple ID used when first accepting Apple VPP agreement, (2) Move to device licensing that does not involve user Apple ID, (3) Create a brand new Intune user and associate a brand new Apple ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/Troubleshooting iOS VPP app install.md

symptom: VPP app install fails with VppDeviceLicenseAssignmentFailedEvent. User gets message Your organization has depleted its licenses for this app.
rootCause: All available VPP user licenses for the app have been claimed. iOS VPP user licensing only allows 5-10 devices per user, and the limit has been reached.
solution: Options: (1) Switch to device-based VPP licensing, (2) Temporarily revoke app deployment for some users to reclaim licenses, (3) Purchase additional VPP licenses in Apple Business Manager.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/Troubleshooting iOS VPP app install.md

symptom: VPP apps not auto-updating on iOS devices despite tenant having auto-update enabled in Intune.
rootCause: VPP auto-update is not supported for devices running iOS versions below 11.3. The MDM protocol for VPP app updates was introduced in iOS 11.3.
solution: Upgrade devices to iOS 11.3 or later to enable VPP auto-update functionality.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/Troubleshooting iOS VPP app install.md

symptom: VPP app keeps re-installing or prompting for update on iOS/macOS. App version shows mismatch between Intune policy and device-reported version.
rootCause: Could be: (1) Intune service packages incorrect app version - check CreateIntuneAppMetadataToPatch events, (2) VPP token revoked by Apple (error 9625: The server has revoked the sToken), (3) Stale policy cache on Intune backend causing version mismatch.
solution: TSG steps: (1) Check app version via Apple lookup API, (2) Query IntuneEvent for CreateIntuneAppMetadataToPatch to verify synced version, (3) Compare GWVersion vs DeviceReportedVersion via AppleAppSummary events, (4) Check VPP token validity for error 9625, (5) Monitor policy cache via goodDeviceCount/badDeviceCount.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/TSG_ VPP App keep re-install _ prompting for updat.md

symptom: Intune VPN profile for Palo Alto GlobalProtect deployed successfully to iOS device but the GlobalProtect app cannot pick up the VPN configuration in China.
rootCause: Known issue: The standard GlobalProtect VPN identifier is com.paloaltonetworks.globalprotect.vpn, but the China-specific GlobalProtect app uses a different identifier: com.paloaltonetworks.globalprotect.vpncn.
solution: Use a Custom VPN profile or create a custom profile with XML payload that uses the China-specific identifier com.paloaltonetworks.globalprotect.vpncn instead of the standard one.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/VPN profile deploy succeed but Global Protect app.md

symptom: iOS/iPadOS enrollment fails with error NoEnrollmentPolicy - no enrollment policy found. Device cannot complete enrollment.
rootCause: Apple Push Notification Service (APNs) certificate is missing, invalid, or expired. Or iOS/iPadOS platform is not enabled as enrollment target.
solution: 1. Verify APNs certificate status in Intune > Tenant administration > Apple MDM Push certificate. 2. Renew if expired (do NOT replace - renewing keeps existing enrollments). 3. Ensure iOS/iPadOS platform is enabled.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/iOS_iPad enrollment general error.md

symptom: iOS/iPadOS enrollment fails with error DeviceCapReached - too many mobile devices are enrolled already.
rootCause: User has reached the maximum number of device enrollments allowed by the Intune device limit restriction policy.
solution: User must remove one of their currently enrolled devices from Company Portal before enrolling another. Admin can also increase the device enrollment limit in Intune > Devices > Enrollment restrictions > Device limit restrictions.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/iOS_iPad enrollment general error.md

symptom: iOS enrollment fails with Company Portal Temporarily Unavailable error message.
rootCause: The Company Portal app on the device is out of date or corrupted.
solution: 1. Remove the Company Portal app from the device. 2. Validate user credentials (Azure AD/Entra ID sign-in). 3. Reinstall Company Portal from the App Store. 4. Retry enrollment.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/iOS_iPad enrollment general error.md

symptom: iOS enrollment fails with APNSCertificateNotValid or AccountNotOnboarded error. Device cannot communicate with Intune.
rootCause: APNs certificate was not properly configured (steps incomplete) or has expired. For AccountNotOnboarded, APNs cert was replaced instead of renewed, breaking existing device communication.
solution: 1. Check APNs cert expiry in Intune admin center. 2. RENEW (not replace) the APNs certificate - replacing requires re-enrollment of ALL iOS devices. 3. For AccountNotOnboarded, re-enroll affected devices after cert renewal. Kusto: PushNotificationProvider | where env_time > ago(1d) | where accountId == '<tenantId>' | project env_time, EventId, TaskName, accountId, responseCode, ex, errorCode, errorCodeString
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/iOS_iPad enrollment general error.md

symptom: iOS enrollment fails with UserLicenseTypeInvalid - device cannot be enrolled because user does not have correct license.
rootCause: User account is not a member of a required user group, or user does not have the correct Intune license assigned. Common when Intune is MDM authority but user has SCCM license instead.
solution: 1. Verify user has Intune license assigned in Microsoft 365 admin center. 2. Ensure user is in a group targeted by enrollment policy. 3. Check MDM authority setting matches license type.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/iOS_iPad enrollment general error.md

symptom: iOS enrollment fails with MdmAuthorityNotDefined - mobile device management authority has not been defined.
rootCause: MDM authority was not set in Intune tenant configuration. Required before any device enrollment can occur.
solution: Set the MDM authority in Intune admin center > Tenant administration. For new tenants, MDM authority is automatically set to Intune. For migrated tenants, verify the setting is configured correctly.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/iOS_iPad enrollment general error.md

symptom: ADE (Automated Device Enrollment) sync between Intune and Apple fails with Expired or invalid token error.
rootCause: The ADE/DEP token uploaded to Intune has expired (tokens expire annually), been revoked in ABM/ASM, or is malformed.
solution: 1. If expired: renew token in ABM/ASM, download new token, upload to Intune. 2. If invalid: create new token - can reuse existing MDM Server via Edit > MDM Server settings > Upload public key. 3. Renewed token can be uploaded to existing Intune ADE profile.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Intune/## iOS TSG/iOS_iPad enrollment general error.md

symptom: ADE sync fails with Access denied error. Intune cannot communicate with Apple for device enrollment sync.
rootCause: Intune has been removed from the MDM server list in Apple Business Manager/Apple School Manager. Or the ADE token has expired.
